items.text: 3. LEASE UPDATE PROCEDURE(art. 8 par 2)

classification.title: 3. LEASE UPDATE PROCEDURE(art. 8 par 2)
classification.raw: 3. LEASE UPDATE PROCEDURE(art. 8 par 2)

items.text: In the event of a change of accommodation subsequent to submitting the declaration, the student, only and exclusively if he/she is a scholarship winner with a "non-resident" amount, is obliged to communicate the updated data within one month of the registration of the new lease (or stipulation of the same in the case of a different payment basis). This is done via the specific online procedure: "aggiornamento contratto di locazione” (“lease update”) relating to the change of lease and the receipt of registration of the new lease must be attached to guarantee coverage of the minimum period of 10 months. Read art. 8 carefully.

classification.raw: In the event of a change of accommodation subsequent to submitting the declaration, the student, only and exclusively if he/she is a scholarship winner with a "non-resident" amount, is obliged to communicate the updated data within one month of the registration of the new lease (or stipulation of the same in the case of a different payment basis). This is done via the specific online procedure: "aggiornamento contratto di locazione” (“lease update”) relating to the change of lease and the receipt of registration of the new lease must be attached to guarantee coverage of the minimum period of 10 months. Read art. 8 carefully.

items.text: 4. SPECIFIC PROVISIONS FOR STUDENTS ATTENDING THE “FILTER SEMESTER” OF MEDICINA E CHIRURGIA, MEDICINA VETERINARIA E ODONTOIATRIA E PROTESI DENTARIA (art 9 par 3)

classification.title: 4. SPECIFIC PROVISIONS FOR STUDENTS ATTENDING THE “FILTER SEMESTER” OF MEDICINA E CHIRURGIA, MEDICINA VETERINARIA E ODONTOIATRIA E PROTESI DENTARIA (art 9 par 3)
classification.raw: 4. SPECIFIC PROVISIONS FOR STUDENTS ATTENDING THE “FILTER SEMESTER” OF MEDICINA E CHIRURGIA, MEDICINA VETERINARIA E ODONTOIATRIA E PROTESI DENTARIA (art 9 par 3)

items.text: Students attending the filter semester at a university in Piedmont must apply by 9 September.

classification.raw: Students attending the filter semester at a university in Piedmont must apply by 9 September.

items.text: Students attending the filter semester at a non-Piedmont university must submit an application at least to the institution in the region in which they are attending the filter semester and subsequently to EDISU Piemonte, if they are assigned to a Piedmont university or enrol in a related or another course at a Piedmont university.

classification.raw: Students attending the filter semester at a non-Piedmont university must submit an application at least to the institution in the region in which they are attending the filter semester and subsequently to EDISU Piemonte, if they are assigned to a Piedmont university or enrol in a related or another course at a Piedmont university.

items.text: All provisions for both types of students are included in art 9(3) and (10) of this notice.

classification.raw: All provisions for both types of students are included in art 9(3) and (10) of this notice.

You should only read those other sections that interest you according to the type of application you want to submit.

classification.title: Art. 1 (1) COURSES
classification.raw: Art. 1(1) COURSES

items.text: Italian and international students who meet the financial and merit requirements set out in the following articles 6 (general requirements), 9 (first years), 15 (subsequent years), 24 (PhDs and specialisation schools), 29 (students with disabilities) and 30 and 31 (international students), enrolled for the academic year 2025/26 at Piedmont universities identified in the following paragraph, may apply for the scholarship and, if non-resident, the accommodation service in the following types of courses:

classification.raw: Italian and international students who meet the financial and merit requirements set out in the following articles 6 (general requirements), 9 (first years), 15 (subsequent years), 24 (PhDs and specialisation schools), 29 (students with disabilities) and 30 and 31 (international students), enrolled for the academic year 2025/26 at Piedmont universities identified in the following paragraph, may apply for the scholarship and, if non-resident, the accommodation service in the following types of courses:

items.text: 1. bachelor’s degree; 2. single-cycle master's degree; 3. master's degree (Ministerial Decree 270/04); 4. PhD courses (Legislative Decree 210/98) who do not benefit from the scholarship1P; 5. specialisation schools of at least two years' duration, excluding medical schools under Legislative Decree 368/1999.

items: 1. bachelor’s degree;
2. single-cycle master's degree;
3. master's degree (Ministerial Decree 270/04);
4. PhD courses (Legislative Decree 210/98) who do not benefit from the scholarship1P;
5. specialisation schools of at least two years' duration, excluding medical schools under Legislative Decree 368/1999.
classification.raw: 1. bachelor’s degree; 2. single-cycle master's degree; 3. master's degree (Ministerial Decree 270/04); 4. PhD courses (Legislative Decree 210/98) who do not benefit from the scholarship1P; 5. specialisation schools of at least two years' duration, excluding medical schools under Legislative Decree 368/1999.

items.text: Students enrolled at PhD courses or specialisation schools can apply only for scholarship

classification.raw: Students enrolled at PhD courses or specialisation schools can apply only for scholarship

items.text: The scholarships relate to the academic year 2025/26. With regard to students enrolled in specialisation schools, scholarships may be granted to those with active enrolment in the relevant academic year of the related school2.

classification.raw: The scholarships relate to the academic year 2025/26. With regard to students enrolled in specialisation schools, scholarships may be granted to those with active enrolment in the relevant academic year of the related school2.

items.text: With regard to scholarships awarded to students enrolled in the additional semester, in addition to the merit and income requirements, enrolment in the current academic year at the time of the application is sufficient, as stipulated in art. 3(5) of Ministerial Decree 1320/2021. Therefore, EDISU (Regional Agency for the Right to Education) will not proceed with the verification of their enrolment for the 2025/26 academic year.

classification.raw: With regard to scholarships awarded to students enrolled in the additional semester, in addition to the merit and income requirements, enrolment in the current academic year at the time of the application is sufficient, as stipulated in art. 3(5) of Ministerial Decree 1320/2021. Therefore, EDISU (Regional Agency for the Right to Education) will not proceed with the verification of their enrolment for the 2025/26 academic year.

items.text: ART. 4 “STUDENT TYPE” FOR SCHOLARSHIP PURPOSES

classification.title: Art. 4 “STUDENT TYPE” FOR SCHOLARSHIP PURPOSES
classification.raw: ART. 4 “STUDENT TYPE” FOR SCHOLARSHIP PURPOSES

items.text: The amount of the scholarship is differentiated according to the following types of students.

classification.raw: The amount of the scholarship is differentiated according to the following types of students.

items.text:
RESIDENT1. Residents of the municipality in which their course of study is based. 2. Attending distance learning courses run by universities in Piedmont (not to be confused with distance learning because of the pandemic) 3. Subject to detention. Such students may apply for the scholarship only, without the accommodation service.
COMPUTERResidents in a Municipality other than the one in which their course of study is based, which can be reached by public transport within 60 minutes. Such students may apply for the scholarship only, without the accommodation service.
NON-RESIDENTResidents in a Municipality other than the one in which their course of study is based, which cannot be reached by public transport within 60 minutes. Such students may apply for: 1. the accommodation service at EDISU residences and the scholarship; 2. the scholarship alone. Information on the requirements, how and when to obtain the non-resident scholarship-only amount is available in art. 5(1.3) and art. 8(2). Independent students pursuant to art. 6(1.5) of this notice of competition

items.text: If students take up or change their official residence after the application for benefits has been submitted, but before 12 November 2025, they must promptly notify EDISU by submitting a ticket; the type of student will be considered on the basis of the new official residence and the date the official residence was changed. The official residence on 12 November 2025 is taken into account in awarding the scholarship.

classification.raw: If students take up or change their official residence after the application for benefits has been submitted, but before 12 November 2025, they must promptly notify EDISU by submitting a ticket; the type of student will be considered on the basis of the new official residence and the date the official residence was changed. The official residence on 12 November 2025 is taken into account in awarding the scholarship.

items.text: On the other hand, in the case of awarding the accommodation service or scholarship with a non-resident amount, students are required to declare their change of residence, even if it is after 12 November 2025. If the change of residence changes the student type, these students lose their non-resident status and the corresponding scholarship amount, which will consequently be recalculated as a commuter amount, for the academic year 2025/26.

classification.raw: On the other hand, in the case of awarding the accommodation service or scholarship with a non-resident amount, students are required to declare their change of residence, even if it is after 12 November 2025. If the change of residence changes the student type, these students lose their non-resident status and the corresponding scholarship amount, which will consequently be recalculated as a commuter amount, for the academic year 2025/26.

items.text: It should be noted that EU and non-EU international students with their families residing entirely abroad maintain their non-resident status regardless of whether or not they take up official residence in Italy, if they submit the declaration of paid residence pursuant to art. 8(2) below.

classification.raw: It should be noted that EU and non-EU international students with their families residing entirely abroad maintain their non-resident status regardless of whether or not they take up official residence in Italy, if they submit the declaration of paid residence pursuant to art. 8(2) below.

items.text: EU and non-EU international students enrolled in a doctoral course or specialisation school who take up residence in Italy and apply for EDISU Piemonte benefits by submitting an ISEE Dottorato (PhD ISEE) certificate or ISEE con opzione Nucleo Ristretto (ISEE with “restricted Household” option) will instead be considered on the basis of their residence in Italy.

classification.raw: EU and non-EU international students enrolled in a doctoral course or specialisation school who take up residence in Italy and apply for EDISU Piemonte benefits by submitting an ISEE Dottorato (PhD ISEE) certificate or ISEE con opzione Nucleo Ristretto (ISEE with “restricted Household” option) will instead be considered on the basis of their residence in Italy.

items.text: If international students take up residency in Italy and this is the same as at least one member of their household (parent, uncle, cousin, sibling, etc.) who generates income of any kind, the type of student will be considered on the basis of the residency taken up in Italy.

classification.raw: If international students take up residency in Italy and this is the same as at least one member of their household (parent, uncle, cousin, sibling, etc.) who generates income of any kind, the type of student will be considered on the basis of the residency taken up in Italy.

items.text: The travel time is calculated using the times defined by the companies operating public transport. These times are transposed by the “Muoversi in Piemonte” system (reference for the Piedmont Region) and similar detection systems such as Google and train timetables at local transport sites.

classification.raw: The travel time is calculated using the times defined by the companies operating public transport. These times are transposed by the “Muoversi in Piemonte” system (reference for the Piedmont Region) and similar detection systems such as Google and train timetables at local transport sites.

items.text: The routes taken into account are those between the railway or bus station of the student's municipality of residence and that of the municipality where they are studying, in addition to any walking route connecting two stops. Only those routes are examined that ensure, as quickly as possible, the arrival of students in the municipality where they are studying by 8 a.m. and the return of students to their municipality of residence by 8 p.m. in the evening. For the City of Turin, the reference station is the first arrival/departure station on the route concerned. For the City of Milan, the reference station is the Central Station.

classification.raw: The routes taken into account are those between the railway or bus station of the student's municipality of residence and that of the municipality where they are studying, in addition to any walking route connecting two stops. Only those routes are examined that ensure, as quickly as possible, the arrival of students in the municipality where they are studying by 8 a.m. and the return of students to their municipality of residence by 8 p.m. in the evening. For the City of Turin, the reference station is the first arrival/departure station on the route concerned. For the City of Milan, the reference station is the Central Station.

items.text: For the purposes of the calculation, only municipalities are observed and any places and administrative divisions belonging to them are not taken into account.

classification.raw: For the purposes of the calculation, only municipalities are observed and any places and administrative divisions belonging to them are not taken into account.

items.text: 1. students enrolled in years subsequent to the first year, who are eligible in the final scholarship rankings, are eligible15 for a free daily meal in EDISU canteens or alternative services during the calendar year 2026, starting in January. Students who apply for the additional semester of their course can take advantage of the free daily meal until 31 March 2026. In the event of circumstances that prevent the free daily meal (such as participation in international mobility programmes, carrying out internships or other), the value of the meals is not monetised under any circumstances; 2. students enrolled in the first years who are eligible in the final scholarship rankings may have their meals in EDISU canteens or alternative services at the band 1 rate, which for the academic year 2025/26 is \notin 3 . 0, 0 for a full meal16; 3. students applying for the accommodation service who are eligible in the accommodation service and final scholarship ranking are granted, if available, a place in EDISU residences or in residences that have an agreement with the organisation.

items.text: On 31 October 2025 (for first-year students of single-cycle bachelor’s degree and master’s degree courses who enrol by 31 October 2025), 30 November 2025 (for first-year and subsequent-year students who enrol by 30 November 2025) and 3 April 2026 (for first-year and subsequent-year students suspended in the December rankings with eligibility to compete in the final April rankings), checks will be carried out on enrolment and type of commitment. Students enrolled on a part-time basis on that date, if they are awarded a scholarship in the definitive rankings, will be paid the amount indicated above and there will be no supplement if, after that date, they change their type of commitment from “part time” to “full time”.

classification.raw: On 31 October 2025 (for first-year students of single-cycle bachelor’s degree and master’s degree courses who enrol by 31 October 2025), 30 November 2025 (for first-year and subsequent-year students who enrol by 30 November 2025) and 3 April 2026 (for first-year and subsequent-year students suspended in the December rankings with eligibility to compete in the final April rankings), checks will be carried out on enrolment and type of commitment. Students enrolled on a part-time basis on that date, if they are awarded a scholarship in the definitive rankings, will be paid the amount indicated above and there will be no supplement if, after that date, they change their type of commitment from “part time” to “full time”.

items.text: Students from Ukraine holding a temporary residence permit do NOT have to submit the ISEE Universitario or any consular documents relating to their family of origin. They are only required to send the documents relating to the part of the family present in Italy with whom they may reside to EDISU Piemonte according to the methods identified in art. 31(1) of this Notice of competition. These documents include: the identity document and copy of the residence permit of all the family members and any current account balance, including average balance as of 31 December 2024, for the processing of the ISEE Parificato by the EDISU Piemonte CAAF operators.

items.text: Art. 6(1.2) INCOME ACCRUED IN THE 2023 TAX YEAR FOR ISEE CALCULATION

items.text: For the purposes of determining the ISEE Universitario, any declared or assimilated income received in the 2023 tax year must be considered, as well as any other tax-exempt income component, including scholarships and all EDISU contributions received in the 2023 tax year (with the exception of support for disabled students and reimbursements for regional tax or travel expenses), part- time collaborations with EDISU, Universities or other Bodies, income from employment abroad exclusively taxed in the foreign country, child support payments actually received, welfare, social security and allowances (not already included in total income).

classification.raw: For the purposes of determining the ISEE Universitario, any declared or assimilated income received in the 2023 tax year must be considered, as well as any other tax-exempt income component, including scholarships and all EDISU contributions received in the 2023 tax year (with the exception of support for disabled students and reimbursements for regional tax or travel expenses), part- time collaborations with EDISU, Universities or other Bodies, income from employment abroad exclusively taxed in the foreign country, child support payments actually received, welfare, social security and allowances (not already included in total income).

items.text: Only non-EU international students must also refer to income received in the year 2024 to determine the calculation of the ISEE Parificato (for more information, please refer to the section on international students, art. 30 below).

classification.raw: Only non-EU international students must also refer to income received in the year 2024 to determine the calculation of the ISEE Parificato (for more information, please refer to the section on international students, art. 30 below).

items.text: Pursuant to the Council of State's Ruling of 29 February 201630, it is noted that amounts deriving from disability INDEMNITY are NOT to be considered INCOME and therefore should NOT be indicated in the DSU.

items.text: For the purposes of completing the ISEE Universitario/Parificato statement, the income received by all members of the household must be declared for ISEE Univesitario/Parificato purposes even in the case of exemption from the obligation to submit a tax return and the same is not subject to IRPEF.

classification.raw: For the purposes of completing the ISEE Universitario/Parificato statement, the income received by all members of the household must be declared for ISEE Univesitario/Parificato purposes even in the case of exemption from the obligation to submit a tax return and the same is not subject to IRPEF.

items.text: For the purpose of determining the Asset Situation Indicator (ISP), the amount of movable assets (including the average stock) and immovable assets of all the members of the household for ISEE Universitario/Parificato purposes as at 31/12/2023 must be indicated31; non-EU students must instead refer to the date of 31/12/2024 (please refer to the section on international students, art. 30 below).

items.text: For the purpose of calculating the ISEE for subsidised benefits for the right to university study to apply for a scholarship and, if non- resident, the accommodation service, the amount of the scholarship from EDISU Piemonte or another body for the right to university study, received in the 2023 tax year, is subsequently deducted by the Granting Body, dividing it by the equivalence scale and then subtracting the result of this division from the previously calculated ISEE value. NO amounts relating to other contributions granted by EDISU or scholarship amounts received by other household members for ISEE purposes in the same tax year other than the benefit applicant may be deducted.

NO amounts relating to other contributions granted by EDISU or scholarship amounts received by other household members for ISEE purposes in the same tax year other than the benefit applicant may be deducted.

items.text: It is advisable to check the composition of one's household by means of a family status certificate updated at the time of applying for the scholarship, and to go to the CAAF for completing a new ISEE Universitario/Parificato certificate if there is a discrepancy.

items.text: Please note that the household for ISEE Universitario purposes is defined primarily on the basis of the registered household, to which any components such as the spouse with a different residence or registered with AIRE, as well as the natural parent not cohabiting with the recognised child, must be added. It is, therefore, of the utmost importance to make one's family situation explicit when signing the DSU, since it is the initial and fundamental element for defining the DSU and is one of the few elements that is still self- declared based on Presidential Decree 445/2000.

items.text: Art. 6(1.5) “INDEPENDENT STUDENT” HOUSEHOLD

classification.title: Art. 6 (1.5) “INDEPENDENT STUDENT” HOUSEHOLD
classification.raw: Art. 6(1.5) “INDEPENDENT STUDENT” HOUSEHOLD

items.text: In order to be granted the scholarship, students constitute an independent household32 from that of their family of origin if both of the following requirements are met:

classification.raw: In order to be granted the scholarship, students constitute an independent household32 from that of their family of origin if both of the following requirements are met:

items.text: • residence outside the housing unit of the family of origin, for at least two actual years from the date of submission of the application, in housing not owned by a member of the family; • presence of adequate personal income 3 3 received in the tax years 2, 0, 2, 3 and 2024 not less than € 9,000.00, 35 for each year. This amount includes any tax-declared or assimilated income or self-employment income received in the tax years 2023 and 2024, as well as any other tax-exempt income components, income from employment abroad exclusively taxed in the foreign country, child support payments actually received, welfare, social security and indemnity payments (not already included in total income).

items: • residence outside the housing unit of the family of origin, for at least two actual years from the date of submission of the application, in housing not owned by a member of the family;

• presence of adequate personal income 3 3 received in the tax years 2, 0, 2, 3 and 2024 not less than € 9,000.00, 35 for each year. This amount includes any tax-declared or assimilated income or self-employment income received in the tax years 2023 and 2024, as well as any other tax-exempt income components, income from employment abroad exclusively taxed in the foreign country, child support payments actually received, welfare, social security and indemnity payments (not already included in total income).
classification.raw: • residence outside the housing unit of the family of origin, for at least two actual years from the date of submission of the application, in housing not owned by a member of the family; • presence of adequate personal income 3 3 received in the tax years 2, 0, 2, 3 and 2024 not less than € 9,000.00, 35 for each year. This amount includes any tax-declared or assimilated income or self-employment income received in the tax years 2023 and 2024, as well as any other tax-exempt income components, income from employment abroad exclusively taxed in the foreign country, child support payments actually received, welfare, social security and indemnity payments (not already included in total income).

items.text: When filling out the application for benefits, to confirm their status, the independent student is required to enclose the rental contract or deed of ownership of their home, even if it is encumbered by a mortgage.

classification.raw: When filling out the application for benefits, to confirm their status, the independent student is required to enclose the rental contract or deed of ownership of their home, even if it is encumbered by a mortgage.

items.text: If the independent student does not submit documentation regarding the use of paid accommodation, they will not be considered non-resident for the purposes of the scholarship amount but will receive the commuter amount.

classification.raw: If the independent student does not submit documentation regarding the use of paid accommodation, they will not be considered non-resident for the purposes of the scholarship amount but will receive the commuter amount.

items.text: In all cases where students, although no longer living with their family of origin, do not meet the requirements to be considered independent, reference is made to their parents' income and assets in accordance with current legislation.

classification.raw: In all cases where students, although no longer living with their family of origin, do not meet the requirements to be considered independent, reference is made to their parents' income and assets in accordance with current legislation.

items.text: In the event that married students do not meet the threshold for suitable income required for independent status, their spouse's personal income36 received in tax years 2023 and 2024 must be taken into account. If the latter also does not reach the minimum amount of € 9,000.00, the requirement can be met by adding the student's income with that of their spouse.37 Therefore, the DSU will only refer to the household that students have formed with their spouse, excluding their family of origin. In all other cases where the personal income received does not reach the required minimum limit for the 2023 and 2024 tax years (€ 9,000.00 for each year), students must refer to their family of origin, thus excluding their spouse and any children.

classification.raw: In the event that married students do not meet the threshold for suitable income required for independent status, their spouse's personal income36 received in tax years 2023 and 2024 must be taken into account. If the latter also does not reach the minimum amount of € 9,000.00, the requirement can be met by adding the student's income with that of their spouse.37 Therefore, the DSU will only refer to the household that students have formed with their spouse, excluding their family of origin. In all other cases where the personal income received does not reach the required minimum limit for the 2023 and 2024 tax years (€ 9,000.00 for each year), students must refer to their family of origin, thus excluding their spouse and any children.

items.text: If independent students are found to be living with other persons in their registered family status, the income of all the persons in the registered household, even if there are no family ties, must be taken into account.

classification.raw: If independent students are found to be living with other persons in their registered family status, the income of all the persons in the registered household, even if there are no family ties, must be taken into account.

items.text: Please note: students enrolled in a doctoral course or specialisation school cannot apply for benefits as independent students since this case only applies to the ISEE Universitario and not to the ISEE Dottorato. Please refer to article 24(3) below.

items.text: To apply for a scholarship and, if non-resident, for accommodation service, students must apply for an ISEE certificate that applies to the Facilitated Benefits for the Right to University Study (hereinafter referred to as ISEE Universitario)38 in respect of students applying for benefits, at an Authorised Tax Assistance Centre (CAAF), or, if they have the SPID, by connecting to the INPS website39. Please note that, in order to obtain the ISEE Universitario, it is mandatory to fill in box C (University Benefits) of the Single Self- Declaration (Dichiarazione Sostitutiva Unica, DSU). In the event that the ISEE Universitario has observations regarding omissions/discrepancies, EDISU Piemonte requires that the documentation proving the correctness of the contents of the DSU be sent, as indicated in the INPS and Ministry of Labour message no. 8415 of 26/10/2020.

classification.raw: To apply for a scholarship and, if non-resident, for accommodation service, students must apply for an ISEE certificate that applies to the Facilitated Benefits for the Right to University Study (hereinafter referred to as ISEE Universitario)38 in respect of students applying for benefits, at an Authorised Tax Assistance Centre (CAAF), or, if they have the SPID, by connecting to the INPS website39. Please note that, in order to obtain the ISEE Universitario, it is mandatory to fill in box C (University Benefits) of the Single Self- Declaration (Dichiarazione Sostitutiva Unica, DSU). items.text: You may fill in the application for EDISU benefits if you have a ISEE Universitario certificate for the person applying for benefits, or with only the receipt attesting to the signing of the DSU at the CAAF40 without the relative processing of the certificate by INPS.

items.text: Please note that the issue of the ISEE Universitario is NOT immediate. On the basis of the information contained in the DSU and other information collected at INPS and the Italian Revenue Agency, a certificate containing the ISEE calculation and the information used to obtain it will be issued within ten working days. Therefore, in order to have the ISEE Universitario within the deadline for complaints (art. 7(6)), you should not contact the CAAF in the days immediately preceding the deadlines for requesting the benefits indicated in the following Notice of competition to sign the DSU.

items.text: Students will be listed as “excluded” in the provisional ranking when:

classification.title: Students will be listed as “excluded” in the provisional ranking when:
classification.raw: Students will be listed as “excluded” in the provisional ranking when:

items.text: • the data of the ISEE certificate or the Single Self-Declaration are not available in the INPS database. The data can only be rectified if the applicant has a valid DSU for the right to study, signed before the deadline for applying for benefits; • the last ISEE Certificate in the INPS database is not valid for the right to university study for the benefit applicant. The data can only be rectified if the applicant has a valid ISEE certificate for the right to study, processed by INPS by the deadline for complaints relating to the benefit requested; • the certificate is found to have an ISEE value that cannot be calculated, and therefore no certificate or DSU was found in the INPS database by the expiry date of the benefit application. The data cannot be rectified.

items: • the data of the ISEE certificate or the Single Self-Declaration are not available in the INPS database. The data can only be rectified if the applicant has a valid DSU for the right to study, signed before the deadline for applying for benefits;

The data can only be rectified if the applicant has a valid ISEE certificate for the right to study, processed by INPS by the deadline for complaints relating to the benefit requested;

• the certificate is found to have an ISEE value that cannot be calculated, and therefore no certificate or DSU was found in the INPS database by the expiry date of the benefit application. The data cannot be rectified.

classification.raw: Following the online retrieval of ISEE data, students will receive a receipt containing the ISEE data retrieved from the INPS database. The same shall be sent within 48 working hours of receipt of the e-mail with the subject line “Receipt of file” as described in art. 7(1.1) and (2) below. The receipt entitled “INPS Data Receipt” may contain:

items.text: • the summary of the ISEE Universitario-Equivalence Scale-ISP values deriving from the last ISEE Universitario certificate found in the INPS database and the relative recalculation carried out automatically for the purposes of the request; • any notice of non-acceptance of the same certificate for the purposes of the competition, i.e.: the non-validity of the ISEE for the right to university study for the person requesting the benefits, the failure to find data relating to the ISEE certificate due to the absence of valid certificates in the INPS database;

items.text: If an ISEE, even an ISEE Universitario, is found that contains observations regarding an “omission or discrepancy”, in the INPS Data receipt, the students will be required to transmit the documentation proving the correctness of the contents of the DSU, as indicated in the INPS and Ministry of Labour message no. 8415 of 26/10/2020. Documentation may be submitted via the Data modification procedure by 12:00 p.m. on 9 September 2025, the application deadline, or later via the ticketing system, ISEE economic requirements section. It is also possible to submit a new ISEE Universitario Certificate that rectifies the omissions/ discrepancies found in the previous one.

items.text: In the event that the automatic search for the last ISEE statement processed for the year 2025 in the INPS database does not produce any results, students will still be sent the receipt entitled “INPS Data”. In it, however, the failure to find the ISEE certificate data will be specified. In this case, EDISU Piemonte will regularly query the INPS database until the deadline for the benefit complaint. If an ISEE certificate processed in the year 2024 is found by that date, students will then receive a new “INPS Data” receipt containing, however, a summary of the ISEE-SE-ISP values found and/or any notice of non-acceptance. Otherwise, students will be excluded in the provisional ranking.

items.text: If students, when completing the online application in the appropriate box in the economic data section, have indicated a scholarship amount received in the 2023 tax year, the amount will be automatically deducted. Students will be included in the definitive ranking with the ISEE and ISPE indicators, recalculated net of the grant amount entered, relating to the last valid certificate for Right to University Study benefits. The “INPS Data” receipt described above will show the recalculated ISEE and ISPE values.

items.text: Art. 6(3) ISEE CORRENTE (CURRENT ISEE)

classification.title: Art. 6 (3) ISEE CORRENTE (CURRENT ISEE)
classification.raw: Art. 6(3) ISEE CORRENTE (CURRENT ISEE)

items.text: If there are significant changes in income as a result of adverse events (e.g. loss of job, suspension or reduction of work activity or interruption of social welfare benefits) or a reduction in assets, INPS offers citizens the possibility to calculate a ISEE Corrente41 based on the income of the last twelve months (or two months in the case of a permanent employee) and, from 1 April of each year, on the assets held as at 31 December of the year preceding the DSU (31 December 2023). The ISEE Corrente may also be issued following a significant change in the household's income, greater than 2, 5 \% or in the event of a reduction in assets of more than 2, 0 \% ^ { 4, 2 } , compared to the income or asset situation identified in the ISEE calculated according to the ordinary rules. Before requesting the calculation of the ISEE Corrente, an ISEE certificate, calculated according to the ordinary rules, must already have been issued.

classification.raw: If there are significant changes in income as a result of adverse events (e.g. loss of job, suspension or reduction of work activity or interruption of social welfare benefits) or a reduction in assets, INPS offers citizens the possibility to calculate a ISEE Corrente41 based on the income of the last twelve months (or two months in the case of a permanent employee) and, from 1 April of each year, on the assets held as at 31 December of the year preceding the DSU (31 December 2023). The ISEE Corrente may also be issued following a significant change in the household's income, greater than 2, 5 \% or in the event of a reduction in assets of more than 2, 0 \% ^ { 4, 2 } , compared to the income or asset situation identified in the ISEE calculated according to the ordinary rules. Before requesting the calculation of the ISEE Corrente, an ISEE certificate, calculated according to the ordinary rules, must already have been issued.

items.text: The ISEE Corrente must be valid on the date of submission of the application for the benefit requested (or on the date of submission If only incomes are updated, the ISEE Corrente [Current ISEE] is valid for six months from the time of submission of this DSU, unless there are changes in the employment situation or in the receipt of benefits, in which case the ISEE Corrente must be updated within two months of the change. If only assets or assets and income are updated, the ISEE Corrente is valid until 31 December 2025. If during the ISEE CORRENTE validity period, in which the income component has been updated, or only this, changes occur in the employment situation or in the receipt of benefits, the ISEE Corrente must be updated within two months of the change. If the ISEE corrente certificate is the latest one in the INPS database, it will be retrieved electronically (section 2.2 above): it is the student's responsibility to check the data in the “INPS data receipt” and, if there are any anomalies, to proceed with the modification of the economic data using the appropriate procedures.

If the ISEE corrente certificate is the latest one in the INPS database, it will be retrieved electronically (section 2.2 above): it is the student's responsibility to check the data in the “INPS data receipt” and, if there are any anomalies, to proceed with the modification of the economic data using the appropriate procedures.

items.text: The household of students resident in Italy enrolled exclusively in PhD courses or specialisation schools may ONLY be made up of the applicants themselves and any spouses, children under the age of 18 (nucleo RISTRETTO, RESTRICTED household)43, as well as children over the age of 18, without prejudice to the possibility for beneficiaries to form the household according to the ordinary 44 rules

classification.raw: The household of students resident in Italy enrolled exclusively in PhD courses or specialisation schools may ONLY be made up of the applicants themselves and any spouses, children under the age of 18 (nucleo RISTRETTO, RESTRICTED household)43, as well as children over the age of 18, without prejudice to the possibility for beneficiaries to form the household according to the ordinary 44 rules

items.text: It should be noted that the ISEE nucleo RISTRETTO may also be requested by students enrolled in PhD courses or specialisation schools whose registered household is made up of their family of origin. In this case, an explicit request must be made to the CAAF when completing the DSU by filling in box A “RESTRICTED HOUSEHOLD”. Please note that in order to take advantage of the “Nucleo Ristretto” (Restricted Household) option, interested students must sign the DSU for the issue of the ISEE certificate as “declarant”. Please note that students enrolled in PhD courses or specialisation schools residing abroad must present an ISEE Parificato certificate, and therefore will not be able to take advantage of the “restricted household” option.

Please note that students enrolled in PhD courses or specialisation schools residing abroad must present an ISEE Parificato certificate, and therefore will not be able to take advantage of the “restricted household” option.

items.text: Art. 6(5) ECONOMIC REQUIREMENTS FOR CALCULATION OF ISEE PARIFICATO

classification.title: Art. 6 (5) ECONOMIC REQUIREMENTS FOR CALCULATION OF ISEE PARIFICATO
classification.raw: Art. 6(5) ECONOMIC REQUIREMENTS FOR CALCULATION OF ISEE PARIFICATO

items.text: The ISEE Parificato takes into account income generated and assets owned abroad even by household members who are not resident in Italy.

classification.raw: The ISEE Parificato takes into account income generated and assets owned abroad even by household members who are not resident in Italy.

items.text: EDISU Piemonte only accepts the ISEE Parificato drawn up by the operators of affiliated CAAFs and calculated directly in EDISU. Even if valid, ISEE Parificato certificates handed in by students and processed c/o CAAF offices, even if affiliated with the organisation, will not be accepted under any circumstances.

classification.raw: EDISU Piemonte only accepts the ISEE Parificato drawn up by the operators of affiliated CAAFs and calculated directly in EDISU. Even if valid, ISEE Parificato certificates handed in by students and processed c/o CAAF offices, even if affiliated with the organisation, will not be accepted under any circumstances.

items.text: For the calculation of the ISEE Parificato, it is compulsory to submit consular documentation, translated and legalised in accordance with the regulations in force, relating to the composition of the household, and the income produced and assets held abroad by all the members of the household (for more information, please refer to article 30).

classification.raw: For the calculation of the ISEE Parificato, it is compulsory to submit consular documentation, translated and legalised in accordance with the regulations in force, relating to the composition of the household, and the income produced and assets held abroad by all the members of the household (for more information, please refer to article 30).

items.text: The Equivalent indicator is issued exclusively by the CAAFs affiliated with EDISU Piemonte present in EDISU Piemonte premises, which will process the ISEE Parificato following the compliant evaluation of the consular documentation. Please note that the evaluation of consular documentation and the processing of the calculation of the ISEE Parificato will only be visible to students when the provisional or definitive ranking of the benefit applied for is published; therefore, it is very important to carefully read the list of consular documentation contained in art. 30, l’Allegato E for international students and, in case of doubt, it is advisable to contact the EDISU offices through the ticketing system.

classification.raw: The Equivalent indicator is issued exclusively by the CAAFs affiliated with EDISU Piemonte present in EDISU Piemonte premises, which will process the ISEE Parificato following the compliant evaluation of the consular documentation. Please note that the evaluation of consular documentation and the processing of the calculation of the ISEE Parificato will only be visible to students when the provisional or definitive ranking of the benefit applied for is published; therefore, it is very important to carefully read the list of consular documentation contained in art. 30, l’Allegato E for international students and, in case of doubt, it is advisable to contact the EDISU offices through the ticketing system.

items.text: Please note that consular documentation will not be evaluated through the ticketing system and that offices are only required to provide information.

classification.raw: Please note that consular documentation will not be evaluated through the ticketing system and that offices are only required to provide information.

items.text: Non-EU and EU international students with a household not resident in Italy must follow the provisions of art. 30 and 31.

classification.raw: Non-EU and EU international students with a household not resident in Italy must follow the provisions of art. 30 and 31.

items.text: These directives also apply to Italian students whose household members (all or some of them) reside abroad without being registered with AIRE (Register of Italians Resident Abroad), as well as in the case of a household wholly resident abroad. Students in this situation are invited to refer to paragraph 5.1 below for further details on the documentation required and the method of submitting the application for benefits.

classification.raw: These directives also apply to Italian students whose household members (all or some of them) reside abroad without being registered with AIRE (Register of Italians Resident Abroad), as well as in the case of a household wholly resident abroad. Students in this situation are invited to refer to paragraph 5.1 below for further details on the documentation required and the method of submitting the application for benefits.

items.text: The AIRE is regulated under art. 27 of Presidential Decree 223/89 and contains the data of Italian citizens residing abroad for a period of more than twelve months. This registration, which is not compulsory, entails the cancellation of national residence, so if both parents are registered there, the economic and asset situation of the household must be reported in the ISEE Parificato.

classification.raw: The AIRE is regulated under art. 27 of Presidential Decree 223/89 and contains the data of Italian citizens residing abroad for a period of more than twelve months. This registration, which is not compulsory, entails the cancellation of national residence, so if both parents are registered there, the economic and asset situation of the household must be reported in the ISEE Parificato.

items.text: Please note that the consular documentation must be sent to EDISU Piemonte, under penalty of definitive exclusion from the competition:

classification.raw: Please note that the consular documentation must be sent to EDISU Piemonte, under penalty of definitive exclusion from the competition:

items.text: • according to the timetable and methods described in article 31 below for non-EU and EU international students; • according to the time frames and methods described in paragraph 5.1 below for students with Italian citizenship (including students with dual citizenship who use Italian citizenship).

items: • according to the timetable and methods described in article 31 below for non-EU and EU international students;
• according to the time frames and methods described in paragraph 5.1 below for students with Italian citizenship (including students with dual citizenship who use Italian citizenship).
classification.raw: • according to the timetable and methods described in article 31 below for non-EU and EU international students; • according to the time frames and methods described in paragraph 5.1 below for students with Italian citizenship (including students with dual citizenship who use Italian citizenship).

items.text: Nucleo Ristretto, art. 8(4) Prime Ministerial Decree 159/2013 t. 8(3) Prime Ministerial Decree 159/2013

classification.raw: Nucleo Ristretto, art. 8(4) Prime Ministerial Decree 159/2013 t. 8(3) Prime Ministerial Decree 159/2013

items.text: The ISEE Parificato is calculated by EDISU exclusively for internal use and only once the documentation has been assessed as suitable and complete. The ISEE Parificato is not calculated in case of incomplete or inadequate documentation, nor can it be used to apply for benefits outside of EDISU, unless otherwise specified by official regulations.

classification.raw: The ISEE Parificato is calculated by EDISU exclusively for internal use and only once the documentation has been assessed as suitable and complete. The ISEE Parificato is not calculated in case of incomplete or inadequate documentation, nor can it be used to apply for benefits outside of EDISU, unless otherwise specified by official regulations.

items.text: The ISEE Parificato is calculated on the basis of the data obtained from the first complete and correct documentation received by EDISU: no further documentation on incomes and assets will be accepted in order to vary the value of the ISEE Parificato already calculated by EDISU.

classification.raw: The ISEE Parificato is calculated on the basis of the data obtained from the first complete and correct documentation received by EDISU: no further documentation on incomes and assets will be accepted in order to vary the value of the ISEE Parificato already calculated by EDISU.

items.text: If a student believes that the ISEE Parificato calculation carried out by EDISU does not reflect the real economic situation of their family and that there have been errors in its calculation, they may communicate this exclusively through the complaints procedure (art. 7 paragraph 6). For this reason, too, students are invited to submit their documentation by the deadlines indicated in art. 31, paragraphs 1, 2 and 3, so that their ISEE value can be displayed in the provisional ranking.

classification.raw: If a student believes that the ISEE Parificato calculation carried out by EDISU does not reflect the real economic situation of their family and that there have been errors in its calculation, they may communicate this exclusively through the complaints procedure (art. 7 paragraph 6). For this reason, too, students are invited to submit their documentation by the deadlines indicated in art. 31, paragraphs 1, 2 and 3, so that their ISEE value can be displayed in the provisional ranking.

items.text: Art. 6 paragraph 5.1 SPECIFICATIONS FOR STUDENTS WITH ITALIAN CITIZENSHIP WHO NEED TO CALCULATE THE ISEE PARIFICATO

classification.title: Art. 6 paragraph 5.1 SPECIFICATIONS FOR STUDENTS WITH ITALIAN CITIZENSHIP WHO NEED TO CALCULATE THE ISEE PARIFICATO
classification.raw: Art. 6 paragraph 5.1 SPECIFICATIONS FOR STUDENTS WITH ITALIAN CITIZENSHIP WHO NEED TO CALCULATE THE ISEE PARIFICATO

items.text: Italian students (including students with dual citizenship who use their Italian citizenship) who need to calculate the ISEE Parificato must submit all the consular documentation indicated in article 30, paragraph 3, under penalty of exclusion from the competition. Please note that in the case of students with Italian citizenship, the documents relating to the income produced by the household both in Italy and abroad must only refer to the 2023 tax year, and to the date of 31/12/2023 as regards assets.

classification.raw: Italian students (including students with dual citizenship who use their Italian citizenship) who need to calculate the ISEE Parificato must submit all the consular documentation indicated in article 30, paragraph 3, under penalty of exclusion from the competition. Please note that in the case of students with Italian citizenship, the documents relating to the income produced by the household both in Italy and abroad must only refer to the 2023 tax year, and to the date of 31/12/2023 as regards assets.

items.text: It should be noted that students with Italian citizenship are also required to have foreign consular documentation legalised and translated into Italian if the document has been drawn up in another language. For further information in this regard, students are invited to carefully consult article 30, paragraph 3 below. This documentation must be submitted to EDISU Piemonte by the application deadline

classification.raw: It should be noted that students with Italian citizenship are also required to have foreign consular documentation legalised and translated into Italian if the document has been drawn up in another language. For further information in this regard, students are invited to carefully consult article 30, paragraph 3 below. This documentation must be submitted to EDISU Piemonte by the application deadline

items.text: Italian students (including students with dual citizenship who use Italian citizenship) who are required to calculate the ISEE Parificato must submit documentation on the composition of their household, foreign income and assets using the attachment procedure available when completing the online application. Students must therefore attach consular documentation (both the original language version and the version translated into Italian) relating to the composition of their household and to income and assets produced/possessed (both in Italy and abroad) by the student's household via the online procedure and by the application deadline, under penalty of exclusion from the competition.

classification.raw: Italian students (including students with dual citizenship who use Italian citizenship) who are required to calculate the ISEE Parificato must submit documentation on the composition of their household, foreign income and assets using the attachment procedure available when completing the online application. Students must therefore attach consular documentation (both the original language version and the version translated into Italian) relating to the composition of their household and to income and assets produced/possessed (both in Italy and abroad) by the student's household via the online procedure and by the application deadline, under penalty of exclusion from the competition.

items.text: If students are resident in Italy, before submitting their application for a scholarship and/or accommodation service, they must sign a DSU to request an ordinary ISEE containing the data of their household in Italy, which will subsequently be supplemented with the data relating to their family of origin. These data are calculated on the basis of the above-mentioned consular documentation, which must be translated and legalised in accordance with current legislation. The ordinary ISEE certificate must be attached to the scholarship application together with the required consular documentation.

classification.raw: If students are resident in Italy, before submitting their application for a scholarship and/or accommodation service, they must sign a DSU to request an ordinary ISEE containing the data of their household in Italy, which will subsequently be supplemented with the data relating to their family of origin. These data are calculated on the basis of the above-mentioned consular documentation, which must be translated and legalised in accordance with current legislation. The ordinary ISEE certificate must be attached to the scholarship application together with the required consular documentation.

items.text: If the ordinary ISEE is absent by the deadline for the application for benefits, the copy of the certificate and any missing consular documents for the calculation of the ISEE Parificato must be transmitted by the students by means of a complaint procedure, in accordance with the methods indicated in article 7, paragraph 6 below.

classification.raw: If the ordinary ISEE is absent by the deadline for the application for benefits, the copy of the certificate and any missing consular documents for the calculation of the ISEE Parificato must be transmitted by the students by means of a complaint procedure, in accordance with the methods indicated in article 7, paragraph 6 below.

items.text: Once the online application has been completed and submitted, the attached consular documentation will be examined by EDISU Piemonte operators and students will see the outcome of the evaluation of the documentation in the ranking.

classification.raw: Once the online application has been completed and submitted, the attached consular documentation will be examined by EDISU Piemonte operators and students will see the outcome of the evaluation of the documentation in the ranking.

items.text: For the evaluation to be published in the provisional ranking, it must be received by EDISU by the deadline for the online application (9 September 2025, 12:00 p.m.). If the documentation is received after this deadline but before the deadline for submitting complaints, the evaluation will be published directly in the definitive ranking, without the possibility of submitting further complaints. Therefore, students are invited to send in their documentation in advance, so that the provisional outcome of the assessment can be seen.

classification.raw: For the evaluation to be published in the provisional ranking, it must be received by EDISU by the deadline for the online application (9 September 2025, 12:00 p.m.). If the documentation is received after this deadline but before the deadline for submitting complaints, the evaluation will be published directly in the definitive ranking, without the possibility of submitting further complaints. Therefore, students are invited to send in their documentation in advance, so that the provisional outcome of the assessment can be seen.

items.text: In the event of a negative outcome of the consular documentation assessment, students may submit missing/corrected documentation in accordance with the provisions of article 7, paragraph 5.1 and 6.1 below.

classification.raw: In the event of a negative outcome of the consular documentation assessment, students may submit missing/corrected documentation in accordance with the provisions of article 7, paragraph 5.1 and 6.1 below.

items.text: After the new documentation has been received at EDISU via attachment in the online complaint procedure, the new outcome will be published in the definitive ranking.

classification.raw: After the new documentation has been received at EDISU via attachment in the online complaint procedure, the new outcome will be published in the definitive ranking.

items.text: The procedure for uploading the required documentation involves uploading a maximum of 5 attachments in .pdf version, each no larger than 1MB. Students must combine the different types of documents in a single file, e.g. a single file containing the ordinary and ISEE Parificato, another containing the composition of the household and any divorce decrees or death certificates, another containing all the income received by the household and one relating to movable assets (current accounts, savings, deposits, etc.) and real estate (buildings expressed in square metres).

items.text: Art. 7 para. 1 ACCESS CREDENTIALS AND ONLINE PROCEDURE

classification.raw: Art. 7 para. 1 ACCESS CREDENTIALS AND ONLINE PROCEDURE

items.text: To submit your application, you must log on to the www.edisu.piemonte.it website. From the “ONLINE SERVICES” section, access “Scholarships, Accommodation, Catering, Refunds and Contributions” (hereinafter referred to as the Online Helpdesk) within the deadlines indicated in arts. 10 paragraph 2 (first years), 15 paragraph 2 (subsequent years), and 25 paragraph 3 (PhDs and specialisation school), with your SPID, CIE or, if you are an international student without residence in Italy, with your Temporary Access Code academic year 2025/26.

classification.raw: To submit your application, you must log on to the www.edisu.piemonte.it website. From the “ONLINE SERVICES” section, access “Scholarships, Accommodation, Catering, Refunds and Contributions” (hereinafter referred to as the Online Helpdesk) within the deadlines indicated in arts. 10 paragraph 2 (first years), 15 paragraph 2 (subsequent years), and 25 paragraph 3 (PhDs and specialisation school), with your SPID, CIE or, if you are an international student without residence in Italy, with your Temporary Access Code academic year 2025/26.

items.text: When filling out the application, students must already be in possession of a ISEE Universitario certificate issued on or after 1 January 2025 or of the receipt attesting to the submission and signing of the DSU at the CAAF (without the relative processing of the certificate by INPS).

classification.raw: When filling out the application, students must already be in possession of a ISEE Universitario certificate issued on or after 1 January 2025 or of the receipt attesting to the submission and signing of the DSU at the CAAF (without the relative processing of the certificate by INPS).

items.text: When registering for its online services, EDISU Piemonte always asks you to specify an email address. It is recommended that this address be directly attributable to the requesting student, not to any other party, and that it be directly managed by them. items.text: Art. 7(1.2) TEMPORARY ACCESS CODE 2025/26

classification.raw: Art. 7(1.2) TEMPORARY ACCESS CODE 2025/26

items.text: The Temporary Access Code 2025/26 is a code that is requested starting in July and expires annually (it expires in June of the following year). It can only be used by international students who do not have a SPID and cannot apply for it because they are not resident in Italy. These students must perform the following steps:

classification.raw: The Temporary Access Code 2025/26 is a code that is requested starting in July and expires annually (it expires in June of the following year). It can only be used by international students who do not have a SPID and cannot apply for it because they are not resident in Italy. These students must perform the following steps:

items.text: 1. Register in the Online Helpdesk to obtain the Temporary Access Code 2025/26. 2. Fill in all the mandatory spaces of the online application for the requested benefit. 3. Press the “FINAL SAVE” button to complete filling in. After pressing it, you will no longer be able to edit the application, so make sure that you have entered all the information requested on the previous pages that need filling in. 4. Press the “PRINT” button: a new web tab will open in your browser where you will find the application form that must be printed on paper (except for students using the qualified electronic signature referred to in point 5 below) 5. Sign the application form printed in accordance with point 4 above in pen and in your own hand, or with a qualified electronic (or digital) signature45. Signatures entered in word or other forms will not be accepted. 6. Please attach only the following documents in PDF format, following the instructions in the procedure:

items.text: All documents must be correctly saved as .pdf and must not exceed 1 MB in size. A single PDF is required for each attachment, even if the attachment has several pages. Attachments in formats other than PDF (e.g. jpg, btm,) will not be accepted. If students attach a document that has not been correctly converted into a .pdf file, this file will be damaged and therefore not visible. Therefore, it will not be accepted for the purposes of the competition.

Attachments in formats other than PDF (e.g. jpg, btm,) will not be accepted. If students attach a document that has not been correctly converted into a .pdf file, this file will be damaged and therefore not visible. Therefore, it will not be accepted for the purposes of the competition.

items.text: Students are required to check the correctness and formats of the attached files.

classification.raw: Students are required to check the correctness and formats of the attached files.

items.text: The identity document must be scanned in its entirety, both front and back, and be legible in all its parts, including the signature. Images that cannot be displayed (pixelated, blurred, distorted, incomplete, etc.) must not be attached.

classification.raw: The identity document must be scanned in its entirety, both front and back, and be legible in all its parts, including the signature. Images that cannot be displayed (pixelated, blurred, distorted, incomplete, etc.) must not be attached.

items.text: If the student accesses the application with both the SPID/CIE and the Temporary Code, they must submit it in accordance with the provisions of this paragraph for submitting the application with the Temporary Code (print, sign, attach the application, and send)

items.text: It can take up to 6 hours from the request for the Temporary Access Code for it to be issued by EDISU. The applicant must take this into account when calculating the time needed to complete and submit the application within the deadlines set in the notice of competition, assuming full responsibility for it.

classification.raw: It can take up to 6 hours from the request for the Temporary Access Code for it to be issued by EDISU. The applicant must take this into account when calculating the time needed to complete and submit the application within the deadlines set in the notice of competition, assuming full responsibility for it.

items.text: Failure to sign the form or to present an identity document is grounds for exclusion that CANNOT be rectified with a complaint.

items.text: Please note that access codes are strictly personal, so applications are considered to be completed directly by students holding the Temporary Access Code 2025/26.

items.text: As this is a temporary code, students must always also attach a copy of their identity document. The temporary code 2025/26 will expire on 30 June 2026.
