items.text: In the event of problems related to the loss of the Temporary Access Code or password, the wrong e-mail address or a change of e-mail address, students should use the procedures available on the Online Helpdesk login page, under “Temporary Access Code 2025/26 Assistance”.

classification.raw: In the event of problems related to the loss of the Temporary Access Code or password, the wrong e-mail address or a change of e-mail address, students should use the procedures available on the Online Helpdesk login page, under “Temporary Access Code 2025/26 Assistance”.

items.text: Art. 7(2) RECEIPTS (CONFIRMATIONS)

classification.title: Art. 7 (2) RECEIPTS (CONFIRMATIONS)
classification.raw: Art. 7(2) RECEIPTS (CONFIRMATIONS)

items.text: Following the transmission of the application, two receipts are sent to the e-mail address indicated:

classification.raw: Following the transmission of the application, two receipts are sent to the e-mail address indicated:

items.text: 1. The first confirmation, with the subject “Acquisizione dati via web” (acquisition of web data), is sent after the online transmission of the application.

classification.title: The first confirmation, with the subject “Acquisizione dati via web” (acquisition of web data), is sent after the online transmission of the application.
classification.raw: 1. The first confirmation, with the subject “Acquisizione dati via web” (acquisition of web data), is sent after the online transmission of the application.

items.text: For students who have applied with a Temporary Access Code, the documents previously signed and attached to the application procedure are available attached. For students who have applied with a SPID, a PDF of the application form is attached and should be kept.

classification.raw: For students who have applied with a Temporary Access Code, the documents previously signed and attached to the application procedure are available attached. For students who have applied with a SPID, a PDF of the application form is attached and should be kept.

items.text: 2. The second confirmation, with the subject “Ricezione pratica” (receipt of file), is sent after the application has been imported into the EDISU database. The protocol number assigned to the application is indicated in the text.

classification.title: The second confirmation, with the subject “Ricezione pratica” (receipt of file), is sent after the application has been imported into the EDISU database. The protocol number assigned to the application is indicated in the text.
classification.raw: 2. The second confirmation, with the subject “Ricezione pratica” (receipt of file), is sent after the application has been imported into the EDISU database. The protocol number assigned to the application is indicated in the text.

items.text: The application is deemed to have been correctly transmitted if and only if the first confirmation is delivered. Receipts will remain available on the students' personal page of the Online Helpdesk under “Ricevute” (Receipts).

classification.raw: The application is deemed to have been correctly transmitted if and only if the first confirmation is delivered. Receipts will remain available on the students' personal page of the Online Helpdesk under “Ricevute” (Receipts).

items.text: Students who entered the ISEE protocol or the DSU issuing protocol when completing the application will receive another confirmation within 48 working hours of the second confirmation with the subject “Ricevuta dati INPS” (INPS data receipt) containing the data relating to EDISU’s query of the INPS database. If EDISU does not find any valid ISEE, the reason for the lack of validity will be indicated.

classification.raw: Students who entered the ISEE protocol or the DSU issuing protocol when completing the application will receive another confirmation within 48 working hours of the second confirmation with the subject “Ricevuta dati INPS” (INPS data receipt) containing the data relating to EDISU’s query of the INPS database. If EDISU does not find any valid ISEE, the reason for the lack of validity will be indicated.

items.text: Students whose confirmation with the subject “Ricevuta dati INPS” (INPS data receipt) has the wording “In the INPS data bank there is an ISEE Certificate not valid for the right to university study for the applicant benefits” must enter the ISEE data relating to the new and corrected ISEE Universitario Certificate in the data modification procedure by 12:00 p.m. on 9 September 2025, the deadline for submitting the application.

classification.raw: Students whose confirmation with the subject “Ricevuta dati INPS” (INPS data receipt) has the wording “In the INPS data bank there is an ISEE Certificate not valid for the right to university study for the applicant benefits” must enter the ISEE data relating to the new and corrected ISEE Universitario Certificate in the data modification procedure by 12:00 p.m. on 9 September 2025, the deadline for submitting the application.

items.text: Students whose confirmation with the subject “Ricevuta dati INPS” (INPS data receipt) has the wording “ISEE with observations on omissions and/or discrepancies” must send the documentation proving the correctness of the contents of the DSU, as indicated in the INPS and Ministry of Labour message no. 8415 of 26/10/2020. Documentation may be submitted via the data modification procedure by 12:00 p.m. on 9 September 2025, the application deadline, or later via the ticketing system, ISEE economic requirements section.

classification.raw: Students whose confirmation with the subject “Ricevuta dati INPS” (INPS data receipt) has the wording “ISEE with observations on omissions and/or discrepancies” must send the documentation proving the correctness of the contents of the DSU, as indicated in the INPS and Ministry of Labour message no. 8415 of 26/10/2020. Documentation may be submitted via the data modification procedure by 12:00 p.m. on 9 September 2025, the application deadline, or later via the ticketing system, ISEE economic requirements section.

items.text: They may also submit a complaint, referred to in paragraph 6 below, attaching the ISEE and DSU with observations, together with the documentation proving the discrepancies found.

classification.raw: They may also submit a complaint, referred to in paragraph 6 below, attaching the ISEE and DSU with observations, together with the documentation proving the discrepancies found.

items.text: Applications submitted even partially differently from those described in this article will not be accepted. The online application must be submitted by 12:00 p.m. on the deadline day. The procedure closes automatically at 12:00 p.m. on that day and the countdown is reset. After 12:00 p.m. of the deadline, it will not be possible to submit the application even if the failure to submit is due to force majeure or the fault of a third party (e.g. problems with the internet connection or the terminal being used). Any event occurring after the date on which the application is submitted (award of another scholarship or other financial aid, transfer to another university, withdrawal from studies, change of commitment from part-time to full-time and vice versa, inability to continue studies, withdrawal from enrolment, change of department or course of study, change of course venue, etc.) must be promptly communicated to the EDISU offices and documented. EDISU will check that the requirements are still met and will take any measures necessary to revoke benefits. If this isn’t the case, EDISU reserves the right to take action in relation to the failure to notify.

classification.raw: Applications submitted even partially differently from those described in this article will not be accepted. The online application must be submitted by 12:00 p.m. on the deadline day. The procedure closes automatically at 12:00 p.m. on that day and the countdown is reset. After 12:00 p.m. of the deadline, it will not be possible to submit the application even if the failure to submit is due to force majeure or the fault of a third party (e.g. problems with the internet connection or the terminal being used). Any event occurring after the date on which the application is submitted (award of another scholarship or other financial aid, transfer to another university, withdrawal from studies, change of commitment from part-time to full-time and vice versa, inability to continue studies, withdrawal from enrolment, change of department or course of study, change of course venue, etc.) must be promptly communicated to the EDISU offices and documented. EDISU will check that the requirements are still met and will take any measures necessary to revoke benefits. If this isn’t the case, EDISU reserves the right to take action in relation to the failure to notify.

items.text: Art. 7(3) IBAN

classification.title: Art. 7 (3) IBAN
classification.raw: Art. 7(3) IBAN

items.text: Students must enter the SEPA IBAN via the special procedure on their personal Online Helpdesk page.

classification.raw: Students must enter the SEPA IBAN via the special procedure on their personal Online Helpdesk page.

items.text: Winners may only receive the scholarship by crediting a current account or prepaid card with an IBAN in their name or jointly in their name. Postal savings books are excluded.

classification.raw: Winners may only receive the scholarship by crediting a current account or prepaid card with an IBAN in their name or jointly in their name. Postal savings books are excluded.

items.text: To enter an IBAN ex novo or to modify the IBAN previously entered in the EDISU database, you must access your personal page of the Online Helpdesk, fill in the procedure called “Bank Details - IBAN”, and then send the data exclusively online using the “Save and Send” button.

classification.raw: To enter an IBAN ex novo or to modify the IBAN previously entered in the EDISU database, you must access your personal page of the Online Helpdesk, fill in the procedure called “Bank Details - IBAN”, and then send the data exclusively online using the “Save and Send” button.

items.text: Students enrolled at the University of Turin may give their consent to forward their IBAN to their University's Fees and Refunds Office in order to receive any refunds.

classification.raw: Students enrolled at the University of Turin may give their consent to forward their IBAN to their University's Fees and Refunds Office in order to receive any refunds.

items.text: After the IBAN has been communicated online, a confirmation is sent to the e-mail address indicated by the students containing the data relating to the declared code. This confirmation remains available on the students' personal page of the Online Helpdesk under “Ricevute” (Receipts).

classification.raw: After the IBAN has been communicated online, a confirmation is sent to the e-mail address indicated by the students containing the data relating to the declared code. This confirmation remains available on the students' personal page of the Online Helpdesk under “Ricevute” (Receipts).

items.text: The scholarship will only be paid out if the IBAN has been entered within the deadlines set out in articles 13 (first years), 18 (subsequent years) and 27 (PhDs) below.

classification.raw: The scholarship will only be paid out if the IBAN has been entered within the deadlines set out in articles 13 (first years), 18 (subsequent years) and 27 (PhDs) below.

items.text: International students, who are not allowed to open a current account under current Italian banking regulations, may enter a foreign IBAN, provided it is part of the SEPA circuit. In order to proceed with the payment, the winner must communicate an address in Italy. Until such notice, payment cannot be made.

classification.raw: International students, who are not allowed to open a current account under current Italian banking regulations, may enter a foreign IBAN, provided it is part of the SEPA circuit. In order to proceed with the payment, the winner must communicate an address in Italy. Until such notice, payment cannot be made.

items.text: With regard to payments to students from certain “high-risk third countries” identified by current European legislation, delays or non-credits may occur. In this case, EDISU shall not be held liable.

classification.raw: With regard to payments to students from certain “high-risk third countries” identified by current European legislation, delays or non-credits may occur. In this case, EDISU shall not be held liable.

items.text: It is only possible to grant the scholarship to international students who are minors if a legal guardian is appointed, with registered residence in Italy and a current account with Italian or foreign IBAN, provided it is part of the SEPA circuit. In this case, the student who is a minor must send a copy of a notarial deed by 10 December 2025 (translated and legalised in the same way as the consular documentation referred to in art. 30(3)) in which one of the parents (or both) declares the personal data of the legal guardian and authorises payment of the scholarship to their bank account, indicating the IBAN/SEPA reference code. A copy of an identity document and the legal guardian's tax code must also be attached.

classification.raw: It is only possible to grant the scholarship to international students who are minors if a legal guardian is appointed, with registered residence in Italy and a current account with Italian or foreign IBAN, provided it is part of the SEPA circuit. In this case, the student who is a minor must send a copy of a notarial deed by 10 December 2025 (translated and legalised in the same way as the consular documentation referred to in art. 30(3)) in which one of the parents (or both) declares the personal data of the legal guardian and authorises payment of the scholarship to their bank account, indicating the IBAN/SEPA reference code. A copy of an identity document and the legal guardian's tax code must also be attached.

items.text: In the absence of such a notarial deed, students who are minors must wait until they turn 18 before they can receive payment

classification.raw: In the absence of such a notarial deed, students who are minors must wait until they turn 18 before they can receive payment

items.text: The sums must be collected by 31 December 2027. If the IBAN is not communicated by that date, the benefit amount is no longer payable.

classification.raw: The sums must be collected by 31 December 2027. If the IBAN is not communicated by that date, the benefit amount is no longer payable.

items.text: Art. 7(4) TRANSFER OF APPLICATIONS SUBMITTED TO OTHER BODIES

classification.title: Art. 7 (4) TRANSFER OF APPLICATIONS SUBMITTED TO OTHER BODIES
classification.raw: Art. 7(4) TRANSFER OF APPLICATIONS SUBMITTED TO OTHER BODIES

items.text: Students who have applied to an Agency for the Right to Study in another region and are moving to a Piedmont University may transfer their application. To make such a transfer they must:

classification.raw: Students who have applied to an Agency for the Right to Study in another region and are moving to a Piedmont University may transfer their application. To make such a transfer they must:

items.text: • request that the institution in another region send the application to EDISU Piemonte, at the certified mail address; • open a ticket indicating one's personal data, tax code, telephone number, the institution where the application was submitted, as well as a front/back copy of one's ID; after receiving communication from EDISU Piemonte, submit the application through EDISU Piemonte’s online portal in the section "scholarship and accommodation application" within the deadlines indicated in this paragraph.

items: • request that the institution in another region send the application to EDISU Piemonte, at the certified mail address;

• open a ticket indicating one's personal data, tax code, telephone number, the institution where the application was submitted, as well as a front/back copy of one's ID; after receiving communication from EDISU Piemonte, submit the application through EDISU Piemonte’s online portal in the section "scholarship and accommodation application" within the deadlines indicated in this paragraph.
classification.raw: • request that the institution in another region send the application to EDISU Piemonte, at the certified mail address; • open a ticket indicating one's personal data, tax code, telephone number, the institution where the application was submitted, as well as a front/back copy of one's ID; after receiving communication from EDISU Piemonte, submit the application through EDISU Piemonte’s online portal in the section "scholarship and accommodation application" within the deadlines indicated in this paragraph.

items.text: Tranfer will be considered valid only after the transfer of the request and receipt of receipts referred to in paragraph 2 above

classification.raw: Tranfer will be considered valid only after the transfer of the request and receipt of receipts referred to in paragraph 2 above

items.text: The procedure described above must be completed by 22 September 2025, the deadline for submitting complaints for accommodation service (if you wish to transfer a scholarship and accommodation service application) or by 12 November 2025, the deadline for submitting complaints for the scholarship alone (if you wish to transfer a scholarship application only).

classification.raw: The procedure described above must be completed by 22 September 2025, the deadline for submitting complaints for accommodation service (if you wish to transfer a scholarship and accommodation service application) or by 12 November 2025, the deadline for submitting complaints for the scholarship alone (if you wish to transfer a scholarship application only).

items.text: Students who were not “non-resident” in the region where they applied for the scholarship or had not applied for the accommodation service may supplement their application by requesting the addition of the accommodation service application, if they fall under the status of “non-resident” pursuant to art. 4 and the transfer of the application takes place by 22 September 2025.

classification.raw: Students who were not “non-resident” in the region where they applied for the scholarship or had not applied for the accommodation service may supplement their application by requesting the addition of the accommodation service application, if they fall under the status of “non-resident” pursuant to art. 4 and the transfer of the application takes place by 22 September 2025.

items.text: For students who attend the "filter semester" in other regions and have submitted an application for a scholarship at that institution, there will be a re-opening of applications between 26 January and 16 February 2026 by means of the application re-opening procedure following a transfer from the Agency for the Right to Education of another region, as indicated in art. (9)(3.2) below

classification.raw: For students who attend the "filter semester" in other regions and have submitted an application for a scholarship at that institution, there will be a re-opening of applications between 26 January and 16 February 2026 by means of the application re-opening procedure following a transfer from the Agency for the Right to Education of another region, as indicated in art. (9)(3.2) below

items.text: Art. 7(5) CHANGES TO THE DATA INCLUDED IN THE APPLICATION

classification.title: Art. 7 (5) CHANGES TO THE DATA INCLUDED IN THE APPLICATION
classification.raw: Art. 7(5) CHANGES TO THE DATA INCLUDED IN THE APPLICATION

items.text: For students who apply by logging in with a SPID or CIE, the data entered can be modified until the application is transmitted online.

classification.raw: For students who apply by logging in with a SPID or CIE, the data entered can be modified until the application is transmitted online.

items.text: For international students applying with a Temporary Access Code academic year 2025/26, the online procedure can be re- launched in all its parts and the data entered can be modified until the “final save” button is pressed. Thereafter, the application will be frozen and it will no longer be possible to amend it; the data entered and the requests made may only and exclusively be corrected in the manner set out in section 5.1 below until 12:00 p.m. on the application deadline day.

classification.raw: For international students applying with a Temporary Access Code academic year 2025/26, the online procedure can be re- launched in all its parts and the data entered can be modified until the “final save” button is pressed. Thereafter, the application will be frozen and it will no longer be possible to amend it; the data entered and the requests made may only and exclusively be corrected in the manner set out in section 5.1 below until 12:00 p.m. on the application deadline day.

items.text: After the online submission of the application, the data entered can only be edited online until 12:00 p.m. on the application deadline day, for the following cases and in the following ways:

classification.raw: After the online submission of the application, the data entered can only be edited online until 12:00 p.m. on the application deadline day, for the following cases and in the following ways:

items.text: 1. Via the “DATA MODIFICATION” button, available by accessing the application for benefits from your personal Online Helpdesk page.

classification.title: Via the “DATA MODIFICATION” button, available by accessing the application for benefits from your personal Online Helpdesk page.
classification.raw: 1. Via the “DATA MODIFICATION” button, available by accessing the application for benefits from your personal Online Helpdesk page.

items.text: a. It is possible to edit data separately for each of the following types: “Residence/domicile”, “Disability Data”, “International Protection” (only for Non-EU), “Scholastic data”, “Merit”, “Second Bachelor’s Degree Course”, “Economic Data”, “Attached Documents” (only for Temporary Access Code 2025/26). When opening each individual type, the data declared in the application are displayed in read-only mode. You only have to enter the data you wish to edit and not those you wish to confirm. Only one edit request per type can be submitted. b. Each edit form must be individually completed and submitted online by 12:00 p.m. on the application submission deadline day.

items: a. It is possible to edit data separately for each of the following types: “Residence/domicile”, “Disability Data”, “International Protection” (only for Non-EU), “Scholastic data”, “Merit”, “Second Bachelor’s Degree Course”, “Economic Data”, “Attached Documents” (only for Temporary Access Code 2025/26). When opening each individual type, the data declared in the application are displayed in read-only mode. You only have to enter the data you wish to edit and not those you wish to confirm. Only one edit request per type can be submitted. b. Each edit form must be individually completed and submitted online by 12:00 p.m. on the application submission deadline day.
classification.raw: a. It is possible to edit data separately for each of the following types: “Residence/domicile”, “Disability Data”, “International Protection” (only for Non-EU), “Scholastic data”, “Merit”, “Second Bachelor’s Degree Course”, “Economic Data”, “Attached Documents” (only for Temporary Access Code 2025/26). When opening each individual type, the data declared in the application are displayed in read-only mode. You only have to enter the data you wish to edit and not those you wish to confirm. Only one edit request per type can be submitted. b. Each edit form must be individually completed and submitted online by 12:00 p.m. on the application submission deadline day.

items.text: 2. Non-resident students who have applied for a scholarship only may add a request for accommodation service using the special “Add accommodation service application request” button, available by accessing the benefits application from their personal Online Helpdesk page. Similarly, students who have applied for a scholarship and accommodation, but only intended to apply for the scholarship may delete the request for accommodation service using the special “Delete the accommodation service application” button, available by accessing the benefits application from their personal Online Helpdesk page.

classification.title: Non-resident students who have applied for a scholarship only may add a request for accommodation service using the special “Add accommodation service application request” button, available by accessing the benefits application from their personal Online Helpdesk page. Similarly, students who have applied for a scholarship and accommodation, but only intended to apply for the scholarship may delete the request for accommodation service using the special “Delete the accommodation service application” button, available by accessing the benefits application from their personal Online Helpdesk page.
classification.raw: 2. Non-resident students who have applied for a scholarship only may add a request for accommodation service using the special “Add accommodation service application request” button, available by accessing the benefits application from their personal Online Helpdesk page. Similarly, students who have applied for a scholarship and accommodation, but only intended to apply for the scholarship may delete the request for accommodation service using the special “Delete the accommodation service application” button, available by accessing the benefits application from their personal Online Helpdesk page.

items.text: Students who are applying for the following year for the additional semester of a Bachelor's degree but intend to enrol in a Master's degree course in the academic year 2025/26, may apply to amend the application to a first-year master’s degree one, if they meet the requirements, by editing the “Scholastic data”.

classification.raw: Students who are applying for the following year for the additional semester of a Bachelor's degree but intend to enrol in a Master's degree course in the academic year 2025/26, may apply to amend the application to a first-year master’s degree one, if they meet the requirements, by editing the “Scholastic data”.

items.text: Students enrolled in a degree course during the academic year 2022/23 who apply for the first year of a Master's degree but do not enrol, may amend their application to a subsequent-year application for the additional semester of their degree, if they have not graduated by September 2025 and if they meet the merit requirements, by editing the “Scholastic data”.

classification.raw: Students enrolled in a degree course during the academic year 2022/23 who apply for the first year of a Master's degree but do not enrol, may amend their application to a subsequent-year application for the additional semester of their degree, if they have not graduated by September 2025 and if they meet the merit requirements, by editing the “Scholastic data”.

items.text: For students with disabilities, this provision applies whether they enrolled in the academic year 2022/23 or in the academic year 2021/22.

classification.raw: For students with disabilities, this provision applies whether they enrolled in the academic year 2022/23 or in the academic year 2021/22.

items.text: Only one data modification can be sent per type. Students are invited to check the information entered before submitting the correction.

classification.raw: Only one data modification can be sent per type. Students are invited to check the information entered before submitting the correction.

items.text: Requests to edit data in a manner that differs even partially from those indicated in this article will not be processed, and in any case no later than the deadline for submitting the application.

classification.raw: Requests to edit data in a manner that differs even partially from those indicated in this article will not be processed, and in any case no later than the deadline for submitting the application.

items.text: Following online transmission, a confirmation is sent to the e-mail address indicated by the students with the subject relating to the transaction carried out (edit ^ + edit type, addition/deletion of accommodation), with the relevant PDF attached.

classification.raw: Following online transmission, a confirmation is sent to the e-mail address indicated by the students with the subject relating to the transaction carried out (edit ^ + edit type, addition/deletion of accommodation), with the relevant PDF attached.

items.text: The transaction is deemed to have been correctly transmitted if and only if this confirmation is delivered to the e-mail address the student indicates and appears in the “Ricevute” (receipts) section of their personal Online Helpdesk page.

classification.raw: The transaction is deemed to have been correctly transmitted if and only if this confirmation is delivered to the e-mail address the student indicates and appears in the “Ricevute” (receipts) section of their personal Online Helpdesk page.

items.text: Sending the confirmation does not imply acceptance of the requested change: the outcome of the request will be available with the publication of the provisional ranking for the benefit requested.

classification.raw: Sending the confirmation does not imply acceptance of the requested change: the outcome of the request will be available with the publication of the provisional ranking for the benefit requested.

items.text: Please note that the “economic data” section of the online “data modification” procedure will be active after the transmission of the “INPS Data” receipt.

classification.raw: Please note that the “economic data” section of the online “data modification” procedure will be active after the transmission of the “INPS Data” receipt.

items.text: The economic data submitted by means of the data modification procedure must refer to a ISEE Universitario certificate present in the INPS database issued even prior to the date of online retrieval, or through the submission of a new 2025 ISEE that corrects the grounds for which the EDISU did not accept it. Data edits indicating only the DSU protocol number in the absence of a processed ISEE will not be considered valid.

classification.raw: The economic data submitted by means of the data modification procedure must refer to a ISEE Universitario certificate present in the INPS database issued even prior to the date of online retrieval, or through the submission of a new 2025 ISEE that corrects the grounds for which the EDISU did not accept it. items.text: Students whose confirmation with the subject “Ricevuta dati INPS” (INPS data receipt) has the wording “In the INPS data bank there is an ISEE Certificate not valid for the right to university study for the applicant benefits” must enter the ISEE data relating to the new and corrected ISEE Universitario Certificate in the Data modification procedure by 12:00 p.m., the deadline for submitting the application.

classification.raw: Students whose confirmation with the subject “Ricevuta dati INPS” (INPS data receipt) has the wording “In the INPS data bank there is an ISEE Certificate not valid for the right to university study for the applicant benefits” must enter the ISEE data relating to the new and corrected ISEE Universitario Certificate in the Data modification procedure by 12:00 p.m., the deadline for submitting the application.

items.text: Students whose confirmation with the subject “Ricevuta dati INPS” (INPS data receipt) has the wording “ISEE with observations on omissions and/or discrepancies” must send the documentation proving the correctness of the contents of the DSU, as indicated in the INPS and Ministry of Labour message no. 8415 of 26/10/2020. It is also possible to submit a new ISEE Universitario Certificate that rectifies the omissions/ discrepancies found in the previous one.

items.text: After submitting the online data modification to replace the ISEE data, EDISU Piemonte does not send an additional INPS Data Receipt; therefore, the ISEE Universitario values that the student will display in the provisional rankings will be those self-certified in the modification procedure.

items.text: With regard to applicants for PhDs and specialisation schools, please refer to art. 25(2).

classification.raw: With regard to applicants for PhDs and specialisation schools, please refer to art. 25(2).

classification.raw: CHANGES TO DATA AND REQUESTS AFTER SUBMISSION BUT BEFORE THE APPLICATION SUBMISSION DEADLINE

items.text: After the application submission deadline, the data entered can only be corrected online by submitting a complaint after the publication of the provisional ranking in accordance with the procedures set out in the following paragraph.

items.text: Art. 7(6) COMPLAINTS AGAINST PROVISIONAL RANKINGS

classification.title: Art. 7 (6) COMPLAINTS AGAINST PROVISIONAL RANKINGS
classification.raw: Art. 7(6) COMPLAINTS AGAINST PROVISIONAL RANKINGS

items.text: Complaints may be made by both successful and unsuccessful students in the provisional ranking. Excluded students must take all grounds for exclusion into account. Only applications for the correction of material errors, relating to data found in documents dated before the application deadline, will be accepted. It is not possible to add the request referred to in paragraph 5.1, point 2 above, to a complaint.

classification.raw: Complaints may be made by both successful and unsuccessful students in the provisional ranking. Excluded students must take all grounds for exclusion into account. Only applications for the correction of material errors, relating to data found in documents dated before the application deadline, will be accepted. It is not possible to add the request referred to in paragraph 5.1, point 2 above, to a complaint.

items.text: The complaint procedure is available by consulting the result of the provisional ranking for the benefit applied for, by selecting the “complaint” item.

classification.raw: The complaint procedure is available by consulting the result of the provisional ranking for the benefit applied for, by selecting the “complaint” item.

items.text: 1. Separate complaints may be submitted for each of the following types: “Enrolment”, “Scholastic data”, “Merit Self-certification”, “Validations”, “Economic data”, “Pending financial obligations with EDISU” and “Other reasons” and “Second Bachelor’s Degree Course” (available only for those who have already applied for the increase by the application submission deadline). When opening each individual complaint, the data present in the provisional ranking are displayed in read-only mode. You only have to enter the data you want to edit and not the data you want to confirm. Only one complaint can be sent per type. If necessary, documents can be attached to support the complaint through the procedure, if any. You cannot rectify your exclusion with a claim based on the reason "no equal calculation" 2. Each complaint form must be individually completed and submitted online by 12:00 p.m. on the complaint submission deadline day.

items.text: Complaints against exclusions from the provisional rankings for economic reasons are compulsory in order to rectify one's position for the definitive rankings, since EDISU Piemonte will not proceed with an additional online retrieval through the INPS database before the publication of the definitive rankings.

classification.raw: Complaints against exclusions from the provisional rankings for economic reasons are compulsory in order to rectify one's position for the definitive rankings, since EDISU Piemonte will not proceed with an additional online retrieval through the INPS database before the publication of the definitive rankings.

items.text: The economic data submitted by means of the complaint procedure must refer to an ISEE certificate present in the INPS database issued even prior to the date of online retrieval, or through the submission of a new 2025 ISEE that corrects the grounds for which the EDISU did not accept it. Complaints indicating only the DSU protocol number in the absence of a processed ISEE will not be considered valid.

items.text: Below are the possible reasons for exclusion from the provisional ranking linked to economic reasons and indications on the possibility and method of lodging a complaint:

classification.raw: Below are the possible reasons for exclusion from the provisional ranking linked to economic reasons and indications on the possibility and method of lodging a complaint:

items.text: • the data of the ISEE certificate or the Single Self-Declaration cannot be found in the INPS database: in this case, the data can only be rectified with a complaint if the applicant has a valid DSU for the right to university study and without observations on omissions/discrepancies signed by the deadline for the submission of the application for the benefit to be applied for; • the ISEE certificate is not valid for the right to University study for the benefit applicant: in this case, the data can only be rectified with a complaint if the applicant has a valid ISEE certificate for the right to university study, processed by INPS within the deadline for complaints relating to the benefit requested; • the ISEE certificate contains observations on omissions or discrepancies: in this case, the data can only be rectified with a complaint if the applicant has a valid ISEE certificate for the right to university study without observations on omissions/discrepancies and processed by INPS within the deadline for complaints relating to the benefit requested.

items.text: Art. 7(7) WAIVER OF BENEFITS 2025/26

classification.title: Art. 7 (7) WAIVER OF BENEFITS 2025/26
classification.raw: Art. 7(7) WAIVER OF BENEFITS 2025/26

items.text: As of 10 September 2025, students who wish to waive a benefit must use the special “Waiver” button, available by accessing the benefit application on their personal EDISU Online Helpdesk page in “the Previous years Beneficts”. This procedure will only be activated when the application has been uploaded to the EDISU database and students receive the e-mail with the subject “Ricezione pratica” (reception of the application) (paragraphs 1.1 and 2.1 of this article). Students who have applied for both the grant and the accommodation service may renounce the accommodation service alone but cannot renounce the grant alone, since renouncing the latter necessarily entails renouncing the accommodation service.

classification.raw: As of 10 September 2025, students who wish to waive a benefit must use the special “Waiver” button, available by accessing the benefit application on their personal EDISU Online Helpdesk page in “the Previous years Beneficts”. This procedure will only be activated when the application has been uploaded to the EDISU database and students receive the e-mail with the subject “Ricezione pratica” (reception of the application) (paragraphs 1.1 and 2.1 of this article). Students who have applied for both the grant and the accommodation service may renounce the accommodation service alone but cannot renounce the grant alone, since renouncing the latter necessarily entails renouncing the accommodation service.

items.text: In the event that students are already allocated a place in EDISU residences, they must submit their renunciation directly to their University Residence reception and must fill in the online renunciation form by selecting the reason why they wish to renounce the benefit from the list within the procedure. Once completed, they must submit the request online by clicking the Send button. Students will be required to pay for the period of use in accordance with art. 35(1).

classification.raw: In the event that students are already allocated a place in EDISU residences, they must submit their renunciation directly to their University Residence reception and must fill in the online renunciation form by selecting the reason why they wish to renounce the benefit from the list within the procedure. items.text: Following the online transmission of the renunciation, a confirmation is sent to the e-mail address the students indicated with the subject line “Acquisizione dati web, rinuncia benefici 2025/26” (acquisition of web data, benefits withdrawal 2024/25). The PDF on the renunciation is available in the annex. If the student has applied for a scholarship and accommodation service, the waiver will be a "waiver of scholarship and accommodation service"; if the student has applied for a scholarship only the waiver will be a "waiver of scholarship".

items.text: Art. 8(1) NON-RESIDENT STUDENTS WHO MUST SUBMIT THE SELF-CERTIFICATION OF PAID ACCOMMODATION

classification.title: Art. 8 (1) NON-RESIDENT STUDENTS WHO MUST SUBMIT THE SELF-CERTIFICATION OF PAID ACCOMMODATION
classification.raw: Art. 8(1) NON-RESIDENT STUDENTS WHO MUST SUBMIT THE SELF-CERTIFICATION OF PAID ACCOMMODATION

items.text: Non-resident students who are not guests of EDISU residences and who belong to the categories listed in art. 5(1.3), are required to pay for accommodation, at the university location they attend (or in a neighbouring municipality), using public residential facilities or accommodation belonging to private parties or institutions for at least 10 months of the academic year 2025/26 altogether. Students must also complete the special online self-certification in accordance with the procedures and time frames set out in paragraph 2 of this article, in order to receive the non-resident amount due to them, only and exclusively if they are successful in the final scholarship rankings. Otherwise, they will be paid the amount provided for commuter students (art. 5(1.2)).

classification.raw: Non-resident students who are not guests of EDISU residences and who belong to the categories listed in art. 5(1.3), are required to pay for accommodation, at the university location they attend (or in a neighbouring municipality), using public residential facilities or accommodation belonging to private parties or institutions for at least 10 months of the academic year 2025/26 altogether. Students must also complete the special online self-certification in accordance with the procedures and time frames set out in paragraph 2 of this article, in order to receive the non-resident amount due to them, only and exclusively if they are successful in the final scholarship rankings. Otherwise, they will be paid the amount provided for commuter students (art. 5(1.2)).

Foreign contract (foreign study/exchange): students must enter the details of the rental/hospitality contract and attach the contract as a .pdf file. The contract must clearly state the name of the student, the exact duration of the contract and the rental fee to be paid. For foreign/exchange students who are guests of EDISU residences, see also art. 33(4.1). Students staying at EDISU residences who have “Erasmus Option” status and stay abroad for less than 8 months do not have to submit a self- certification of paid residence.

items.text: Accommodation declarations made in previous academic years are not valid for the purposes of the accommodation declaration for the academic year 2025/26, even if the previously declared contract is still in force.

classification.raw: Accommodation declarations made in previous academic years are not valid for the purposes of the accommodation declaration for the academic year 2025/26, even if the previously declared contract is still in force.

items.text: Students who have submitted a declaration of paid accommodation, declaring a contract that does not cover the 10 months (including any contracts involving tacit renewals), will compete for the allocation of the commuter amount only.In any case, if funds are available, the “non-resident” amount may be supplemented on the second instalment if the paid accommodation supplementary declaration is sent, complete with the registration of the new contract or renewal of the previous one, in the period between 20 March and 12 noon on 10 April 2026.

classification.raw: Students who have submitted a declaration of paid accommodation, declaring a contract that does not cover the 10 months (including any contracts involving tacit renewals), will compete for the allocation of the commuter amount only.In any case, if funds are available, the “non-resident” amount may be supplemented on the second instalment if the paid accommodation supplementary declaration is sent, complete with the registration of the new contract or renewal of the previous one, in the period between 20 March and 12 noon on 10 April 2026.

items.text: Contract supplements submitted after this date will not be accepted, not even by ticket.

classification.raw: Contract supplements submitted after this date will not be accepted, not even by ticket.

items.text: The self-certification must be filled in and sent exclusively online by 12:00 p.m. on the deadline day, using the “Save and Send” button.

classification.raw: The self-certification must be filled in and sent exclusively online by 12:00 p.m. on the deadline day, using the “Save and Send” button.

items.text: Following the transmission of the self-certification, a confirmation is sent to the e-mail address the students indicated with the subject line “Autocertificazione di alloggio a titolo oneroso” (Self-certification of paid accommodation). The self-certification is deemed to have been correctly sent if and only if this confirmation is received, which will remain available on the students' personal Online Helpdesk page, in the “Ricevute” (receipts) section.

classification.raw: Following the transmission of the self-certification, a confirmation is sent to the e-mail address the students indicated with the subject line “Autocertificazione di alloggio a titolo oneroso” (Self-certification of paid accommodation). The self-certification is deemed to have been correctly sent if and only if this confirmation is received, which will remain available on the students' personal Online Helpdesk page, in the “Ricevute” (receipts) section.

items.text: If there is a change in accommodation after the declaration has been submitted, the applicant, if a scholarship winner with a "non- resident" amount, must submit the details of the new contract. This is done through a specific online procedure: "aggiornamento contratto di locazione" relating to the contract change and the receipt of registration of the new contract must be attached to guarantee coverage of the minimum period of 10 months. This procedure will be oen fron 15 January to on 15 June 2026 at 12 noon.

classification.raw: If there is a change in accommodation after the declaration has been submitted, the applicant, if a scholarship winner with a "non- resident" amount, must submit the details of the new contract. This is done through a specific online procedure: "aggiornamento contratto di locazione" relating to the contract change and the receipt of registration of the new contract must be attached to guarantee coverage of the minimum period of 10 months. This procedure will be oen fron 15 January to on 15 June 2026 at 12 noon.

items.text: If, during the checks, it emerges that the declared contract has been terminated without notification of a new contract or without renewal of the same (even in the case of tacit renewal), the scholarship amount will be reclassified as the amount provided for the “commuter” status. Amendment registrations made after the irregularity has been reported by EDISU will not be accepted.

classification.raw: If, during the checks, it emerges that the declared contract has been terminated without notification of a new contract or without renewal of the same (even in the case of tacit renewal), the scholarship amount will be reclassified as the amount provided for the “commuter” status. Amendment registrations made after the irregularity has been reported by EDISU will not be accepted.

items.text: The scholarship amount is not dependent on the type of contract or the monthly rent. Therefore, the students will receive, if successful in the final scholarship rankings, the amount they are entitled to under art. 5(1.3).

classification.raw: The scholarship amount is not dependent on the type of contract or the monthly rent. Therefore, the students will receive, if successful in the final scholarship rankings, the amount they are entitled to under art. 5(1.3).

items.text: EDISU will carry out checks with the Revenue Agency to verify the actual registration of rental contracts, with the host structures in the case of a different payment basis, and spot checks on at least 30 \% of the declarations to ascertain the actual payment of 10 months' rent or fees. If the contract or the other payment basis is not checked, payment of the second instalment will be suspended until the check has been completed and, if necessary, the amount of the scholarship will be reduced to the “commuter” amount.

classification.raw: EDISU will carry out checks with the Revenue Agency to verify the actual registration of rental contracts, with the host structures in the case of a different payment basis, and spot checks on at least 30 \% of the declarations to ascertain the actual payment of 10 months' rent or fees. If the contract or the other payment basis is not checked, payment of the second instalment will be suspended until the check has been completed and, if necessary, the amount of the scholarship will be reduced to the “commuter” amount.

items.text: EDISU may also require that regular payment receipts, which must be in the student's name, be submitted. Further documentation in support of the declared status may be requested: if it is not submitted by 15 June 2026, payment of the second instalment of the scholarship will be suspended.

classification.raw: EDISU may also require that regular payment receipts, which must be in the student's name, be submitted. Further documentation in support of the declared status may be requested: if it is not submitted by 15 June 2026, payment of the second instalment of the scholarship will be suspended.

items.text: In addition, if checks show that the declared accommodation is not located in the municipality where the course is held or in a neighbouring municipality, the scholarship will also be reclassified as the “commuter” amount. (The list of municipalities bordering the course venues is available at www.edisu.piemonte.it).

classification.raw: In addition, if checks show that the declared accommodation is not located in the municipality where the course is held or in a neighbouring municipality, the scholarship will also be reclassified as the “commuter” amount. items.text: Students enrolled in a bachelor's, single-cycle master’s degree, or master’s degree course are required to take paid accommodation for 10 months, included in the period from 1 September 2025 to 30 September 2026 (or 5 months if students are applying for an additional semester, included in the period from 1 September 2025 to 31 March 2026) and to complete the appropriate self-certification online, in accordance with the procedures and time frames set out in this paragraph.

items.text: Students enrolled in a PhD/specialisation school are required to have paid accommodation for 10 months, included in the period from 1 October 2025 to 31 March 2027, and to complete an online self-certification, in accordance with the procedures and time frames set out in this paragraph.

classification.raw: Students who do NOT attend the "filter semester" in Piedmont, but who continue their studies by enrolling in the second semester at a Piedmont university as non-resident students, pursuant to art. 4 of this notice, and students who attend the "filter semester" in Piedmont, but who continue their studies by enrolling in a different Piedmont university, moving from resident/commuter to non- resident students, must have and declare at least three months of paid accommodation in the academic year 2025/26 to be considered non-resident.

items.text: This section is aimed at students enrolling in the academic year 2025/26, in a first year of a bachelor's degree, master's degree or single-cycle master's degree, first and second Level academic diploma (AFAM).

classification.raw: This section is aimed at students enrolling in the academic year 2025/26, in a first year of a bachelor's degree, master's degree or single-cycle master's degree, first and second Level academic diploma (AFAM).

items.text: Students who have transferred or are transferring from another University, including from outside Piedmont, may not apply as a first-year but must apply as a subsequent-year student (art. 15(1.3))

classification.raw: Students who have transferred or are transferring from another University, including from outside Piedmont, may not apply as a first-year but must apply as a subsequent-year student (art. 15(1.3))

items.text: In the case of part-time enrolment, you may only submit a standard application and may NOT benefit from the Scholarship for the additional semester.

classification.raw: In the case of part-time enrolment, you may only submit a standard application and may NOT benefit from the Scholarship for the additional semester.

items.text: Students must submit their applications within the deadlines specified in art. 10(2) even if they were not yet duly enrolled at the time of submitting the application. Should students enrol in a course other than the one indicated in their application, they must promptly notify EDISU of the change in the manner indicated in art. 7(5) and (6).

items.text:
COURSE TYPE ADMISSION REQUIREMENTS
Bachelor's degree and single-cycle master's degree courses Standard Application1. High school diploma, regardless of grade. 2. Enrolment in the course within the deadlines set by your University, in any case no later than 31 October 2025 (inclusion in definitive rankings of 7 November 2025) or 30 November 2025 (inclusion in rankings of 16 December 2025). Students not yet enrolled by 30 November 2025 will be “suspended” (if they have no other reasons for exclusion) and will have to be enrolled on 3 April 2026 in order to be included in the definitive rankings of 30 April 2026. International students not residing in Italy must refer to articles 31 (1.1) and (1.2) (for non-EU students) and 31(2) (for EU students) Students attending the “filter semester” of medicina e chirurgia, medicina veterinaria e odontoiatria e protesi dentaria courses, or related courses49, must follow the instructions in paragraph 3 below
Master's degree coursesStandard Application Enrolment in the master's degree course by a) 30 November 2025 to be included in the definitive rankings of 16 December or b) 3 April 2026 to be included in the definitive rankings of 30 April pursuant to art. 11(2) Confirmation of Merit Benefits 1. Winning the scholarship in the academic year 2024/25 at EDISU Piemonte. 2. Degree enrolment in the academic year 2022/23 and graduation by the extraordinary session of the academic year 2024/25. 3. Enrolment in the master's degree course by: a) 30 November 2025 to be included in the definitive rankings of 16 December or b) 3 April 2026 to be included in the definitive rankings of 30 April pursuant to art. 11(2) For confirmation of the accommodation service, students must have been allocated a place, even with an entry extension or be on the single sliding list for the academic year 2024/25. as of 30 May 2025, and have received the 2024/25 scholarship with a commuting amount due to the non-submission of the paid residence. Otherwise, they can only confirm the scholarship on merit (art. 16(1)), while they are included in the accommodation service rankings as first-year Standard Application (art. 12(1)).

items.text: If they apply for a scholarship after withdrawing from their studies or losing their student status and they have benefited from the scholarship or grant and accommodation service for one or more academic years during their previous course enrolment, by enrolling in a new course, they will be prevented from applying for benefits from EDISU for the same number of years as they benefited from these benefits during their previous enrolment. This is true even if they have withdrawn from a bachelor’s degree or master's degree course and have subsequently enrolled in a single-cycle master's degree course, and vice versa. The only exception is if they return the scholarship received by 12 November 2025, the deadline for submitting complaints.

items.text: Students already enrolled in the Italian university system in previous academic years who change course within the same University or transfer from another University, even if not in Piedmont, may NOT submit an application as a first-year student but must apply as a subsequent-year student, regardless of the course year in which they are enrolled by the university or AFAM

classification.raw: Students already enrolled in the Italian university system in previous academic years who change course within the same University or transfer from another University, even if not in Piedmont, may NOT submit an application as a first-year student but must apply as a subsequent-year student, regardless of the course year in which they are enrolled by the university or AFAM

items.text: A change of course within the same University is not equivalent to a career break. Therefore, following a switch, the year of first enrolment will be considered to be that of the previous course and an application cannot be submitted as a first-year student. Such students must refer to art. 15(4) of the Subsequent-Years Section.

items.text: Students who have applied for the foreign career shortening (point a of this subsection) are in any case required to meet the merit requirements indicated in subsections 5 (if successful) and 6 (if eligible) of article 13 below, otherwise any benefits they may have received will be revoked. items.text: Students of courses pertaining to the 5- or 6-year postgraduate degree in medicina e chirurgia, e courses (LM-41), the 5- or 6-year postgraduate degree in Odontoiatria e protesi dentaria (LM-46) or the 5- or 6-year postgraduate degree in medicina veterinaria (LM-42) (henceforth, Classes LM-41, LM-46 and LM-42) must submit a scholarship application to the reference Agency for the Right to Education for the university where they are attending the “filter semester”.

items.text: Please note: students attending one of the courses in Classes LM-41, LM-46 or LM-42 are advised to carefully check the specific provisions laid down by each reference Agency for the Right to Education for the location where they are attending the filter semester and where they intend to continue their studies.

items.text: 9(3.1) STUDENTS ATTENDING THE "FILTER SEMESTER" IN PIEDMONT

items.text: Students attending the “filter semester” for one of the courses belonging to the Classes LM-41 or LM-46 or LM-42 at a Piedmont University, must submit the scholarship or scholarship and accommodation service (if a non-resident student) application to EDISU Piemonte by 9 September 2025 at 12 noon, according to the methods indicated in art. 10 below.

classification.raw: Students attending the “filter semester” for one of the courses belonging to the Classes LM-41 or LM-46 or LM-42 at a Piedmont University, must submit the scholarship or scholarship and accommodation service (if a non-resident student) application to EDISU Piemonte by 9 September 2025 at 12 noon, according to the methods indicated in art. 10 below.

items.text: The application must be completed exclusively by indicating the details of one of the courses, from those belonging to Classes LM- 41 or LM-46 or LM-42, for which you intend to attend the “filter semester”.

classification.raw: The application must be completed exclusively by indicating the details of one of the courses, from those belonging to Classes LM- 41 or LM-46 or LM-42, for which you intend to attend the “filter semester”.

items.text: Indicating a "related course" is not permitted, not even for the purpose of claiming the 20 \% increase for the second course.

classification.raw: Indicating a "related course" is not permitted, not even for the purpose of claiming the 20 \% increase for the second course.

items.text: These students will appear with the outcome “suspended for filter semester enrolment” in the final December scholarship rankings. Please refer to art. 12 for provisions on the publication of rankings and the submission of any claims.

items.text: Students who, though not attending the filter semester at a Piedmont university, indicate it as one of the ten locations where they intend to continue their studies, in accordance with art. 2 of Ministerial Decree no. 418 of 30 May 2025, may also apply to EDISU Piemonte. In this case, the application must include the details of the course, from those belonging to Classes LM-41 or LM-46 or LM-42, offered by the Piedmont University.

classification.raw: Students who, though not attending the filter semester at a Piedmont university, indicate it as one of the ten locations where they intend to continue their studies, in accordance with art. 2 of Ministerial Decree no. 418 of 30 May 2025, may also apply to EDISU Piemonte. In this case, the application must include the details of the course, from those belonging to Classes LM-41 or LM-46 or LM-42, offered by the Piedmont University.

items.text: Students who do NOT submit an application to EDISU Piemonte by 9 September 2025 will only be able to access an extraordinary application procedure. This is provided that they have submitted, by the relevant deadlines, an application for a scholarship to the competent Agency for the Right to Education for the university where they are attending the "filter semester". This procedure will be reserved for those who intend to continue their studies at a Piedmont university and will be active from 26 January to 16 February 2026 at 12 noon, in accordance with the procedures to be defined in a special notice published in the days preceding the opening of the procedure.

items.text: Applications can be submitted from 25 July 2025. The application submission methods are set out in art. 7(1.1) (for students with a SPID or CIE) and (1.2) (for international students without residence in Italy with a Temporary Access Code). International students with a household resident abroad must follow the application submission methods outlined in arts. 30 and 31.

classification.raw: Applications can be submitted from 25 July 2025. The application submission methods are set out in art. 7(1.1) (for students with a SPID or CIE) and (1.2) (for international students without residence in Italy with a Temporary Access Code). International students with a household resident abroad must follow the application submission methods outlined in arts. 30 and 31.

items.text: Students who are minors on the application deadline date must have their parents/guardians complete the scholarship application authorisation form, available on the EDISU website in the Documentazione > Modulistica> Borse e altri contributi (Documentation > Forms > Grants and other contributions) section, which must be attached to the application in the upload relating to the identity document, together with the parents' identity document.

items.text: Any event occurring after the date on which the application is submitted (award of another scholarship or other financial aid, transfer to another university, withdrawal from studies, change of commitment from part-time to full-time and vice versa, inability to continue studies, withdrawal from enrolment, change of department or course of study, change of course venue, etc.) must be promptly communicated to the EDISU offices and documented by opening a ticket. EDISU will check that the requirements are still met and will take any measures necessary to revoke benefits. If this is not the case, EDISU reserves the right to take action in relation to the failure to notify. In the event of transfer to another University in Piedmont, students will be placed at the bottom of the ranking of the new University and course level

items.text: If students enrolled in a degree course during the academic year 2022/23 apply for the first year of a master’s degree but do not enrol, they may inform EDISU by 12 November 2025 by means of a special complaint procedure, indicating the data for the bachelor's degree course. The application will be converted into an application for subsequent years for the additional semester of a bachelor's degree, if they have not graduated by September 2025 and if they meet the merit requirements.

classification.raw: If students enrolled in a degree course during the academic year 2022/23 apply for the first year of a master’s degree but do not enrol, they may inform EDISU by 12 November 2025 by means of a special complaint procedure, indicating the data for the bachelor's degree course. The application will be converted into an application for subsequent years for the additional semester of a bachelor's degree, if they have not graduated by September 2025 and if they meet the merit requirements.

items.text: Students who apply for a first-level degree career with enrolment prior to 2022/23 but who, following checks with the University indicated in their application, are found to be enrolled in the first year of a Master's degree course, will be included in the rankings for that career.

classification.raw: Students who apply for a first-level degree career with enrolment prior to 2022/23 but who, following checks with the University indicated in their application, are found to be enrolled in the first year of a Master's degree course, will be included in the rankings for that career.

items.text: If students apply for the subsequent year for the additional semester of a bachelor’s degree but enrol in a master's degree course by 30 November 2025, they may inform EDISU by 12:00 p.m. on 12 November via a special complaint procedure, indicating the details of the master's degree course; the application will be converted into a first-year, master’s degree application, if they meet the requirements.

classification.raw: If students apply for the subsequent year for the additional semester of a bachelor’s degree but enrol in a master's degree course by 30 November 2025, they may inform EDISU by 12:00 p.m. on 12 November via a special complaint procedure, indicating the details of the master's degree course; the application will be converted into a first-year, master’s degree application, if they meet the requirements.

items.text: If students applying for the first year of the Master's degree are not enrolled in the Master's degree course by 30 November 2025, they will have the outcome “suspended for non-enrolment” in the final Scholarship rankings of 16 December 2025 and will have to enrol by 3 April 2026.

classification.raw: If students applying for the first year of the Master's degree are not enrolled in the Master's degree course by 30 November 2025, they will have the outcome “suspended for non-enrolment” in the final Scholarship rankings of 16 December 2025 and will have to enrol by 3 April 2026.

items.text: These students will be included in the provisional rankings of 20 March 2026 and may lodge a complaint by 12:00 p.m. on 3 April 2026. They will then be included in the final Scholarship rankings on 30 April 2026 (12:00 p.m.).

classification.raw: These students will be included in the provisional rankings of 20 March 2026 and may lodge a complaint by 12:00 p.m. on 3 April 2026. They will then be included in the final Scholarship rankings on 30 April 2026 (12:00 p.m.).

items.text: Successful 2024/25 scholarship-winning students in their final course year will have priority in these rankings.

classification.raw: Successful 2024/25 scholarship-winning students in their final course year will have priority in these rankings.

items.text: If they do not enrol and meet the merit requirements for the additional semester grant, they will have to file a complaint and the application will be converted back to an additional semester application if they have not graduated by September 2025 and meet the merit requirements.

classification.raw: If they do not enrol and meet the merit requirements for the additional semester grant, they will have to file a complaint and the application will be converted back to an additional semester application if they have not graduated by September 2025 and meet the merit requirements.

items.text: Students included in the rankings of 30 April 2026 must achieve 20 credits by 10 August 2026 to be eligible for the second 2025/26 grant instalment, including the integration for the second course.

classification.raw: Students included in the rankings of 30 April 2026 must achieve 20 credits by 10 August 2026 to be eligible for the second 2025/26 grant instalment, including the integration for the second course.

items.text: In order to apply for the scholarship for the 2026/27 academic year, they must achieve the required credits for the scholarship application by 10 August 2026.

classification.raw: In order to apply for the scholarship for the 2026/27 academic year, they must achieve the required credits for the scholarship application by 10 August 2026.

items.text: If these students also apply for the accommodation service:

classification.raw: If these students also apply for the accommodation service:

items.text: ART. 12 RANKINGS AND COMPLAINTS

classification.title: Art. 12 RANKINGS AND COMPLAINTS
classification.raw: ART. 12 RANKINGS AND COMPLAINTS

items.text: Interested students will make sure to view the rankings in the manner indicated below as no further communications will be sent. The rankings will be published on your personal Online Helpdesk page, “Scholarship” section, by clicking on “Ranking”. Students who have applied for both the accommodation service and the grant must consult two separate rankings: the first for the accommodation service, the second for the scholarship.

classification.raw: Interested students will make sure to view the rankings in the manner indicated below as no further communications will be sent. The rankings will be published on your personal Online Helpdesk page, “Scholarship” section, by clicking on “Ranking”. Students who have applied for both the accommodation service and the grant must consult two separate rankings: the first for the accommodation service, the second for the scholarship.

items.text: For each benefit applied for, first a provisional ranking and then a definitive ranking will be published.

classification.raw: For each benefit applied for, first a provisional ranking and then a definitive ranking will be published.

items.text: After the deadline for the submission of grant applications, before the publication of the provisional grant ranking and in the period between the publication of the provisional and final grant rankings, checks will be carried out with the Universities in order to ascertain the regular enrolment of students and any merit requirements. The provisional and final scholarship rankings will incorporate the results of these assessments.

classification.raw: After the deadline for the submission of grant applications, before the publication of the provisional grant ranking and in the period between the publication of the provisional and final grant rankings, checks will be carried out with the Universities in order to ascertain the regular enrolment of students and any merit requirements. The provisional and final scholarship rankings will incorporate the results of these assessments.

items.text: The accommodation service rankings will instead be drawn up on the basis of self-certified data from students.

classification.raw: The accommodation service rankings will instead be drawn up on the basis of self-certified data from students.

items.text: At the request of students, checks may also be carried out when the application is being completed in particular situations.

classification.raw: At the request of students, checks may also be carried out when the application is being completed in particular situations.

items.text: No individual communication will be sent regarding the outcome of the competitions. The rankings can only be consulted on your personal Online Helpdesk page (by clicking on “Ranking” in the “Scholarship” section) and at EDISU counters.

classification.raw: No individual communication will be sent regarding the outcome of the competitions. The rankings can only be consulted on your personal Online Helpdesk page (by clicking on “Ranking” in the “Scholarship” section) and at EDISU counters.

items.text: Art. 12(1) CRITERIA FOR DRAWING UP THE RANKINGS

classification.title: Art. 12 (1) CRITERIA FOR DRAWING UP THE RANKINGS
classification.raw: Art. 12(1) CRITERIA FOR DRAWING UP THE RANKINGS

items.text: For students enrolled in the first years of bachelor’s degree, single-cycle master’s degree, and master's degree courses who submit a Standard Application, the grants are allocated on the basis of the provisions of art. 1(4).

classification.raw: For students enrolled in the first years of bachelor’s degree, single-cycle master’s degree, and master's degree courses who submit a Standard Application, the grants are allocated on the basis of the provisions of art. 1(4).

items.text: The rankings are divided by University and course level (bachelor’s degree, single-cycle master's degree, and master's degree), without distinction between departments, and within each level they are ordered in ascending order on the basis of the ISEE University (ISEE Parificato for non-EU students with family residing in a non-EU country). If the ISEE Universitario is the same, younger students have priority.51

classification.raw: The rankings are divided by University and course level (bachelor’s degree, single-cycle master's degree, and master's degree), without distinction between departments, and within each level they are ordered in ascending order on the basis of the ISEE University (ISEE Parificato for non-EU students with family residing in a non-EU country). If the ISEE Universitario is the same, younger students have priority.51

items.text: The accommodation service rankings are formulated in the same way as for the scholarship, guaranteeing at least one spot per university.

classification.raw: The accommodation service rankings are formulated in the same way as for the scholarship, guaranteeing at least one spot per university.

items.text: In drawing up the accommodation service rankings, a standard first-year ranking will be drawn up for each location for students with non-EU citizenship and a household resident in a non-EU country and students with international protection status, as provided for in point 1 of art. 4(1), for which 1, 5 \% of the spots allocated to each University for first-year students will be reserved.

classification.raw: In drawing up the accommodation service rankings, a standard first-year ranking will be drawn up for each location for students with non-EU citizenship and a household resident in a non-EU country and students with international protection status, as provided for in point 1 of art. 4(1), for which 1, 5 \% of the spots allocated to each University for first-year students will be reserved.

classification.raw: Art. 12(2) PROVISIONAL RANKINGS

items.text: Two outcomes are possible in the provisional ranking:

classification.raw: Two outcomes are possible in the provisional ranking:

items.text: 1. Eligible: students meet the requirements of the notice of competition; they must therefore wait for the publication of the definitive ranking. 2. Excluded: students do not meet the requirements of the notice of competition or have made material errors when completing the application. If errors can be rectified at the complaint stage, the words “can be rectified by complaint” will be indicated. If students do not rectify their exclusion at the complaint stage against the provisional ranking, they will be disqualified.

If students do not rectify their exclusion at the complaint stage against the provisional ranking, they will be disqualified.

items.text: Students excluded from the provisional rankings, or eligible students who need to change their data, may submit a complaint via the online procedure in accordance with the procedures set out in art. 7(6) and within the deadlines indicated in art. 7(5). The outcomes of the complaints will be available with the publication of the definitive rankings.

classification.title: Art. 12 (4) FINAL ACCOMMODATION RANKINGS
classification.raw: Art. 12(4) FINAL ACCOMMODATION RANKINGS

items.text: Three outcomes are possible:

classification.raw: Three outcomes are possible:

items.text: 1. Winner: students meet the requirements set out in the notice of competition and are entitled to accommodation in EDISU residences; they must also accept the online accommodation service in the manner set out in art. 33(1). Eligible: students who meet the requirements set out in the notice of competition but are not eligible for the accommodation service at EDISU residences due to a shortage of available beds, must also declare their interest in the online accommodation service in accordance with the procedures set out in art. 33(2). 3. Excluded: students do not meet the requirements of the notice of competition or have not rectified material errors present in the application via complaint.

items.text: If students change course location after the publication of the final accommodation service rankings or scrolling rankings, they will be placed at the end of the definitive ranking or scrolling ranking of the new course location. If there is more than one student to be placed in the new course location, the order of placement will be based on the date and time of notification by the student of the change of course location. They must, in any case, be accepted at the location indicated in the final ranking.

items.text: Art. 6 of Ministerial Decree 1320/2022 provides that students enrolled at the same time in more than one course are allowed to obtain and keep the scholarship, increased by 2, 0 \% , if they meet and maintain the merit requirements, set out in the decree itself, for the entire duration of the courses. The increase is not due if the student loses eligibility for the grant. Nor is it paid if the student loses eligibility for the increase itself. Students enrolling in the filter semester may NOT indicate a related course as their second course and must follow the provisions of art. 9(3) of this notice.

items.text: Students applying for the scholarship as a first-year bachelor’s degree or single-cycle master's degree student may only request the increase for a first year of a bachelor’s degree or single-cycle master's degree, they may NOT request it for a subsequent year or a master's degree.

items.text: The increase, reserved exclusively for students enrolling in both the main course and the second full-time course, will be disbursed together with the second instalment of the scholarship for students enrolled in the first years of the course, subject to any changes introduced by the MIUR Ministerial Decree implementing Law 33/2022.
