items.text: To obtain the increase, the student must achieve 20 credits by 10 August 2026 in both the course for which they applied for the scholarship and in the course in which they applied for the increase, if related to a first year of a course.

classification.raw: To obtain the increase, the student must achieve 20 credits by 10 August 2026 in both the course for which they applied for the scholarship and in the course in which they applied for the increase, if related to a first year of a course.

items.text: If 20 credits are not obtained in the course for which the scholarship was applied for, the right to the 20 \% increase also lapses. If the 20 credits are obtained for the course for which the scholarship is requested but not for the course for which the 20 \% increase is requested, the student remains a scholarship recipient but loses the right to the increase.

The same provisions apply for the 20% increase of the grant for the second course referred to in paragraph 2 above.

items.text: 55 Ministerial Decree 509/99, Ministerial Decree 270/04

classification.title: Ministerial Decree 509/99, Ministerial Decree 270/04
classification.raw: 55 Ministerial Decree 509/99, Ministerial Decree 270/04

items.text: Students who have are transferring from another University, including from outside Piedmont, may not apply as a first-year but must apply as a subsequent-year student. For more information, please refer to paragraph 4 of this article. 2. If students have to repeat the same year, the merit requirements, set out in the table in paragraph 2, will in any case be calculated on the basis of the academic year of first absolute enrolment for the same course level. 3. Students who enrol after waiving their studies and obtain credit validation must check, based on the number of credits recognised, whether they can apply as a subsequent-year student, or whether they will be considered first-year students again. Please note that the scholarship cannot be applied for in respect of course years for which it was already obtained56. 4. If the students who withdraw from their studies or lose their student status have benefited from a scholarship or scholarship and accommodation service for more than one academic year during their previous course enrolment, by enrolling in a new course, they will be prevented from applying for benefits from EDISU for the same number of years as they benefited from these during their previous enrolment. This is also true if they have withdrawn from a bachelor’s degree or master's degree course and have subsequently enrolled in a single-cycle master's degree course, and vice versa. The only exception is if they return the scholarship, they benefited from by the deadline established for scholarship complaints for the academic year 2025/26. 5. International student visa applicants who wish to change their course of study must check with their University whether or not they can opt out of their studies without having to apply for a new visa, based on the MUR circular for the academic year 2025/26. In the event of a transfer of course or university, reference is made to paragraph 4 of this article. 6. If students have regularly frozen or suspended57 their student career in previous academic years, the period of freezing/suspension will not be taken into account if no degree-related action (passing examinations, submitting a career plan, etc.) has been taken during that period. To this end, EDISU will modify the year of enrolment of students in such a way that the freezing/suspension period does not negatively affect the calculation of merit requirements (e.g. if a student enrolled in 2023/24 has frozen/suspended their career in 2024/25, by applying for 2025/26 they will be considered as having enrolled in 2024/25). When submitting their application, students must notify the freezing of their previous career by opening a ticket. 7. Students who “enrol early” from March 2025 must follow the provisions of art. 9(2) and (5) of this article.

items.text: Students who, by 10 August 2025, fail to reach the minimum credit thresholds set out in Tables A2 and B, which are an integral part of this notice of competition, have the opportunity to benefit from “bonus” credits that are accrued on the basis of the year attended, starting from the year of first enrolment in bachelor’s degree or single-cycle master's degree courses and according to the following progression:

classification.raw: Students who, by 10 August 2025, fail to reach the minimum credit thresholds set out in Tables A2 and B, which are an integral part of this notice of competition, have the opportunity to benefit from “bonus” credits that are accrued on the basis of the year attended, starting from the year of first enrolment in bachelor’s degree or single-cycle master's degree courses and according to the following progression:

items.text: 1. Students enrolled full-time.

classification.title: Students enrolled full-time.
classification.raw: 1. Students enrolled full-time.

items.text: a. 5 bonuses: to claim benefits for the second year; b. 12 bonuses: to claim benefits for the third year; c. 15 bonuses: to claim benefits for years after the third year.

items.text: Once used, the bonus available to the student may no longer be increased, but any remaining bonus share may be used in subsequent years.

classification.raw: Once used, the bonus available to the student may no longer be increased, but any remaining bonus share may be used in subsequent years.

items.text: During enrolment in a master's degree course, NO new “bonus” credits are accrued; therefore, students enrolled in subsequent years of master's degree courses may only benefit from any remaining, unused bonus credits (if enrolled on a part-time basis, they will have a maximum of 9 “bonus” credits available).

classification.raw: During enrolment in a master's degree course, NO new “bonus” credits are accrued; therefore, students enrolled in subsequent years of master's degree courses may only benefit from any remaining, unused bonus credits (if enrolled on a part-time basis, they will have a maximum of 9 “bonus” credits available).

items.text: Students who have completed their bachelor's degree abroad automatically have a residual 15 “bonus” credits.

classification.raw: Students who have completed their bachelor's degree abroad automatically have a residual 15 “bonus” credits.

items.text: Only students who have graduated from a non-Piedmontese Italian university must self-certify the number of residual “bonus” credits in the application for benefits (only indicate 0 residual “bonus” credits if you used them in full during your previous degree career). Students who did not apply for any scholarships during their enrolment in the bachelor’s degree have the maximum number of bonus credits accrued available.

Students who did not apply for any scholarships during their enrolment in the bachelor’s degree have the maximum number of bonus credits accrued available.

items.text: In the event of the declaration of residual bonuses (between 1 and 15) and should it be necessary to use them for the current academic year, EDISU will check with the reference agency for the right to study for one's degree.

classification.raw: In the event of the declaration of residual bonuses (between 1 and 15) and should it be necessary to use them for the current academic year, EDISU will check with the reference agency for the right to study for one's degree.

items.text: For all students, the bonus will be awarded automatically in the right number for meeting the minimum merit requirement. Any bonus credits used out of the number of available bonuses will be visible in the ranking.

classification.raw: For all students, the bonus will be awarded automatically in the right number for meeting the minimum merit requirement. Any bonus credits used out of the number of available bonuses will be visible in the ranking.

items.text: Please note:

classification.title: Please note:
classification.raw: Please note:

items.text: 1. bonus credits can only be used to reach the minimum merit thresholds for admission to the competition (Standard Application); they cannot be used to reach the merit required for Confirmation of Merit Benefits; 2. students enrolled in master's degree courses who were enrolled in bachelor’s degree courses under the old system are not eligible for bonuses; 3. bonus credits have value only in the year of use and will not be counted in subsequent years as credits earned. It should be noted, in fact, that “bonuses” are additional credits provided solely to enable students lacking certain credits to be admitted to the competition for the academic year 2025/26; they do not contribute to the calculation of the merit score nor to the calculation of the ranking score (art. 17(2)) 4. bonus credits may NOT be used to meet the merit requirements for the course for which the possible 20 \% increase in the grant amount is being applied for, in the event of enrolment in a subsequent year.

items.text: Changing course within the same University and transferring from another University, even if not in Piedmont, does not amount to a career break. Therefore, following a transfer, the year of first enrolment will be considered to be that of the previous course and an application cannot be submitted as a first-year student, irrespective of the course year in which the student is enrolled by the University or AFAM, as laid down by Ministerial Decree 1320/2021 and the Regional Criteria.

classification.raw: Changing course within the same University and transferring from another University, even if not in Piedmont, does not amount to a career break. Therefore, following a transfer, the year of first enrolment will be considered to be that of the previous course and an application cannot be submitted as a first-year student, irrespective of the course year in which the student is enrolled by the University or AFAM, as laid down by Ministerial Decree 1320/2021 and the Regional Criteria.

items.text: For example, if students begin their student career in a Bachelor of Science in Nursing course at the University of Turin in the academic year 2023/24 or in the academic year 2025/26 and switch to the Biology Degree course, the year of first enrolment in the latter course will also be considered 2023/24 for the purposes of awarding the scholarship.

classification.raw: For example, if students begin their student career in a Bachelor of Science in Nursing course at the University of Turin in the academic year 2023/24 or in the academic year 2025/26 and switch to the Biology Degree course, the year of first enrolment in the latter course will also be considered 2023/24 for the purposes of awarding the scholarship.

items.text: The same provisions also apply in the event of a transfer from a bachelor’s degree course to a single-cycle master’s degree course and vice versa.

classification.raw: The same provisions also apply in the event of a transfer from a bachelor’s degree course to a single-cycle master’s degree course and vice versa.

items.text: Merit requirements will therefore be calculated on the basis of the year of first enrolment: students must possess the required number of credits by 10 August 2025 and at least as many credits must be validated following the transfer. Credits earned before transfers and not validated cannot be taken into account.

items.text: In order to be included in the final scholarship rankings of 16 December 2025, students must have their credits validated by 30 November 2025. Should the validation be decided after this date, EDISU will verify that the students have maintained the merit requirements.

The awarding of the scholarship is subject to the availability of funds.

items.text: ART. 16: METHODS AND DEADLINES FOR SUBMITTING AN APPLICATION

classification.title: Art. 16 : METHODS AND DEADLINES FOR SUBMITTING AN APPLICATION
classification.raw: ART. 16: METHODS AND DEADLINES FOR SUBMITTING AN APPLICATION

items.text: Art. 16(1) METHOD FOR SUBMITTING THE APPLICATION

classification.title: Art. 16 (1) METHOD FOR SUBMITTING THE APPLICATION
classification.raw: Art. 16(1) METHOD FOR SUBMITTING THE APPLICATION

items.text: Applications can be submitted from 25 July 2025. The application submission methods are set out in art. 7(1.1) (for students who have a SPID or CIE) and (1.2) (for international students who have a 2025/26 Temporary Access Code). International students with a household resident abroad must also abide by the application submission methods outlined in arts. 30 and 31.

classification.raw: Applications can be submitted from 25 July 2025. The application submission methods are set out in art. 7(1.1) (for students who have a SPID or CIE) and (1.2) (for international students who have a 2025/26 Temporary Access Code). International students with a household resident abroad must also abide by the application submission methods outlined in arts. 30 and 31.

items.text: Any event occurring after the date on which the application is submitted (award of another scholarship or other financial aid, transfer to another university, withdrawal from studies, change of commitment from part-time to full-time and vice versa, inability to continue studies, withdrawal from enrolment, change of department or course of study, etc.) must be promptly communicated to the EDISU offices and documented. EDISU will check that the requirements are still met and will take any measures necessary to revoke benefits. If this is not the case, EDISU reserves the right to take action in relation to the failure to notify. In the event of transfer to another University outside Piedmont or withdrawal from studies after the publication of the definitive rankings and during the academic year, the benefit won or the eligibility condition will be revoked.

classification.raw: Any event occurring after the date on which the application is submitted (award of another scholarship or other financial aid, transfer to another university, withdrawal from studies, change of commitment from part-time to full-time and vice versa, inability to continue studies, withdrawal from enrolment, change of department or course of study, etc.) must be promptly communicated to the EDISU offices and documented. EDISU will check that the requirements are still met and will take any measures necessary to revoke benefits. If this is not the case, EDISU reserves the right to take action in relation to the failure to notify. In the event of transfer to another University outside Piedmont or withdrawal from studies after the publication of the definitive rankings and during the academic year, the benefit won or the eligibility condition will be revoked.

items.text: For students who submit their application with a Temporary Access Code, transmission by 12:00 p.m. will only be possible after attaching the signed PDF of the application and a copy of their valid identity document.

items.text: Art. 16(3) APPLICATION CONVERSION

classification.title: Art. 16 (3) APPLICATION CONVERSION
classification.raw: Art. 16(3) APPLICATION CONVERSION

items.text: If students apply for the subsequent year for the additional semester of a bachelor’s degree but enrol in a master's degree course by 30 November, they may inform EDISU by 12 November via a special complaint procedure, indicating the details of the master's degree course; the application will be converted into a first-year, master’s degree application, if they meet the requirements.

classification.raw: If students apply for the subsequent year for the additional semester of a bachelor’s degree but enrol in a master's degree course by 30 November, they may inform EDISU by 12 November via a special complaint procedure, indicating the details of the master's degree course; the application will be converted into a first-year, master’s degree application, if they meet the requirements.

items.text: If students enrolled in a degree course during the academic year 2022/23 apply for the first year of a master’s degree but do not enrol, they may inform EDISU by means of a special complaint procedure by 12 November to be included in the rankings of 16 December or by 3 April if “suspended” in the rankings of 16 December to be included in those of 30 April 2026. They will need to indicate the data for the bachelor's degree course. The application will be converted into an application for subsequent years for the additional semester of a bachelor's degree, if they have not graduated by September 2025 and if they meet the merit requirements.

classification.raw: If students enrolled in a degree course during the academic year 2022/23 apply for the first year of a master’s degree but do not enrol, they may inform EDISU by means of a special complaint procedure by 12 November to be included in the rankings of 16 December or by 3 April if “suspended” in the rankings of 16 December to be included in those of 30 April 2026. They will need to indicate the data for the bachelor's degree course. The application will be converted into an application for subsequent years for the additional semester of a bachelor's degree, if they have not graduated by September 2025 and if they meet the merit requirements.

items.text: ART. 17 RANKINGS AND COMPLAINTS

classification.title: Art. 17 RANKINGS AND COMPLAINTS
classification.raw: ART. 17 RANKINGS AND COMPLAINTS

items.text: Interested students will make sure to view the rankings in the manner indicated below as no further communications will be sent. The rankings will be published on your personal Online Helpdesk page, “Scholarship” section, by clicking on “Ranking”. Students who have applied for both the accommodation service and the grant must consult two separate rankings: the first for the accommodation service, the second for the scholarship. For each benefit applied for, first a provisional ranking and then a definitive ranking will be published.

classification.raw: Interested students will make sure to view the rankings in the manner indicated below as no further communications will be sent. The rankings will be published on your personal Online Helpdesk page, “Scholarship” section, by clicking on “Ranking”. Students who have applied for both the accommodation service and the grant must consult two separate rankings: the first for the accommodation service, the second for the scholarship. For each benefit applied for, first a provisional ranking and then a definitive ranking will be published.

items.text: After the deadline for the submission of grant applications, before the publication of the provisional grant ranking and in the period between the publication of the provisional and final grant rankings, checks will be carried out with the Universities in order to ascertain the regular enrolment of students and the merit requirements. The provisional and final scholarship rankings will incorporate the results of these assessments.

classification.raw: After the deadline for the submission of grant applications, before the publication of the provisional grant ranking and in the period between the publication of the provisional and final grant rankings, checks will be carried out with the Universities in order to ascertain the regular enrolment of students and the merit requirements. The provisional and final scholarship rankings will incorporate the results of these assessments.

items.text: The accommodation service rankings will instead be drawn up on the basis of self-certified data from students. At the request of students, checks may also be carried out when the application is being completed in particular situations. Changes will also be automatically made following checks carried out with the Universities on the applicant's merit conditions

items.text: The grants are distributed according to art. 1(4).

classification.raw: The grants are distributed according to art. 1(4).

items.text: The rankings are formulated in the following manner and in the following order of priority:

classification.raw: The rankings are formulated in the following manner and in the following order of priority:

items.text: Confirmations of Merit Benefits years subsequent to the first (also includes additional semesters). The ranking is ordered by decreasing score and broken down by University, without distinction by level and department. If their points are equal, students with the lowest ISEE Universitario (ISEE Parificato for non-EU international students with family residing in a non-EU country) have priority. If the ISEE Universitario is the same, younger students have priority. 2. Confirmations Merit Benefits first year of master's degree. The ranking is ordered by lower ISEE Universitario (ISEE Parificato for non-EU international students with family residing in a non-EU country) increasing and subdivided by University, without distinction by department. If the ISEE Universitario is the same, younger students have priority. 3. Standard application years subsequent to the first. The rankings are broken down by University, level and department, then sorted in descending order of points. If their points are equal, students with the lowest ISEE Universitario (ISEE Parificato for non-EU students with family residing in a non-EU country) have priority. If the ISEE Universitario is the same, younger students have priority.

items: Confirmations of Merit Benefits years subsequent to the first (also includes additional semesters). The ranking is ordered by decreasing score and broken down by University, without distinction by level and department. If their points are equal, students with the lowest ISEE Universitario (ISEE Parificato for non-EU international students with family residing in a non-EU country) have priority. If the ISEE Universitario is the same, younger students have priority.

2. Confirmations Merit Benefits first year of master's degree. The ranking is ordered by lower ISEE Universitario (ISEE Parificato for non-EU international students with family residing in a non-EU country) increasing and subdivided by University, without distinction by department. If the ISEE Universitario is the same, younger students have priority.

If their points are equal, students with the lowest ISEE Universitario (ISEE Parificato for non-EU students with family residing in a non-EU country) have priority. If the ISEE Universitario is the same, younger students have priority.

items.text: For students enrolled in degree courses on a part-time basis, the score is calculated by multiplying the actual score by the 1.53 coefficient already applied to identify the minimum merit requirements with respect to those required by the Prime Ministerial Decree of 9 April 2001 in the case of full-time enrolment.

items.text: The accommodation service rankings are drawn up in the same way as for the scholarship, in order to distribute the beds indicated in the notice of competition among the Universities, maintaining the same percentage in relation to those entitled and guaranteeing at least one bed per University.

classification.raw: The accommodation service rankings are drawn up in the same way as for the scholarship, in order to distribute the beds indicated in the notice of competition among the Universities, maintaining the same percentage in relation to those entitled and guaranteeing at least one bed per University.

items.text: If students change course location after the publication of the final accommodation service rankings or scrolling rankings, they will be placed at the end of the definitive ranking or scrolling ranking of the new course location. If there is more than one student to be placed in the new course location, the order of placement will be based on the date and time of notification by the student of the change of course location.

classification.raw: If students change course location after the publication of the final accommodation service rankings or scrolling rankings, they will be placed at the end of the definitive ranking or scrolling ranking of the new course location. If there is more than one student to be placed in the new course location, the order of placement will be based on the date and time of notification by the student of the change of course location.

items.text: The merit score relates to a student's academic career and is obtained by multiplying the credits by the grade for each examination and adding up all the resulting partials.

classification.raw: The merit score relates to a student's academic career and is obtained by multiplying the credits by the grade for each examination and adding up all the resulting partials.

items.text: The ranking score is the score used to determine the position of students in the ranking and is obtained by dividing the merit score by the number of years of enrolment of the students decreased by one unit (in the case of a second year the merit score remains the same, in the case of a third year it is divided by two, in the case of an additional semester by three, etc.).

classification.raw: The ranking score is the score used to determine the position of students in the ranking and is obtained by dividing the merit score by the number of years of enrolment of the students decreased by one unit (in the case of a second year the merit score remains the same, in the case of a third year it is divided by two, in the case of an additional semester by three, etc.).

items.text: Examinations with a grade not expressed out of thirty (“merit”, “pass”, etc.) are assigned a grade equal to the weighted average calculated on the other examinations with a numerical grade.

classification.raw: Examinations with a grade not expressed out of thirty (“merit”, “pass”, etc.) are assigned a grade equal to the weighted average calculated on the other examinations with a numerical grade.

items.text: Honours are not taken into account in the calculation of scores.

classification.raw: Honours are not taken into account in the calculation of scores.

The increase is not due if the student loses eligibility for the grant. Nor is it paid if the student loses eligibility for the increase itself.

classification.title: Art. 18 (1) DISBURSEMENT PERIOD
classification.raw: Art. 18(1) DISBURSEMENT PERIOD

items.text: Scholarship winners receive the benefit, according to the total amounts indicated in art. 5, in two instalments:

classification.raw: Scholarship winners receive the benefit, according to the total amounts indicated in art. 5, in two instalments:

items.text: 1. the first instalment (equal to 50 \% of the total amount) is disbursed starting from the end of December 2025;

classification.title: the first instalment (equal to 50 \% of the total amount) is disbursed starting from the end of December 2025;
classification.raw: 1. the first instalment (equal to 50 \% of the total amount) is disbursed starting from the end of December 2025;

items.text: The first instalment will be disbursed from the end of December 2025 if students provide their SEPA-circuit IBAN by 14 December 2025.

classification.raw: The first instalment will be disbursed from the end of December 2025 if students provide their SEPA-circuit IBAN by 14 December 2025.

items.text: If students communicate their IBAN by 13 April 2026, the first instalment will be disbursed from the end of April 2026.

classification.raw: If students communicate their IBAN by 13 April 2026, the first instalment will be disbursed from the end of April 2026.

items.text: For only those students suspended in the December rankings and successful in the rankings of 30 April 2026, the first instalment will be disbursed from the end of May 2025 if students communicate their IBAN by 10 May 2026.

classification.raw: For only those students suspended in the December rankings and successful in the rankings of 30 April 2026, the first instalment will be disbursed from the end of May 2025 if students communicate their IBAN by 10 May 2026.

items.text: Students awarded scholarships with FSE funds must provide the IBAN on which the amount of the scholarship will be credited by EDISU no later than 15 March 2026, without prejudice to derogations previously authorised.

classification.raw: Students awarded scholarships with FSE funds must provide the IBAN on which the amount of the scholarship will be credited by EDISU no later than 15 March 2026, without prejudice to derogations previously authorised.

items.text: Subsequent payments of the first instalment are scheduled to coincide with the disbursement period of the second instalments.

classification.raw: Subsequent payments of the first instalment are scheduled to coincide with the disbursement period of the second instalments.

items.text: No ad-hoc payments will be made to students who enter their IBAN late.

classification.raw: No ad-hoc payments will be made to students who enter their IBAN late.

items.text: 2. the second instalment, equal to the remaining 50 \% of the total amount, is disbursed from the end of June 2026, by crediting an IBAN from mid-July 2026. per IBAN inseriti entro il 30 giugno 2026

classification.title: the second instalment, equal to the remaining 50 \% of the total amount, is disbursed from the end of June 2026, by crediting an IBAN from mid-July 2026. per IBAN inseriti entro il 30 giugno 2026
classification.raw: 2. the second instalment, equal to the remaining 50 \% of the total amount, is disbursed from the end of June 2026, by crediting an IBAN from mid-July 2026. per IBAN inseriti entro il 30 giugno 2026

items.text: The second instalment of the scholarship will be paid automatically upon verification that the student career requirements have been met. There is no further merit requirement than required for the submission of the scholarship application and therefore no self-certification of merit must be submitted for the second instalment.

classification.raw: The second instalment of the scholarship will be paid automatically upon verification that the student career requirements have been met. There is no further merit requirement than required for the submission of the scholarship application and therefore no self-certification of merit must be submitted for the second instalment.

items.text: Possible 20 \% increase of amount: 5, 0 \% on the first instalment and 5, 0 \% on the second instalment upon verification of the achievement of 20 credits in the case of first-year enrolment.

classification.raw: Possible 20 \% increase of amount: 5, 0 \% on the first instalment and 5, 0 \% on the second instalment upon verification of the achievement of 20 credits in the case of first-year enrolment.

items.text: In the case of enrolment in the first year for the course for which the increase is requested, in order to receive the second part of the increase together with the second instalment of the scholarship, the student who achieves the 20 credits by 31 May 2026 must also self-certify them by 8 June 2026 as stipulated in art. 13(4). Otherwise, EDISU will carry out automatic checks in November 2025 and, if the outcome is positive, the increase will be paid out in December (preparation of the appropriate deed at the end of November together with the checks for the second instalment of the first-year scholarship).

classification.raw: In the case of enrolment in the first year for the course for which the increase is requested, in order to receive the second part of the increase together with the second instalment of the scholarship, the student who achieves the 20 credits by 31 May 2026 must also self-certify them by 8 June 2026 as stipulated in art. 13(4). Otherwise, EDISU will carry out automatic checks in November 2025 and, if the outcome is positive, the increase will be paid out in December (preparation of the appropriate deed at the end of November together with the checks for the second instalment of the first-year scholarship).

items.text: Students awarded a scholarship for an additional semester receive a single instalment at the end of December 2025, which will not be revoked if the students do not graduate by 31 March 2026.

classification.raw: Students awarded a scholarship for an additional semester receive a single instalment at the end of December 2025, which will not be revoked if the students do not graduate by 31 March 2026.

items.text: It should be noted that the disbursement of the instalment means the start of the administrative process, which is concluded the following month with the provision of the payment by EDISU to the Credit Institute indicated in the IBAN provided by the students. The sum may, therefore, become available in the current account in the month following the disbursement, based on the timing of the individual credit institutions.

classification.raw: It should be noted that the disbursement of the instalment means the start of the administrative process, which is concluded the following month with the provision of the payment by EDISU to the Credit Institute indicated in the IBAN provided by the students. The sum may, therefore, become available in the current account in the month following the disbursement, based on the timing of the individual credit institutions.

items.text: Winners may only receive the scholarship by crediting a current account or prepaid card with a SEPA-circuit IBAN in their name or jointly in their name. Postal savings books are excluded. For more information on how to communicate the IBAN, please refer to art. 7(3).

classification.raw: Winners may only receive the scholarship by crediting a current account or prepaid card with a SEPA-circuit IBAN in their name or jointly in their name. Postal savings books are excluded. For more information on how to communicate the IBAN, please refer to art. 7(3).

items.text: For students in subsequent years who were successful or eligible for a scholarship in the definitive ranking for the academic year. 2024/25 and who, at the time of enrolment, have paid the first instalment of tuition fees, may submit an application for reimbursement of the regional tax of € 140.00 online, in accordance with the Regional Tax Reimbursement Regulations available on the EDISU website. The special procedure must be completed from January to June 2026.

classification.raw: For students in subsequent years who were successful or eligible for a scholarship in the definitive ranking for the academic year. 2024/25 and who, at the time of enrolment, have paid the first instalment of tuition fees, may submit an application for reimbursement of the regional tax of € 140.00 online, in accordance with the Regional Tax Reimbursement Regulations available on the EDISU website. The special procedure must be completed from January to June 2026.

items.text: Reimbursement will only be granted if the benefit is maintained; payment will be made after the second instalment of the scholarship has been paid (Notice of Award of scholarship, accommodation service, and degree award for the relevant academic year).

items.text: Disbursement of the degree award will take place from the end of July 2026.

items.text: It should be noted that disbursement means the act by which EDISU orders payment. The actual availability of the sum in the students' current account or on their card will therefore take place in the following working days, based on the technical timing of the individual credit institutions.

classification.raw: It should be noted that disbursement means the act by which EDISU orders payment. The actual availability of the sum in the students' current account or on their card will therefore take place in the following working days, based on the technical timing of the individual credit institutions.

items.text: Winners may only receive the degree award by crediting a current account or prepaid card with an Italian IBAN in their name or jointly in their name. Postal savings books are excluded. For more information on how to communicate the IBAN, please refer to art. 7(3).

items.text: Art. 24(1) ADMISSION REQUIREMENTS

classification.title: Art. 24 (1) ADMISSION REQUIREMENTS
classification.raw: Art. 24(1) ADMISSION REQUIREMENTS

items.text: For both the first years and subsequent years, in addition to the financial requirements (art. 6 and Section VII Specific Provisions for international Students - and paragraph 3 below), only admission to or enrolment in a doctoral course or specialisation school is required, which will be verified when the provisional and final scholarship rankings are drawn up.

classification.raw: For both the first years and subsequent years, in addition to the financial requirements (art. 6 and Section VII Specific Provisions for international Students - and paragraph 3 below), only admission to or enrolment in a doctoral course or specialisation school is required, which will be verified when the provisional and final scholarship rankings are drawn up.

items.text: Art. 24(2) MERIT REQUIREMENTS

classification.title: Art. 24 (2) MERIT REQUIREMENTS
classification.raw: Art. 24(2) MERIT REQUIREMENTS

items.text: In addition to the financial (art. 6 and Section VII Specific Provisions for international Students - and paragraph 3 below) and admission requirements (paragraph 1 of this article), for years subsequent to the first there are no further merit requirements for admission to the rankings except for admission to the year for which the scholarship is requested

classification.raw: In addition to the financial (art. 6 and Section VII Specific Provisions for international Students - and paragraph 3 below) and admission requirements (paragraph 1 of this article), for years subsequent to the first there are no further merit requirements for admission to the rankings except for admission to the year for which the scholarship is requested

items.text: The scholarship will be withdrawn if students renounce their PhD during the academic year or apply to free or suspend their student career in the academic year 2025/26.

classification.raw: The scholarship will be withdrawn if students renounce their PhD during the academic year or apply to free or suspend their student career in the academic year 2025/26.

items.text: If students have regularly frozen or suspended63 their student career in previous academic years, the period of freezing/suspension will not be taken into account if no degree-related action has been taken during that period. To this end, EDISU will amend the year of enrolment of students in such a way that the freezing/suspension period does not adversely affect the calculation of merit requirements. When submitting their application, students must notify the freezing of their previous career by opening a ticket.

classification.raw: If students have regularly frozen or suspended63 their student career in previous academic years, the period of freezing/suspension will not be taken into account if no degree-related action has been taken during that period. To this end, EDISU will amend the year of enrolment of students in such a way that the freezing/suspension period does not adversely affect the calculation of merit requirements. When submitting their application, students must notify the freezing of their previous career by opening a ticket.

items.text: The ISEE Dottorato certificate must be signed and issued as of 1 January 2025. Please note that the ISEE certificate, in order to be considered valid for the competition, must apply, in respect of the applicant benefits, to doctoral courses or, alternatively, be valid for a restricted family, should the applicant wish to take advantage of it. It must also be free of observations regarding omissions/formalities (art. 6(1)).

items.text: Please note that in order to take advantage of the “Nucleo Ristretto” (Restricted Household) option, interested students must sign the DSU for the issue of the ISEE certificate as “declarants”. It should be noted that, in the case of married students, the household for the processing the certificate with the “Nucleo Ristretto” (Restricted Household) option also includes the spouse and children (if any).

classification.raw: Please note that in order to take advantage of the “Nucleo Ristretto” (Restricted Household) option, interested students must sign the DSU for the issue of the ISEE certificate as “declarants”. It should be noted that, in the case of married students, the household for the processing the certificate with the “Nucleo Ristretto” (Restricted Household) option also includes the spouse and children (if any).

items.text: It should be noted that international students may submit the ISEE Dottorato certificate with the “nucleo ristretto” (restricted household) option only if, on the date of submission of the application they have already taken up residence in Italy (art. 6(1)).

classification.raw: It should be noted that international students may submit the ISEE Dottorato certificate with the “nucleo ristretto” (restricted household) option only if, on the date of submission of the application they have already taken up residence in Italy (art. 6(1)).

items.text: Please note: students enrolled in a doctoral course or specialisation school cannot apply as independent students (art. 6(1.5)) since this case only applies to the ISEE Universitario – not to the ISEE Dottorato.

items.text: It should be noted that the request to process the ISEE Parificato according to the ordinary rules excludes the use of the ISEE Dottorato and the “Nucleo Ristretto” (Restricted Household) option. Please refer to arts. 30 and 31 of this notice of competition for the Consular Documents required to apply for their issue.

items.text: Please note that, if students are resident in Italy while their spouses and/or children, if any, reside abroad, they will need to apply for a certificate for ISEE Dottorato/nucleo ristretto that includes the applicant and then supplement the financial data relating to the members residing abroad by requesting an ISEE Parificato certificate. In this case, in order to complete the application for benefits, both certificates, as well as the consular documentation relating to the members of the household residing abroad (arts. 30 and 31 of this notice of competition), must be submitted.

In this case, in order to complete the application for benefits, both certificates, as well as the consular documentation relating to the members of the household residing abroad (arts. 30 and 31 of this notice of competition), must be submitted.

items.text: If non-EU or EU international students residing in Italy benefit from the ISEE Dottorato or ISEE nucleo ristretto, in the online application procedure they must consider only themselves (and any spouses and minor children) as a family; therefore, the type of scholarship will be subject to their registered residence in Italy (art. 4 of this notice of competition).

items.text: Applications can be submitted from 28 October 2025. The application submission methods are set out in art. 7(1.1) (for Italian students who have a SPID or CIE) and (1.2) (for international students without residence in Italy). International students with a household resident in a non-EU or EU country must also follow the application submission methods outlined in arts. 30 and 31.

International students with a household resident in a non-EU or EU country must also follow the application submission methods outlined in arts. 30 and 31.

items.text: Students who have already applied for the additional semester of a master’s degree or single-cycle master's degree may still apply for benefits for the first year of a PhD/specialisation school. If they are awarded a scholarship, they may receive one instalment for the master's degree and two instalments for the PhD/specialisation school. To submit an application, this type of student must open a ticket by 28 November 2025, enclosing proof of identity, indicating the University and course and its duration.

To submit an application, this type of student must open a ticket by 28 November 2025, enclosing proof of identity, indicating the University and course and its duration.

items.text: Art. 26(1) CRITERIA FOR DRAWING UP THE RANKINGS

classification.title: Art. 26 (1) CRITERIA FOR DRAWING UP THE RANKINGS
classification.raw: Art. 26(1) CRITERIA FOR DRAWING UP THE RANKINGS

items.text: According to art. 1(4), those enrolled in the first years of doctoral and specialisation school courses are included in the allocation of 1, 5 \% of the grants assigned to each University. The allocation is made based on students applying for the grant as first-year students. The rankings are formulated according to the University they attend in an ascending manner based on the students' ISEE Dottorato. Where the ISEE Dottorato is equal, preference is given to younger applicants.

classification.raw: According to art. 1(4), those enrolled in the first years of doctoral and specialisation school courses are included in the allocation of 1, 5 \% of the grants assigned to each University. The allocation is made based on students applying for the grant as first-year students. The rankings are formulated according to the University they attend in an ascending manner based on the students' ISEE Dottorato. Where the ISEE Dottorato is equal, preference is given to younger applicants.

items.text: According to art. 1(4), those enrolled in years subsequent to the first of doctoral and specialisation school courses are included in the allocation of 85 \% of the grants assigned to each University. The allocation is made based on students applying for the grant as subsequent-year students. The rankings are formulated according to the University they attend in an ascending manner based on the students' ISEE Dottorato. Where the ISEE Dottorato is equal, preference is given to younger applicants.

classification.raw: According to art. 1(4), those enrolled in years subsequent to the first of doctoral and specialisation school courses are included in the allocation of 85 \% of the grants assigned to each University. The allocation is made based on students applying for the grant as subsequent-year students. The rankings are formulated according to the University they attend in an ascending manner based on the students' ISEE Dottorato. Where the ISEE Dottorato is equal, preference is given to younger applicants.

items.text: Any event occurring after the date on which the application is submitted (award of another scholarship or other financial aid, transfer to another university, withdrawal from studies, inability to continue studies, withdrawal from enrolment, etc.) must be promptly communicated to the EDISU offices and documented. EDISU will check that the requirements are still met and will take any measures necessary to revoke benefits. If this is not the case, EDISU reserves the right to take action in relation to the failure to notify. In the event of transfer to another University outside Piedmont or withdrawal from studies after the publication of the definitive rankings and during the academic year, the benefit won or the eligibility condition will be revoked.

items.text: Non-resident students are required to self-certify their paid accommodation to receive the appropriate amount due (art. 5(1.3)), only if they are successful in the final grant ranking. Otherwise, if they are awarded a grant, they will be paid the amount provided for commuter students (art. 5(1.2)).

classification.raw: Non-resident students are required to self-certify their paid accommodation to receive the appropriate amount due (art. 5(1.3)), only if they are successful in the final grant ranking. Otherwise, if they are awarded a grant, they will be paid the amount provided for commuter students (art. 5(1.2)).

items.text: It should be noted that students enrolled in a PhD course or in a specialisation school cannot apply as independent students (art. 6(1.5)) since this case only applies to the ISEE Universitario – not to the ISEE Dottorato. Therefore, all students wishing to apply for the non-resident amount must self-certify the paid basis of their residence, pursuant to art. 8(2).

items.text: 1. the first instalment (equal to 50 \% of the total amount) is disbursed starting from the end of May 2, 0, 2, 6 ; 2. the second instalment (equal to the remaining 50 \% of the total amount) is disbursed starting from the end of June 2026;

items.text: Students receiving a scholarship for an additional semester of a master's degree or single-cycle master's degree and first year of a PhD/specialisation school are granted one instalment for the master's degree and two instalments for the PhD/specialisation school.

classification.raw: Students receiving a scholarship for an additional semester of a master's degree or single-cycle master's degree and first year of a PhD/specialisation school are granted one instalment for the master's degree and two instalments for the PhD/specialisation school.

items.text: The first instalment will be disbursed from the end of May 2026 if students provide their SEPA-circuit IBAN by 10 May 2026. Additional payments are planned to coincide with the disbursement period of the second instalments. If the IBAN is not communicated by 31 December 2027, the instalments will no longer be due.

classification.raw: The first instalment will be disbursed from the end of May 2026 if students provide their SEPA-circuit IBAN by 10 May 2026. Additional payments are planned to coincide with the disbursement period of the second instalments. If the IBAN is not communicated by 31 December 2027, the instalments will no longer be due.

items.text: It should be noted that the disbursement of the instalment means the start of the administrative process, which is concluded the following month with EDISU’s provision of payment to the Credit Institute indicated in the IBAN provided by students. The availability of the sum in the current account may, therefore, take place in the month following the disbursement, based on the timing of the individual credit institutions.

classification.raw: It should be noted that the disbursement of the instalment means the start of the administrative process, which is concluded the following month with EDISU’s provision of payment to the Credit Institute indicated in the IBAN provided by students. The availability of the sum in the current account may, therefore, take place in the month following the disbursement, based on the timing of the individual credit institutions.

items.text: Winners may only receive the scholarship by crediting a current account or prepaid card with an IBAN in their name or jointly in their name. Postal savings books are excluded. For more information on how to communicate the IBAN, please refer to art. 7(3).

Therefore, students who have been awarded scholarships as non-resident students will be paid the non-resident amount if they submit a self-certification of paid accommodation from 20 March to 10 April { 2, 0, 2, 6 } , in accordance with the procedures set out in art. 8(2).

items.text: In addition to the provisions set out in the previous articles, the following specific provisions apply to students with disabilities. For the purposes of the competition, the following students will be considered disabled:

classification.raw: In addition to the provisions set out in the previous articles, the following specific provisions apply to students with disabilities. For the purposes of the competition, the following students will be considered disabled:

items.text: 1. those with a disability of 6, 6 \% or more; 2. those with a disability recognised pursuant to art. 3(1) and/or art. 3(3) of Law 104/199264.

items: 1. those with a disability of 6, 6 \% or more;
2. those with a disability recognised pursuant to art. 3(1) and/or art. 3(3) of Law 104/199264.
classification.raw: 1. those with a disability of 6, 6 \% or more; 2. those with a disability recognised pursuant to art. 3(1) and/or art. 3(3) of Law 104/199264.

items.text: Recognition of learning difficulties does not constitute eligibility under the specific provisions for disabled students.

classification.raw: Recognition of learning difficulties does not constitute eligibility under the specific provisions for disabled students.

items.text: When completing the application form, students must enclose certification attesting to their disability in the appropriate section (indicated in the document “List of certifications to enclose with the application for students with a household composed of one student, holders of international protection, and disabled persons” available in the “Scholarships” > attachments section of the EDISU website).

classification.raw: When completing the application form, students must enclose certification attesting to their disability in the appropriate section (indicated in the document “List of certifications to enclose with the application for students with a household composed of one student, holders of international protection, and disabled persons” available in the “Scholarships” > attachments section of the EDISU website).

items.text: Students who, at the time of submitting their application, do not yet have certification attesting to their disability and international students who have not yet obtained recognition from the Italian SSN (National Health Service), must enclose the certification in the complaint procedure by 12 November 2025. Otherwise, they cannot be considered disabled students for the purposes of the benefits referred to in this article

items.text: Pursuant to art. 3(4) of Ministerial Decree 1320/2021, students with disabilities are allocated a scholarship amount increased by 40 \% for each type of student. This is to allow for the use of prostheses and supports, as well as all measures that facilitate access to education.

This is to allow for the use of prostheses and supports, as well as all measures that facilitate access to education.

items.text: Students with disabilities who apply for the accommodation service are included in the rankings drawn up in accordance with arts. 11(1) (first years), 16(1) (subsequent years), and 26(1) (PhDs and specialisation schools) without place reservations.

classification.raw: Students with disabilities who apply for the accommodation service are included in the rankings drawn up in accordance with arts. 11(1) (first years), 16(1) (subsequent years), and 26(1) (PhDs and specialisation schools) without place reservations.

items.text: Students are required to indicate their legal guardian, if any.

classification.raw: Students are required to indicate their legal guardian, if any.

items.text: When submitting their application, students may request accommodation for a companion. EDISU will assess the appropriateness of assigning accommodation to the companion based on what the SSN certifies on a case-by-case basis.

classification.raw: When submitting their application, students may request accommodation for a companion. EDISU will assess the appropriateness of assigning accommodation to the companion based on what the SSN certifies on a case-by-case basis.

items.text: When accepting accommodation, students must declare the type of disability and needs for residence life.

classification.raw: When accepting accommodation, students must declare the type of disability and needs for residence life.

items.text: A total of 60 spots are reserved in the EDISU Residences, differentiated according to the following types of disability for the purposes of safety and emergency management: self-sufficient (50 spots), non-self-sufficient with companion (10 spots).

classification.raw: A total of 60 spots are reserved in the EDISU Residences, differentiated according to the following types of disability for the purposes of safety and emergency management: self-sufficient (50 spots), non-self-sufficient with companion (10 spots).

items.text: The companion of a student with disability who is not self-sufficient must be present in the facility alongside the student and may not be absent even for work. If they do not certify to EDISU that they have appropriate training in emergency evacuation management, they will be duly trained by EDISU.

classification.raw: The companion of a student with disability who is not self-sufficient must be present in the facility alongside the student and may not be absent even for work. If they do not certify to EDISU that they have appropriate training in emergency evacuation management, they will be duly trained by EDISU.

items.text: No personal services are provided. Students requiring room cleaning or other services must pay the amount directly to the residence hall.

classification.raw: No personal services are provided. Students requiring room cleaning or other services must pay the amount directly to the residence hall.

items.text: Applicants will be contacted by the Residence Department for an informational interview after the application deadline.

classification.raw: Applicants will be contacted by the Residence Department for an informational interview after the application deadline.

items.text: Should the student be unable to find a companion, he or she may not be assigned to an EDISU residence and may be eligible for the scholarship amount including the value of the accommodation service if they certify paid residence in accordance with the procedures and within the deadlines laid down in art. 8(2) above. The student will still have to complete the online acceptance procedure (if a winner) or expression of interest (if eligible) and declare their status via a ticket.

The student will still have to complete the online acceptance procedure (if a winner) or expression of interest (if eligible) and declare their status via a ticket.

items.text: Art. 30(1) INTERNATIONAL STUDENTS WHO DON’T HAVE TO SUBMIT CONSULAR DOCUMENTATION

classification.title: Art. 30 (1) INTERNATIONAL STUDENTS WHO DON’T HAVE TO SUBMIT CONSULAR DOCUMENTATION
classification.raw: Art. 30(1) INTERNATIONAL STUDENTS WHO DON’T HAVE TO SUBMIT CONSULAR DOCUMENTATION

items.text: The following categories of students are NOT required to submit consular documentation:

classification.raw: The following categories of students are NOT required to submit consular documentation:

items.text: 1. International students with their entire household residing in Italy66. When submitting their application, they only need to have a ISEE Universitario (art. 6); please note that in this case, non-EU or EU international students must submit their application in the same way as Italian students (art. 7). 2. Non-EU international students with family fully resident abroad who apply for benefits for a subsequent year of their studies and who in the academic year 2024/25 were found to be beneficiaries or users of EDISU Piemonte benefits/services by submitting new financial documentation or confirming their financial data for the academic year 2023/24 or the academic year 2022/23. This type of student can confirm the financial data of previous years. It should be noted that the confirmation of the financial data presented in the academic year 2022/23 is only granted in the case of enrolment in the additional semester of one's degree programme. In addition, the financial data cannot be confirmed if, in the academic year 2024/25, an application was submitted as an independent student. If the family, income, or asset situation of students has changed, it will not be possible to make use of the data confirmation. 3. Stateless or internationally protected students (see section 5 below for more information).

items.text: Art. 30(3) CONSULAR DOCUMENTATION

classification.title: Art. 30 (3) CONSULAR DOCUMENTATION
classification.raw: Art. 30(3) CONSULAR DOCUMENTATION

items.text: Please note: the intervention of agencies or intermediaries for obtaining, legalising, and translating the required documentation is not necessary. Simply request documentation from the authorities in your country that complies with the guidelines listed below. EDISU urges people to be wary of offers to provide help in exchange for money as these can constitute a serious risk of deception. Please note that EDISU carries out thorough checks on all the documentation submitted and, if it proves to be falsified, will proceed with exclusion from benefits (or withdrawal, if these have already been paid) as well as reporting the offence to the competent authorities.

classification.raw: Please note: the intervention of agencies or intermediaries for obtaining, legalising, and translating the required documentation is not necessary. Simply request documentation from the authorities in your country that complies with the guidelines listed below. EDISU urges people to be wary of offers to provide help in exchange for money as these can constitute a serious risk of deception. Please note that EDISU carries out thorough checks on all the documentation submitted and, if it proves to be falsified, will proceed with exclusion from benefits (or withdrawal, if these have already been paid) as well as reporting the offence to the competent authorities.

items.text: Students are also invited to read Annex E, which provides more information on the indispensable requirements of financial documentation and special cases and which forms an integral part of the notice of competition.

items.text: The required consular documentation consists of certificates or declarations in originals relating to:

classification.raw: The required consular documentation consists of certificates or declarations in originals relating to:

items.text: the composition of the household at the time of students’ applications for benefits, including the first name, surname and date of birth of parents, brothers, sisters and others living in the same household as the students.

classification.raw: the composition of the household at the time of students’ applications for benefits, including the first name, surname and date of birth of parents, brothers, sisters and others living in the same household as the students.

items.text: Information on both parents must always be provided.

classification.raw: Information on both parents must always be provided.

items.text: In the case of married students, the household consists of the student, spouse, children and any other people living with them. Parents of spouses are to be included in the student’s household only if they still reside with the student or if, although their residence is different, neither the student nor their spouse meet the requirements for independent status set out in section 5 below.

classification.raw: In the case of married students, the household consists of the student, spouse, children and any other people living with them. Parents of spouses are to be included in the student’s household only if they still reside with the student or if, although their residence is different, neither the student nor their spouse meet the requirements for independent status set out in section 5 below.

items.text: 2. the total gross annual income produced abroad by each individual member of the household aged 18 or over on 1 January 2025, including any siblings and the applicant for benefits. See Annex E for the exchange rate applied.

classification.raw: 2. the total gross annual income produced abroad by each individual member of the household aged 18 or over on 1 January 2025, including any siblings and the applicant for benefits. See Annex E for the exchange rate applied.

items.text: The documentation referred to above must be produced and certified by the competent authorities in the student’s own country: declarations, in the form of self-certification or affidavits issued on the basis of original documentation produced and submitted by students, or on the basis of simple declarations made by students or other members of the household, will in no way be accepted, with the exception of certificates proving the absence of movable assets, as in point 3 above.

items.text: In the event of the loss or theft of consular documentation on arrival in Italy, students must present a copy of the report made to the competent Italian authority, a copy of their passport, visa or residence permit and a copy of the consular documentation transmitted; only in this case will they be able to submit the new documentation during a complaint for the benefit.

items.text: Students coming from particularly poor countries must submit, as an alternative to the documentation indicated in paragraph 3 of this article, a certification from the Italian Representation in the country of origin stating that the students do not belong to a family known to have a high income and social standing69.

classification.raw: Students coming from particularly poor countries must submit, as an alternative to the documentation indicated in paragraph 3 of this article, a certification from the Italian Representation in the country of origin stating that the students do not belong to a family known to have a high income and social standing69.

items.text: No scholarships or accommodation are reserved for these students, apart from the 1, 5 \% reservation for non-EU students enrolled in the first years (arts. 11 and 33).

items.text: The list of particularly poor countries, established by M.U.R. (Ministry of Education, University and Research) Decree No. 440/2024, can be found in table D at the bottom of the notice of competition70.

items.text: Art. 30(5) STUDENTS HOLDING INTERNATIONAL PROTECTION AND STATELESS PERSONS

classification.title: Art. 30 (5) STUDENTS HOLDING INTERNATIONAL PROTECTION AND STATELESS PERSONS
classification.raw: Art. 30(5) STUDENTS HOLDING INTERNATIONAL PROTECTION AND STATELESS PERSONS

items.text: Students that have international protection must present, as an alternative to the documentation indicated in section 3 of this article, their residence permit indicating the type of international protection.

classification.raw: Students that have international protection must present, as an alternative to the documentation indicated in section 3 of this article, their residence permit indicating the type of international protection.

items.text: If the residence permit has not yet been issued, the “attestato nominativo” (name and data certificate) that may be issued by the Questura or the order issued by the competent Regional Commission in which the international protection status is recognised may be presented.

classification.raw: If the residence permit has not yet been issued, the “attestato nominativo” (name and data certificate) that may be issued by the Questura or the order issued by the competent Regional Commission in which the international protection status is recognised may be presented.

items.text: In the event of a refusal to recognise international protection (with the exception of cases where the application is rejected on the grounds that it is manifestly unfounded), students will be asked to produce appropriate documentation attesting to the lodging of a complaint against this decision and, if necessary, a copy of the residence permit of limited duration issued pending the decision of the Court. In this case, the students will then be required, if their complaint is successful, to submit a copy of the residence permit issued to them showing their recognised international protection status, by opening an “ISEE financial requirements” section ticket.

classification.raw: In the event of a refusal to recognise international protection (with the exception of cases where the application is rejected on the grounds that it is manifestly unfounded), students will be asked to produce appropriate documentation attesting to the lodging of a complaint against this decision and, if necessary, a copy of the residence permit of limited duration issued pending the decision of the Court. In this case, the students will then be required, if their complaint is successful, to submit a copy of the residence permit issued to them showing their recognised international protection status, by opening an “ISEE financial requirements” section ticket.

items.text: Stateless students must submit, as an alternative to the documentation indicated in paragraph 3 of this article, a copy of the residence permit attesting to their “stateless status” or, if they do not yet have this, a certified copy of the judgment declaring their statelessness.

classification.raw: Stateless students must submit, as an alternative to the documentation indicated in paragraph 3 of this article, a copy of the residence permit attesting to their “stateless status” or, if they do not yet have this, a certified copy of the judgment declaring their statelessness.

items.text: Students under international protection and stateless persons must have a ISEE Universitario as regulated by art. 6(1.1) of this notice of competition71.

classification.raw: Students under international protection and stateless persons must have a ISEE Universitario as regulated by art. 6(1.1) of this notice of competition71.

items.text: Students from Ukraine holding a temporary residence permit do NOT have to submit the ISEE Universitario or any consular documents relating to their family of origin. They are only required to send the documents relating to the part of the family present in Italy with whom they may reside to EDISU Piemonte according to the methods identified in art. 31(1) of this Notice of competition. These documents include: the identity document and copy of the residence permit of all the family members and any current account balance, including average balance as of 31 December 2023, for the processing of the ISEE Parificato by the EDISU Piemonte CAAF operators.

items.text: Internationally protected and stateless students with their registered residence in the same municipality as the course or in municipalities other than the one in which the course is based, which can be reached by public transport in less than 60 minutes, in accordance with art. 4, are in any case considered non-resident. Therefore, they can apply for the accommodation service and, by self-certifying their paid accommodation (art. 8), they can compete for the scholarship amounts referred to in art. 5(1.3). This provision does not apply to internationally protected and stateless students who have the same residency as members of their family of origin (family reunification) if this residency is in the same municipality as the course or in municipalities other than that where the course is located that may be reached by public transport in less than 60 minutes

classification.raw: In the case of a household residing partly in Italy and partly abroad, students must have an ordinary ISEE, which must be submitted together with the consular documentation in accordance with the procedures and within the deadline for requesting the benefit.

items.text: Please note that confirmation of financial data is NOT allowed under any circumstances for the part of the household residing in Italy. Therefore, students enrolled in a subsequent year who can confirm their financial data and whose household is partly resident in Italy and partly abroad, must submit an ordinary ISEE 2025 certificate relating to the income and assets of household members resident in Italy, which will be supplemented with the financial and asset documents already in EDISU Piemonte's archives.

classification.raw: Please note that confirmation of financial data is NOT allowed under any circumstances for the part of the household residing in Italy. Therefore, students enrolled in a subsequent year who can confirm their financial data and whose household is partly resident in Italy and partly abroad, must submit an ordinary ISEE 2025 certificate relating to the income and assets of household members resident in Italy, which will be supplemented with the financial and asset documents already in EDISU Piemonte's archives.

items.text: In the case of multiple citizenships, the benefit application methods are identified on the basis of the citizenship chosen at the time of enrolment at the Piedmont University. Students must therefore apply for EDISU benefits using the same citizenship registered with their Piedmont University.

classification.raw: In the case of multiple citizenships, the benefit application methods are identified on the basis of the citizenship chosen at the time of enrolment at the Piedmont University. Students must therefore apply for EDISU benefits using the same citizenship registered with their Piedmont University.

items.text: If the part of the household residing in Italy has a member who generates income and/or possesses assets, in the online procedure, non- EU or EU international students must indicate “family in Italy”. The type of scholarship will be awarded according to the registered residence that the students assumed with the part of their family in Italy. Please consult art. 4 of this notice of competition for more information on the type of scholarship that may be awarded to international students with part of their family in Italy.

classification.raw: If the part of the household residing in Italy has a member who generates income and/or possesses assets, in the online procedure, non- EU or EU international students must indicate “family in Italy”. items.text: Art. 30(7) INTERNATIONAL EU STUDENTS

classification.title: Art. 30 (7) INTERNATIONAL EU STUDENTS
classification.raw: Art. 30(7) INTERNATIONAL EU STUDENTS

items.text: International EU students must submit the online application by enclosing translated and legalised documentation in accordance with current legislation72 regarding the composition of the household, income received in the 2023 tax year, and real estate and movable assets, including average assets, as at 31 December 2023.

classification.raw: International EU students must submit the online application by enclosing translated and legalised documentation in accordance with current legislation72 regarding the composition of the household, income received in the 2023 tax year, and real estate and movable assets, including average assets, as at 31 December 2023.

items.text: The correctness of the documentation attached to the application for benefits will be assessed by EDISU Piemonte and, through their personal Online Helpdesk page, students will be able to view the outcome after the publication of the provisional rankings. If EDISU finds that the documentation is incomplete or erroneous, students may rectify or supplement what has been sent by submitting a formal complaint, as governed by art. 7(6.1) and art. 31(3.2) of this notice of competition.

classification.raw: The correctness of the documentation attached to the application for benefits will be assessed by EDISU Piemonte and, through their personal Online Helpdesk page, students will be able to view the outcome after the publication of the provisional rankings. classification.raw: Art. 31(1) INTERNATIONAL NON-EU STUDENTS

items.text: Non-EU international students must submit benefits applications using the methods described in the following paragraphs, each of which corresponds to a step in the procedure.

classification.raw: Non-EU international students must submit benefits applications using the methods described in the following paragraphs, each of which corresponds to a step in the procedure.

items.text: Art. 31(1.1) OBTAINING CREDENTIALS TO ACCESS THE ONLINE APPLICATION

classification.title: Art. 31 (1.1) OBTAINING CREDENTIALS TO ACCESS THE ONLINE APPLICATION
classification.raw: Art. 31(1.1) OBTAINING CREDENTIALS TO ACCESS THE ONLINE APPLICATION

items.text: 1. Students enrolled in the first years with an entry visa to Italy for academic year 2025/26: students in this category must calculate their tax code if they do not already have an official one. In the online registration procedure on the EDISU website, students will find information on how to calculate their tax code. After calculating the tax code, they have to register on the EDISU website by requesting a Temporary Access Code 2025/26. 2. Students already residing in Italy but without registered residence in Italy: students belonging to this category (enrolled in both first years and subsequent years) must register on the EDISU website by requesting a Temporary Access Code 2025/26. 3. Students already residing in Italy and with registered residence in Italy: students belonging to this category (enrolled in both first years and subsequent years) must have their SPID or CIE credentials to access the EDISU website.

After calculating the tax code, they have to register on the EDISU website by requesting a Temporary Access Code 2025/

2. Students already residing in Italy but without registered residence in Italy: students belonging to this category (enrolled in both first years and subsequent years) must register on the EDISU website by requesting a Temporary Access Code 2025/

3. Students already residing in Italy and with registered residence in Italy: students belonging to this category (enrolled in both first years and subsequent years) must have their SPID or CIE credentials to access the EDISU website.

items.text: Following the transmission of the online application (art. 31(1.2)), students referred to in art. 30(2) must send the following documents to EDISU Piemonte by registered mail with return receipt or courier with proof of delivery:

classification.raw: Following the transmission of the online application (art. 31(1.2)), students referred to in art. 30(2) must send the following documents to EDISU Piemonte by registered mail with return receipt or courier with proof of delivery:

items.text: a. Consular documentation (both the original language version and the version translated into Italian) relating to the composition of the household, income and assets produced/possessed abroad by the student's household (art. 30(3) and Annex E); b. Copy of the Official tax code issued by the Revenue Agency or the Italian Embassy in the country of origin; c. For the sole purpose of file assignment, a copy of a valid ID of the student is required. The Smart Card issued by universities is not an identification document. d. Students who are minors must also enclose the authorisation form signed by a parent or legal guardian. The form can be downloaded from the documentation section of www.edisu.piemonte.it.

items.text: Consular documentation sent to EDISU Piemonte must be in original. Exceptions are marriage, divorce/legal separation or death certificates, as well as documents relating to the presence of movable assets, which may be presented as photocopies; EDISU Piemonte is in no way responsible for any postal or international courier delays and cannot waive the deadlines indicated in this notice of competition; If workers cannot match the documentation to the applicant's application record due to the absence of an identity document or the absence of surname and first name on the envelope, the file will not be assessed; Each student may only send their own documentation. Joint shipments of parcels containing the documentation of several students are not accepted except in cases where two or more students are sisters or brothers It is in no way possible to return the original documentation received in EDISU Piemonte's offices unless a formal request to renounce benefits is submitted.

items.text: A) Students enrolled in the first years of a bachelor’s degree, master's degree or single-cycle master's degree:
