items.text: Documentation must be received by EDISU by 9 September 2025 to apply for the scholarship and accommodation service. If students do not deliver the documentation by 9 September 2024, they will be excluded from the provisional accommodation service rankings but will be able to re-enter the final accommodation service rankings if they deliver the required documentation by 22 September 2025.

classification.raw: Documentation must be received by EDISU by 9 September 2025 to apply for the scholarship and accommodation service. If students do not deliver the documentation by 9 September 2024, they will be excluded from the provisional accommodation service rankings but will be able to re-enter the final accommodation service rankings if they deliver the required documentation by 22 September 2025.

items.text: Documentation must be received by EDISU by 25 September 2025 to apply for the scholarship alone. If students do not deliver the documentation by 25 September 2025, they will be excluded from the provisional rankings but will be able to re-enter the final scholarship ranking if they deliver the required documentation by 12 November 2025.

classification.raw: Documentation must be received by EDISU by 25 September 2025 to apply for the scholarship alone. If students do not deliver the documentation by 25 September 2025, they will be excluded from the provisional rankings but will be able to re-enter the final scholarship ranking if they deliver the required documentation by 12 November 2025.

items.text: The evaluation of documentation not received by 9 September 2025 (application for scholarship and accommodation service) or 25 September 2025 (application for a scholarship only) will not be published in the provisional ranking but, if the documentation is received by the deadline for the submission of complaints, it will be published directly in the definitive ranking, without any further possibility of complaint. Therefore, students are invited to submit the required documentation in advance of the deadline to be able to have a provisional outcome and rectify any shortcomings.

classification.raw: The evaluation of documentation not received by 9 September 2025 (application for scholarship and accommodation service) or 25 September 2025 (application for a scholarship only) will not be published in the provisional ranking but, if the documentation is received by the deadline for the submission of complaints, it will be published directly in the definitive ranking, without any further possibility of complaint. Therefore, students are invited to submit the required documentation in advance of the deadline to be able to have a provisional outcome and rectify any shortcomings.

items.text: Students enrolled in a first year of a bachelor’s degree or single-cycle master’s degree who submit their documentation by 29 October 2025 at 12:00 p.m. may also be included in the final scholarship rankings of 7 November 2024 if they are duly enrolled at the University on 31 October 2025.

classification.raw: Students enrolled in a first year of a bachelor’s degree or single-cycle master’s degree who submit their documentation by 29 October 2025 at 12:00 p.m. may also be included in the final scholarship rankings of 7 November 2024 if they are duly enrolled at the University on 31 October 2025.

items.text: If the consular documentation is still incomplete or non-compliant, students will view the negative assessment via their personal online helpdesk page and may supplement the missing/non-compliant documentation by 12 November 2025 to be included in the definitive rankings on 16 December 2025.

items.text: Visa delay

classification.title: Visa delay
classification.raw: Visa delay

items.text: If students enrolled in the first year who require the accommodation service need a study visa to enter Italy for enrolment in the academic year 2025/26, they will have to declare this through the appropriate procedure when filling out the application for benefits. In the event of a delay in the visa itself and subsequent delivery of the documentation after 22 September 2025, they will be excluded from the final accommodation service ranking of 26 September 2025. However, they may be included in the definitive rankings of 16 December 2025 and in the “single scrolling ranking for accommodation service” published on 16 December 2025, in order of ISEE Parificato, if they submit all consular documentation by 12 November 2025 and enrol in the University by 30 November 2025.

classification.raw: If students enrolled in the first year who require the accommodation service need a study visa to enter Italy for enrolment in the academic year 2025/26, they will have to declare this through the appropriate procedure when filling out the application for benefits. In the event of a delay in the visa itself and subsequent delivery of the documentation after 22 September 2025, they will be excluded from the final accommodation service ranking of 26 September 2025. However, they may be included in the definitive rankings of 16 December 2025 and in the “single scrolling ranking for accommodation service” published on 16 December 2025, in order of ISEE Parificato, if they submit all consular documentation by 12 November 2025 and enrol in the University by 30 November 2025.

items.text: Students suspended in the rankings of 16 December 2025 who will complete registration by 3 April 2026 and will be included in the rankings of 30 April 2026, may be included in a further ranking list for the housing service which will be published on 30 April 2026.

classification.raw: Students suspended in the rankings of 16 December 2025 who will complete registration by 3 April 2026 and will be included in the rankings of 30 April 2026, may be included in a further ranking list for the housing service which will be published on 30 April 2026.

items.text: Students who do not complete their enrolment by 30 November 2025 may also benefit from the extension of the deadline for enrolment only at the university as set out in art. 11(2). However, the required documents must still be delivered by the deadlines indicated above. If the documentation is not received by that date, students will be definitively excluded from the definitive rankings.

classification.raw: 3. Step 3: submission of consular documentation via the upload procedure available when completing the online application Students must attach, via the online procedure, translated and legalised consular documentation relating to the composition, income, and assets of the student's household (art. 30 (3) and (7)) by the deadline for submitting the application for the benefit they intend to apply for. Both the documentation in the original language and the translation into Italian must be attached.

items.text: The procedure for uploading the required documentation involves uploading a maximum of 5 attachments in .pdf version, each no larger than 1MB. Students must combine the different types of document in a single file, e.g. a single file containing the composition of the household and any divorce decrees or death certificates, another containing all the income received by the household, and one relating to movable assets (current accounts, savings, deposits, etc.) and real estate (only assessment of buildings expressed in square metres).

items.text: Once the consular documentation arrives at the offices, students will receive a receipt of delivery73; the consular documentation will then be assessed by EDISU Piemonte workers and students will be able to consult the assessment at the same time as the publication of the provisional rankings on their personal Online Helpdesk page. items.text: Students who have been successful in the final accommodation service ranking must proceed to “acceptance”. Those who are eligible in the final accommodation service ranking must proceed to “expression of interest” by filling in the online procedures that will be activated at the same time, according to the procedures and within the deadlines indicated in paragraphs 1 and 2 of this article. Students enrolled in doctoral courses or specialisation schools, on the other hand, must refer to the provisions set out in art. 28.

Students enrolled in doctoral courses or specialisation schools, on the other hand, must refer to the provisions set out in art. 28.

items.text: Students who have been awarded scholarships and are eligible for the accommodation service because they attend course venues that do not have EDISU residences do not have to accept online (paragraph 1 of this article) or declare their interest (paragraph 2 of this article). To receive the grant amount inclusive of the monetised value of the unused accommodation spot (art. 5(1.3)), they must self-certify their paid accommodation, in accordance with the methods and time frames indicated in art. 8(2).

items.text: 1. the position of students; 2. the option chosen by the students in the expression of interest; 3. the result of the allocation. For students who have declared “I am interested” or “I am interested with the Erasmus Month” Option, the allocation can take place at the department, course type, or University level. Initially, the spots remaining after the acceptance of the winners are redistributed to eligible students of the same department. After this distribution, any places left over are redistributed among all departments at the same course level among the students still to be allocated. Any spots still left over are redistributed within the University and, finally, if there are additional seats to be allocated, a final distribution is made based on the course location only.

items.text: At the same time as the publication of the expression of interest Outcomes, on 2 October 2025, new rankings will be published for eligible students who declared an interest but were not allocated accommodation in the expression of interest outcomes. These rankings are referred to as “Single Scrolling Rankings” and are drawn up as follows:

classification.raw: At the same time as the publication of the expression of interest Outcomes, on 2 October 2025, new rankings will be published for eligible students who declared an interest but were not allocated accommodation in the expression of interest outcomes. These rankings are referred to as “Single Scrolling Rankings” and are drawn up as follows:

items.text: 1. for the first years, divided between EU/Non-EU students and course location, then ordered by increasing ISEE Universitario (or ISEE Parificato); 2. for subsequent years, divided by course location, then ordered by decreasing score.

items.text: In the case of winners in the final scholarship rankings:

classification.raw: In the case of winners in the final scholarship rankings:

items.text: 1. If the accommodation service is accepted before the publication of the final grant rankings (16 December 2025), the value of the accommodation service calculated at € 245.45 per month (the month is counted if students enter the residence up to the 15th day of the month) will be deducted from the grant amount including the monetised value of the accommodation (art. 5(1.3)). 2. If the accommodation service is accepted after the publication of the final grant rankings (16 December 2025): if students have self-certified the cost of accommodation (art. 8(2)), the deduction relating to the value of the accommodation service actually used will be made from the second grant instalment; if students have not self-certified the cost of accommodation, they will receive the commuter grant amount (art. 5(1.2)) but are still entitled to use the accommodation free of charge.

items.text: 1. do not have to self-certify their paid accommodation; 2. only if they are grant winners, they receive a total amount supplemented with the monetised value of the EDISU place not used (art. 5(1.3)).

items: 1. do not have to self-certify their paid accommodation;
2. only if they are grant winners, they receive a total amount supplemented with the monetised value of the EDISU place not used (art. 5(1.3)).
classification.raw: 1. do not have to self-certify their paid accommodation; 2. only if they are grant winners, they receive a total amount supplemented with the monetised value of the EDISU place not used (art. 5(1.3)).

items.text: Guest students at the Renato Einaudi College with an INPS grant o or other scholarships type of accommodation service scholarship are not eligible for this option.

classification.raw: Guest students at the Renato Einaudi College with an INPS grant o or other scholarships type of accommodation service scholarship are not eligible for this option.

items.text: ART. 34 ALLOCATION OF ACCOMMODATION AT EDISU RESIDENCES

classification.title: Art. 34 ALLOCATION OF ACCOMMODATION AT EDISU RESIDENCES
classification.raw: ART. 34 ALLOCATION OF ACCOMMODATION AT EDISU RESIDENCES

items.text: Accommodation allocation consists of the right to use one of the places on offer at university residences located in the region. Allocation does not amount to a student's choice of the type of accommodation but consists solely of the right to use the service.

classification.raw: Accommodation allocation consists of the right to use one of the places on offer at university residences located in the region. Allocation does not amount to a student's choice of the type of accommodation but consists solely of the right to use the service.

items.text: Art. 34(1) ALLOCATION CRITERIA

classification.title: Art. 34 (1) ALLOCATION CRITERIA
classification.raw: Art. 34(1) ALLOCATION CRITERIA

items.text: Residencies are allocated in the following order of priority:

classification.raw: Residencies are allocated in the following order of priority:

items.text: • For confirmations by merit in subsequent years and for winners by standard application in subsequent years, the accommodation is allocated based on a single ranking drawn up solely in order of points, taking into account the order of preference expressed at the time of acceptance; • For confirmations by merit in the first year of a master’s degree, the accommodation is allocated on the basis of a single ranking in order of ISEE Universitario/Parificato, taking into account the order of preference expressed at the time of acceptance; • For first-year bachelor’s, master's, and single-cycle master’s degree winners by standard application, divided between EU and non-EU students, the accommodation will be allocated based on a single ranking in order of ISEE Universitario/Parificato, taking into account the order of preference expressed at the time of acceptance.

items: • For confirmations by merit in subsequent years and for winners by standard application in subsequent years, the accommodation is allocated based on a single ranking drawn up solely in order of points, taking into account the order of preference expressed at the time of acceptance;

• For confirmations by merit in the first year of a master’s degree, the accommodation is allocated on the basis of a single ranking in order of ISEE Universitario/Parificato, taking into account the order of preference expressed at the time of acceptance;

• For first-year bachelor’s, master's, and single-cycle master’s degree winners by standard application, divided between EU and non-EU students, the accommodation will be allocated based on a single ranking in order of ISEE Universitario/Parificato, taking into account the order of preference expressed at the time of acceptance.
classification.raw: • For confirmations by merit in subsequent years and for winners by standard application in subsequent years, the accommodation is allocated based on a single ranking drawn up solely in order of points, taking into account the order of preference expressed at the time of acceptance; • For confirmations by merit in the first year of a master’s degree, the accommodation is allocated on the basis of a single ranking in order of ISEE Universitario/Parificato, taking into account the order of preference expressed at the time of acceptance; • For first-year bachelor’s, master's, and single-cycle master’s degree winners by standard application, divided between EU and non-EU students, the accommodation will be allocated based on a single ranking in order of ISEE Universitario/Parificato, taking into account the order of preference expressed at the time of acceptance.

items.text: If students occupy the same position in the ranking, preference is given to those already allocated a place in 2024/25.

classification.raw: If students occupy the same position in the ranking, preference is given to those already allocated a place in 2024/25.

items.text: After the winners’ allocation, the allocation relating to eligible candidates, who are assigned accommodation in the expression of interest, is carried out in the same way as for the winners.

classification.raw: After the winners’ allocation, the allocation relating to eligible candidates, who are assigned accommodation in the expression of interest, is carried out in the same way as for the winners.

items.text: The allocation criteria are published under Housing > Competitive Accommodation Service on the EDISU website.

classification.raw: The allocation criteria are published under Housing > Competitive Accommodation Service on the EDISU website.

items.text: Students winners of the final rankings of 26 September who refuse the allocation, or do not turn up at the residence on the dates indicated in the allocation e-mail at the time of allocation, will be considered “commuter” students for the purposes of the scholarship amount (art. 5(1.2)).

classification.raw: Students winners of the final rankings of 26 September who refuse the allocation, or do not turn up at the residence on the dates indicated in the allocation e-mail at the time of allocation, will be considered “commuter” students for the purposes of the scholarship amount (art. 5(1.2)).

items.text: Students who are eligible in the final accommodation service ranking but state that they are not interested in the interest declaration step or do not submit the interest declaration procedure art. 33(2) or give up the place attributed in the “interest declaration outcomes” step (give up the place when the residence is assigned or fail to turn up at the assigned residence on time) can compete for the scholarship amount as a non-resident, by submitting the paid housing declaration in the manner and by the deadline set out in art. 8. If, on the other hand, they give up their place after checking in, they can no longer submit the paid accommodation declaration and only contribute to the commuter scholarship amount.

classification.raw: Students who are eligible in the final accommodation service ranking but state that they are not interested in the interest declaration step or do not submit the interest declaration procedure art. 33(2) or give up the place attributed in the “interest declaration outcomes” step (give up the place when the residence is assigned or fail to turn up at the assigned residence on time) can compete for the scholarship amount as a non-resident, by submitting the paid housing declaration in the manner and by the deadline set out in art. 8. If, on the other hand, they give up their place after checking in, they can no longer submit the paid accommodation declaration and only contribute to the commuter scholarship amount.

items.text: Justified extensions may be requested on entry to the Residence. The request for an extension of the entry into a residence must be submitted by sending the extension form available on the website to assegnazioniresidenze@edisu-piemonte.it.

classification.raw: Justified extensions may be requested on entry to the Residence. The request for an extension of the entry into a residence must be submitted by sending the extension form available on the website to assegnazioniresidenze@edisu-piemonte.it.

items.text: The request for an extension will be considered as an acceptance of the allocation, starting from the last day for entry into the Residence. Therefore, if students do not present themselves at the residence at the end of the extension period, they will be deemed to have withdrawn and will have to pay the value of the accommodation service, calculated at \notin 8 . 1, 8 per day, for the period for which they have requested the extension.

classification.raw: The request for an extension will be considered as an acceptance of the allocation, starting from the last day for entry into the Residence. Therefore, if students do not present themselves at the residence at the end of the extension period, they will be deemed to have withdrawn and will have to pay the value of the accommodation service, calculated at \notin 8 . 1, 8 per day, for the period for which they have requested the extension.

items.text: Should students requesting an extension renounce during the course of the year, they will have to pay the value of the accommodation service, calculated at \notin 8 . 1, 8 per day, from the allocation in residence, and not from the check-in date.

classification.raw: Should students requesting an extension renounce during the course of the year, they will have to pay the value of the accommodation service, calculated at \notin 8 . 1, 8 per day, from the allocation in residence, and not from the check-in date.

items.text: Students who have requested an entry extension have the accommodation kept for them until 31 December 2025 exclusively for the following situations:

classification.raw: Students who have requested an entry extension have the accommodation kept for them until 31 December 2025 exclusively for the following situations:

items.text: Students enrolled in master's degree courses for the academic year 2025/26 awaiting a bachelor's degree at non-Piedmont Universities or at a university city in Piedmont other than the city of residence of the allocation; 2. Students with documented health problems that prevent them from entering within the time limit set out in the allocation e- mail; 3. Students who document their participation in training activities (courses, internships, placements, etc.) in a city other than the city of residence of the allocation; 4. International first-year students who are unable to arrive in Italy by the date of allocation of residence (art. 31).

items: Students enrolled in master's degree courses for the academic year 2025/26 awaiting a bachelor's degree at non-Piedmont Universities or at a university city in Piedmont other than the city of residence of the allocation;
2. Students with documented health problems that prevent them from entering within the time limit set out in the allocation e- mail;
3. Students who document their participation in training activities (courses, internships, placements, etc.) in a city other than the city of residence of the allocation;
4. International first-year students who are unable to arrive in Italy by the date of allocation of residence (art. 31).
classification.raw: Students enrolled in master's degree courses for the academic year 2025/26 awaiting a bachelor's degree at non-Piedmont Universities or at a university city in Piedmont other than the city of residence of the allocation; 2. Students with documented health problems that prevent them from entering within the time limit set out in the allocation e- mail; 3. Students who document their participation in training activities (courses, internships, placements, etc.) in a city other than the city of residence of the allocation; 4. International first-year students who are unable to arrive in Italy by the date of allocation of residence (art. 31).

items.text: Even if the accommodation is kept available until 31 December 2025, the amount deducted from the scholarship will be counted from the last day provided in the allocation email and not from check-in.

classification.raw: Even if the accommodation is kept available until 31 December 2025, the amount deducted from the scholarship will be counted from the last day provided in the allocation email and not from check-in.

items.text: Students applying for a first-year of a bachelor’s or master’s degree for the Turin/Grugliasco site will only be housed in the Borsellino, Cappel Verde, Cavour, Claretta, Olimpia, Mollino, Codegone, Cstudio, Sanpaolo, Campus x, Moi, Liborio, Lingotto, Paoli and Verdi residences and for the Novara site in the Castalia residence, as they are equipped with a security service.

classification.raw: Students applying for a first-year of a bachelor’s or master’s degree for the Turin/Grugliasco site will only be housed in the Borsellino, Cappel Verde, Cavour, Claretta, Olimpia, Mollino, Codegone, Cstudio, Sanpaolo, Campus x, Moi, Liborio, Lingotto, Paoli and Verdi residences and for the Novara site in the Castalia residence, as they are equipped with a security service.

items.text: Students participating in foreign study/exchange projects will be accommodated at the facilities indicated by the allocation criteria that will be published prior to the accommodation acceptance period.

classification.raw: Students participating in foreign study/exchange projects will be accommodated at the facilities indicated by the allocation criteria that will be published prior to the accommodation acceptance period.

items.text: The room type (single, double, triple) is allocated based on academic seniority, in order of ranking, subject to available places, and according to the allocation criteria that will be published prior to the period of accommodation acceptance, which will take into account suitable accommodation solutions for students with special documented needs or over 30 years of age.

classification.raw: The room type (single, double, triple) is allocated based on academic seniority, in order of ranking, subject to available places, and according to the allocation criteria that will be published prior to the period of accommodation acceptance, which will take into account suitable accommodation solutions for students with special documented needs or over 30 years of age.

items.text: Single rooms that become vacant during the year, following the attainment of qualifications or renunciation/withdrawal of the accommodation service, will be allocated firstly to students already in the residence in order of academic seniority and ranking, upon request, then to eligible students not called up.

classification.raw: Single rooms that become vacant during the year, following the attainment of qualifications or renunciation/withdrawal of the accommodation service, will be allocated firstly to students already in the residence in order of academic seniority and ranking, upon request, then to eligible students not called up.

items.text: Art. 34(1.2) TRANSFERS FOR HEALTH OR PERSONAL SAFETY REASONS

classification.title: Art. 34 (1.2) TRANSFERS FOR HEALTH OR PERSONAL SAFETY REASONS
classification.raw: Art. 34(1.2) TRANSFERS FOR HEALTH OR PERSONAL SAFETY REASONS

items.text: Students can request transfer to another residence for documented health reasons or for personal safety, which EDISU reserves the right to assess, according to the allocation criteria that will be published prior to the period of accommodation acceptance.

classification.raw: Students can request transfer to another residence for documented health reasons or for personal safety, which EDISU reserves the right to assess, according to the allocation criteria that will be published prior to the period of accommodation acceptance.

items.text: Students enrolled in the first year of a master’s degree and subsequent years may request a transfer to any residence, while those enrolled in a first year of a bachelor's degree or single-cycle master's degree may only request a transfer to another residence with a security service.

classification.raw: Students enrolled in the first year of a master’s degree and subsequent years may request a transfer to any residence, while those enrolled in a first year of a bachelor's degree or single-cycle master's degree may only request a transfer to another residence with a security service.

items.text: Art. 34(1.3) MOBILITY BETWEEN DIFFERENT RESIDENCES

classification.title: Art. 34 (1.3) MOBILITY BETWEEN DIFFERENT RESIDENCES
classification.raw: Art. 34(1.3) MOBILITY BETWEEN DIFFERENT RESIDENCES

items.text: Transfers between residences will be permitted throughout the academic year. Such swaps take place during the periods (mobility windows) indicated in the relevant calendar and in accordance with the procedures set out in the relevant regulations, which will be made known as of 1 October 2025 by publication on the website www.edisu.piemonte.it

classification.raw: Transfers between residences will be permitted throughout the academic year. Such swaps take place during the periods (mobility windows) indicated in the relevant calendar and in accordance with the procedures set out in the relevant regulations, which will be made known as of 1 October 2025 by publication on the website www.edisu.piemonte.it

items.text: Users interested in the periods (mobility windows) indicated in the relevant calendar will be able to make the request via their personal profile on the online portal.

classification.raw: Users interested in the periods (mobility windows) indicated in the relevant calendar will be able to make the request via their personal profile on the online portal.

items.text: Art. 34(2) ALLOCATION PROCEDURE

classification.title: Art. 34 (2) ALLOCATION PROCEDURE
classification.raw: Art. 34(2) ALLOCATION PROCEDURE

items.text: Students who have accepted accommodation will receive an e-mail from noreply@EDISU-piemonte.it with details of the allocation. Students must present themselves at the residence for allocation by the deadline indicated in the e-mail provided with:

classification.raw: Students who have accepted accommodation will receive an e-mail from noreply@EDISU-piemonte.it with details of the allocation. Students must present themselves at the residence for allocation by the deadline indicated in the e-mail provided with:

items.text: 1. a valid identity document (for non-EU students, the identity document must be a passport); 2. residence permit (or copy of the receipt of payment for the residence permit) for non-EU students; 3. passport photo; 4. copy of registration with the National Health Service (health card) or copy of a private insurance policy for non-EU students76.

items.text: Art. 34(3) RULES FOR ASSIGNEES

classification.title: Art. 34 (3) RULES FOR ASSIGNEES
classification.raw: Art. 34(3) RULES FOR ASSIGNEES

items.text: By accepting the place, students undertake to comply with the provisions of this notice and the Internal Regulations for University Residences. The following rules shall also be applied:

classification.raw: By accepting the place, students undertake to comply with the provisions of this notice and the Internal Regulations for University Residences. The following rules shall also be applied:

items.text: Students will not be granted accommodation if they have not paid their outstanding fees of any kind for the previous academic years (EDISU has the right to take action to recover these sums) or if they have incurred a revocation of their benefits as provided for in the internal residence regulations. 2. Students may not enter the residence until they have completed the procedure relating to the acceptance and allocation of the accommodation and handed in the documentation indicated in paragraph 2 of this article, and in any case no later than 48 hours after the deadline for allocation. 3. The accommodation is reserved exclusively for students, who may not cede its use, even temporarily, to others, under penalty of forfeiture of benefits. 4. Security provisions:

It will be mandatory to attend information and training meetings organised by EDISU related to security. The calendar will be announced after the date of entry into the residence.

items.text: Art. 34(5) SUMMER CLOSURE

classification.title: Art. 34 (5) SUMMER CLOSURE
classification.raw: Art. 34(5) SUMMER CLOSURE

items.text: The residences will be closed during the summer period from 24 July to 1 September 2026 and during periods not included in the table in paragraph 4 of this article.

classification.raw: The residences will be closed during the summer period from 24 July to 1 September 2026 and during periods not included in the table in paragraph 4 of this article.

items.text: Some residences will remain open in August 2025 for students who need to stay as guests during the summer period: interested parties must indicate this need when accepting their place. Requests received after acceptance will be assessed by the Accommodation Service sector based on the availability of places.

classification.raw: Some residences will remain open in August 2025 for students who need to stay as guests during the summer period: interested parties must indicate this need when accepting their place. Requests received after acceptance will be assessed by the Accommodation Service sector based on the availability of places.

items.text: Students staying in residence during the month of August will be required to pay the fee of \notin 8 . 1, 8 per day for a minimum of 7 consecutive nights (in the period from 4 August to 29 August, students will be required to pay a minimum of 7 nights even for shorter stays).

classification.raw: Students staying in residence during the month of August will be required to pay the fee of \notin 8 . 1, 8 per day for a minimum of 7 consecutive nights (in the period from 4 August to 29 August, students will be required to pay a minimum of 7 nights even for shorter stays).

items.text: Changes to the summer residence application must be received by 4 August via the ticketing service

classification.raw: Changes to the summer residence application must be received by 4 August via the ticketing service

items.text: Payment for the summer residence must be made in advance when entering the Residence, as stipulated

items.text: Art. 34(6) SPECIAL CASES

classification.title: Art. 34 (6) SPECIAL CASES
classification.raw: Art. 34(6) SPECIAL CASES

items.text: Should a student who, for documented health reasons, pregnancy, or dependent children, be unable to live in an EDISU residence, they may be eligible for the scholarship amount including the value of the accommodation service if they certify paid residence in accordance with the procedures and within the deadlines laid down in art. 8(2) above. The student will still have to complete the online acceptance procedure (if a winner) or expression of interest (if eligible) and declare their status via a ticket. The same provisions apply to the cases indicated in art. 29(5).

classification.raw: Should a student who, for documented health reasons, pregnancy, or dependent children, be unable to live in an EDISU residence, they may be eligible for the scholarship amount including the value of the accommodation service if they certify paid residence in accordance with the procedures and within the deadlines laid down in art. 8(2) above. The student will still have to complete the online acceptance procedure (if a winner) or expression of interest (if eligible) and declare their status via a ticket. The same provisions apply to the cases indicated in art. 29(5).

items.text: Art. 34(6.1) ADDITIONAL SEMESTERS

classification.title: Art. 34 (6.1) ADDITIONAL SEMESTERS
classification.raw: Art. 34(6.1) ADDITIONAL SEMESTERS

items.text: 1. Students receiving the accommodation service for an additional semester who graduate by 31 March 2026 must:

classification.title: Students receiving the accommodation service for an additional semester who graduate by 31 March 2026 must:
classification.raw: 1. Students receiving the accommodation service for an additional semester who graduate by 31 March 2026 must:

items.text: a. Promptly notify their residence of the date of graduation, as soon as it is known, and leave the residence within 10 days of that date. b. If the degree is awarded after 31 March 2026, but according to the course schedule falls within the March graduation session, students must promptly notify their residence and the accommodation service office through the ticketing procedure of the expected graduation date or graduation period. They should bear in mind that they must also notify their residence of the date of graduation and vacate the accommodation within 10 days of that date. c. Otherwise, students will have to repay an amount equal to the value of the accommodation service unduly used, in the period between the eleventh day after the date of graduation and the date of check-out from the residence, at the residence rate. d. The value of the accommodation service in the period between the date of removal from the residence and 31 March 2026 will not be reimbursed in any way.

items.text: They can remain in residence until the publication of the definitive scholarship rankings on 30 April 2026.

items.text: 1. Students who apply for a first year of a master's degree and are assigned the accommodation service but do not enrol in a master's degree course by 3 April 2026: • if they qualify for being considered in their last semester of the degree, they will have to leave the residence and pay for the period used from 1 April 2026 if they qualify for being considered in their last semester of the degree, they will have to leave the residence and pay for the whole period used starting from the allocation date. 2. Students who apply as subsequent-year students and are assigned the accommodation service, but do not complete their enrolment or are not awarded the credits set forth in Table A2 by 3 April 2026, will have to leave their residence within 3 working days and pay for the entire period used from the allocation date.

2. items.text: Art. 34(6.3) STUDENTS WHO MUST LEAVE THEIR RESIDENCE FOR SERIOUS REASONS

items.text: 3. LEGAL BASIS AND PURPOSE OF PERSONAL DATA PROCESSING

classification.title: 3. LEGAL BASIS AND PURPOSE OF PERSONAL DATA PROCESSING
classification.raw: 3. LEGAL BASIS AND PURPOSE OF PERSONAL DATA PROCESSING

items.text: The processing of personal data is lawful if there is a valid legal basis. For the processing covered by this policy, the valid legal bases are as follows:

classification.raw: The processing of personal data is lawful if there is a valid legal basis. For the processing covered by this policy, the valid legal bases are as follows:

items.text: Contract (art. 6(1)(b) of the Regulation): processing is necessary for the performance of a contract to which the data subject is party or for the performance of pre-contractual measures taken at the data subject's request; Legal Obligation (art. 6(1)(c) of the Regulation): processing is necessary for compliance with a legal obligation to which the data controller is subject; Public interest (art. 6(1)(e) of the Regulation): processing is necessary for the performance of a task carried out in the public interest or in the exercise of official authority vested in the controller; Obligation and/or right in relation to social protection (art. 9(2)(b) of the Regulation): processing is necessary for the purposes of fulfilling the obligations and exercising the specific rights of the data controller or the data subject in relation to social protection insofar as authorised by European Union or Member State law.

items.text: Personal data will be processed in order to fulfil the obligations related to participation in the Notice of Competition (drawing up rankings, disbursement of benefits, etc.) and in order to carry out checks on the truthfulness and/or maintenance of the conditions (financial and merit) or of the status (by way of example, that of a beneficiary of international protection) that the participant self-certifies, including for the purpose of the withdrawal and consequent return of the benefits granted.

items.text: Personal data will also be processed for the fulfilment of legal, accounting and tax obligations and for statistical purposes, the latter through communication to public bodies set up for this purpose.

classification.raw: Personal data will also be processed for the fulfilment of legal, accounting and tax obligations and for statistical purposes, the latter through communication to public bodies set up for this purpose.

items.text: They will also be processed for the purposes of reporting and checks carried out by ministerial and regional bodies to verify the correct allocation of funding, in particular NRRP and ESF.

classification.raw: They will also be processed for the purposes of reporting and checks carried out by ministerial and regional bodies to verify the correct allocation of funding, in particular NRRP and ESF.

items.text: 11. RIGHTS GRANTED TO THE DATA SUBJECT

classification.title: 11. RIGHTS GRANTED TO THE DATA SUBJECT
classification.raw: 11. RIGHTS GRANTED TO THE DATA SUBJECT

items.text:
Right of access (art. 15 GDPR)The data subject has the right to obtain confirmation as to whether personal data relating to them are being processed from the Data Controller and, if so, to obtain access to the personal data and the information referred to in art. 15 of the Regulation. If personal data are transferred to a third country, the data subject also has the right to be informed of the existence of adequate safeguards relating to the transfer pursuant to art. 46 of the Regulation.
Right to rectification (art. 16 GDPR)The data subject has the right to obtain the rectification of inaccurate personal data concerning them without undue delay from the Data Controller. Considering the purposes of the processing, the data subject has the right to supplement incomplete personal data, including by providing a supplementary declaration.
Right to erasure (art. 17 GDPR)The data subject has the right to obtain the erasure of personal data concerning them without undue delay from the Data Controller, and the Data Controller is obliged to erase personal data without undue delay when: a) personal data are no longer necessary in relation to the purposes for which they were collected or otherwise processed; b) [letter omitted as not applicable to the processing indicated in this policy]; c) the data subject objects to the processing pursuant to art. 21(1) of the Regulation and there is no overriding legitimate reason for processing; d) personal data have been unlawfully processed; e) personal data must be erased to comply with a legal obligation under European Union or Member State law to which the Data Controller is subject; f) [letter omitted as not applicable to the processing indicated in this policy]
Right to restriction of processing (art. 18 GDPR)The data subject has the right to obtain the restriction of processing from the Data Controller when one of the following cases occurs: a) the data subject contends the accuracy of the personal data, for the period necessary for the Data Controller to verify the accuracy of such personal data; b) the processing is unlawful and the data subject objects to the erasure of the personal data and requests instead that their use be restricted;
c) although the Data Controller no longer needs them for the purposes of the processing, the personal data are necessary for the establishment, exercise, or defence of a legal claim; d) the data subject has objected to the processing pursuant to art. 21(1), pending verification as to whether the Data controller's legitimate reasons prevail over those of the data subject.
Notification obligation (art. 19 GDPR)The Data Controller shall inform each of the recipients to whom the personal data have been transmitted of any rectification or erasure or restriction of processing carried out pursuant to articles 16, 17(1) and 18, unless this proves impossible or involves a disproportionate effort. The Data Controller shall inform the data subject of these recipients if the data subject so requests.
Right to data portability (art. 20 GDPR)[article omitted as not applicable to the processing indicated in this policy]
Right to object (art. 21 GDPR)The data subject has the right to object at any time, on grounds relating to their particular situation, to the processing of personal data concerning them in accordance with art. 6(1)(e) or (f), including profiling. The Data Controller shall refrain from further processing personal data unless they can demonstrate the existence of compelling legitimate grounds for processing that override the interests, rights, and freedoms of the data subject or for the establishment, exercise, or defence of legal claims.
Right to lodge a complaint with the Authority (art. 77 GDPR)Without prejudice to any other administrative or judicial remedy, a data subject who considers that the processing of their personal data is in breach of the Regulation has the right to lodge a complaint before the competent independent administrative Authority in the Member State of the European Union where they normally reside, where they work, or where the alleged breach has occurred.
Right to compensation (art. 82 GDPR)Anyone who suffers material or non-material damage caused by a breach of the Regulation has the right to obtain compensation for the damage from the Controller or the Processor.

classification.title:
Right of access (art. 15 GDPR)The data subject has the right to obtain confirmation as to whether personal data relating to them are being processed from the Data Controller and, if so, to obtain access to the personal data and the information referred to in art. 15 of the Regulation. If personal data are transferred to a third country, the data subject also has the right to be informed of the existence of adequate safeguards relating to the transfer pursuant to art. 46 of the Regulation.
Right to rectification (art. 16 GDPR)The data subject has the right to obtain the rectification of inaccurate personal data concerning them without undue delay from the Data Controller. Considering the purposes of the processing, the data subject has the right to supplement incomplete personal data, including by providing a supplementary declaration.
Right to erasure (art. 17 GDPR)The data subject has the right to obtain the erasure of personal data concerning them without undue delay from the Data Controller, and the Data Controller is obliged to erase personal data without undue delay when: a) personal data are no longer necessary in relation to the purposes for which they were collected or otherwise processed; b) [letter omitted as not applicable to the processing indicated in this policy]; c) the data subject objects to the processing pursuant to art. 21(1) of the Regulation and there is no overriding legitimate reason for processing; d) personal data have been unlawfully processed; e) personal data must be erased to comply with a legal obligation under European Union or Member State law to which the Data Controller is subject; f) [letter omitted as not applicable to the processing indicated in this policy]
Right to restriction of processing (art. 18 GDPR)The data subject has the right to obtain the restriction of processing from the Data Controller when one of the following cases occurs: a) the data subject contends the accuracy of the personal data, for the period necessary for the Data Controller to verify the accuracy of such personal data; b) the processing is unlawful and the data subject objects to the erasure of the personal data and requests instead that their use be restricted;
c) although the Data Controller no longer needs them for the purposes of the processing, the personal data are necessary for the establishment, exercise, or defence of a legal claim; d) the data subject has objected to the processing pursuant to art. 21(1), pending verification as to whether the Data controller's legitimate reasons prevail over those of the data subject.
Notification obligation (art. 19 GDPR)The Data Controller shall inform each of the recipients to whom the personal data have been transmitted of any rectification or erasure or restriction of processing carried out pursuant to articles 16, 17(1) and 18, unless this proves impossible or involves a disproportionate effort. The Data Controller shall inform the data subject of these recipients if the data subject so requests.
Right to data portability (art. 20 GDPR)[article omitted as not applicable to the processing indicated in this policy]
Right to object (art. 21 GDPR)The data subject has the right to object at any time, on grounds relating to their particular situation, to the processing of personal data concerning them in accordance with art. 6(1)(e) or (f), including profiling. The Data Controller shall refrain from further processing personal data unless they can demonstrate the existence of compelling legitimate grounds for processing that override the interests, rights, and freedoms of the data subject or for the establishment, exercise, or defence of legal claims.
Right to lodge a complaint with the Authority (art. 77 GDPR)Without prejudice to any other administrative or judicial remedy, a data subject who considers that the processing of their personal data is in breach of the Regulation has the right to lodge a complaint before the competent independent administrative Authority in the Member State of the European Union where they normally reside, where they work, or where the alleged breach has occurred.
Right to compensation (art. 82 GDPR)Anyone who suffers material or non-material damage caused by a breach of the Regulation has the right to obtain compensation for the damage from the Controller or the Processor.

items.text: Art. 37(1) CHECKING FINANCIAL CONDITIONS

classification.title: Art. 37 (1) CHECKING FINANCIAL CONDITIONS
classification.raw: Art. 37(1) CHECKING FINANCIAL CONDITIONS

items.text: To ensure compliance with the deadlines laid down in the notice, checks and verifications on the truthfulness of students' self- certifications are carried out after the benefits have been granted. In compliance with the provisions of the “EDISU Piemonte Financial Assessment Regulations”77 and subsequent amendments and supplements, EDISU checks the truthfulness of the self- certifications produced by students for aspects relating to their financial status. These checks concern 100 \% of the applicants who are beneficiaries of scholarships and/or the accommodation service for the academic year 2025/26, unless otherwise approved by the Board of Directors. EDISU78 does ex ante checks on the disbursement of the benefit or ex post checks as indicated in art. 1 point 2 of the EDISU Piemonte Financial Assessment Regulation.

classification.raw: To ensure compliance with the deadlines laid down in the notice, checks and verifications on the truthfulness of students' self- certifications are carried out after the benefits have been granted. In compliance with the provisions of the “EDISU Piemonte Financial Assessment Regulations”77 and subsequent amendments and supplements, EDISU checks the truthfulness of the self- certifications produced by students for aspects relating to their financial status. These checks concern 100 \% of the applicants who are beneficiaries of scholarships and/or the accommodation service for the academic year 2025/26, unless otherwise approved by the Board of Directors. EDISU78 does ex ante checks on the disbursement of the benefit or ex post checks as indicated in art. 1 point 2 of the EDISU Piemonte Financial Assessment Regulation.

items.text: In carrying out these checks, EDISU may request appropriate documentation to prove the completeness and truthfulness of the declared data, including to correct material or minor errors.

classification.raw: In carrying out these checks, EDISU may request appropriate documentation to prove the completeness and truthfulness of the declared data, including to correct material or minor errors.

items.text: The start of the process will be communicated to students by registered letter for ex post checks only. The procedural process can also be followed by each individual student on their personal Online Helpdesk page, under the heading “checks/withdrawals”. EDISU Piemonte guarantees that the checks will be carried out within the time frame set forth by the relevant regulations.

classification.raw: The start of the process will be communicated to students by registered letter for ex post checks only. The procedural process can also be followed by each individual student on their personal Online Helpdesk page, under the heading “checks/withdrawals”. EDISU Piemonte guarantees that the checks will be carried out within the time frame set forth by the relevant regulations.

items.text: If the data found in the databases and/or documentation already in the possession of or requested from the students or certifying bodies have an impact on the benefit based on the ISEE Universitario (or, in the case of students with part of their household residing abroad, the calculated value of the ISEE Parificato) or ISPE (or, in the case of students with part of their household residing abroad the calculated value of the ISPE Parificato) automatically recalculated, we proceed, for ex ante verifications, with the exclusion of the student(s) from the definitive scholarship ranking. In the event of disbursement of a first instalment of the scholarship, we proceed with compensation on the second instalment or the request of the amount not due directly to the student. As regards ex post checks, EDISU Piemonte withdraws the scholarship and any other benefits paid in the same academic year and applies the administrative sanctions provided for by Legislative Decree 68/2012 art. 10(3), without prejudice to the provisions of art. 38 Law 122/2010.

items.text: Scholarships are also revoked for students enrolled in the first years of study who, following the financial assessment, are found to have an ISEE Universitario (or, in the case of students with part of their household residing abroad, the ISEE Parificato) that is higher than the declared value, as well as higher than the ISEE Universitario threshold (or, in the case of students with part of their household residing abroad, the ISEE Parificato) that, for the academic year 2025/26, distinguishes winners from those eligible.

items.text: Art. 37(2) SANCTIONS

classification.title: Art. 37 (2) SANCTIONS
classification.raw: Art. 37(2) SANCTIONS

items.text: In compliance with the provisions of the “EDISU Piemonte Financial Assessment Regulations” as amended and supplemented, in the event that the investigations carried out reveal that untrue declarations have been submitted in order to receive benefits, the applicant will be subject to administrative sanctions, which will be ascertained and imposed pursuant to art. 10 of Legislative Decree no. 68/2012, i.e. pursuant to art. 38 of Decree-Law no. 78 of 31 May 2010, as converted by Law no. 122 of 30 July 2010. Such applicants shall forfeit any benefits deriving from the measure issued on the basis of the untrue declaration79 without prejudice to any further measures and actions provided for by the regulations in force.

classification.raw: In compliance with the provisions of the “EDISU Piemonte Financial Assessment Regulations” as amended and supplemented, in the event that the investigations carried out reveal that untrue declarations have been submitted in order to receive benefits, the applicant will be subject to administrative sanctions, which will be ascertained and imposed pursuant to art. 10 of Legislative Decree no. 68/2012, i.e. pursuant to art. 38 of Decree-Law no. 78 of 31 May 2010, as converted by Law no. 122 of 30 July 2010. Such applicants shall forfeit any benefits deriving from the measure issued on the basis of the untrue declaration79 without prejudice to any further measures and actions provided for by the regulations in force.

items.text: Students will have to return the amount paid and will be subject to the sanction of paying three times the amount receivedP80F80P without prejudice in any case to the application of the sanctions referred to in art. 38(3) of Decree-law no. 78 of 31/05/2010, converted, with amendments, by Law no. 122 of 30/07/2010, in proportion to the financial advantage obtained, as well as the criminal provisions for acts constituting offences. They will also lose the right to obtain further benefits for the duration of their course of study, as well as forfeiting ALL benefits81 granted by EDISU Piemonte in the years following the year of the assessment, hereby including the subsidised rate for the catering service.

classification.raw: Students will have to return the amount paid and will be subject to the sanction of paying three times the amount receivedP80F80P without prejudice in any case to the application of the sanctions referred to in art. 38(3) of Decree-law no. 78 of 31/05/2010, converted, with amendments, by Law no. 122 of 30/07/2010, in proportion to the financial advantage obtained, as well as the criminal provisions for acts constituting offences. They will also lose the right to obtain further benefits for the duration of their course of study, as well as forfeiting ALL benefits81 granted by EDISU Piemonte in the years following the year of the assessment, hereby including the subsidised rate for the catering service.

items.text: Anyone who makes false statements, signs false documents or uses them82 shall be punished in accordance with the Criminal Code and the special laws on the subject. The production of a deed containing untrue data is equivalent to the use of a false deed.

classification.raw: Anyone who makes false statements, signs false documents or uses them82 shall be punished in accordance with the Criminal Code and the special laws on the subject. The production of a deed containing untrue data is equivalent to the use of a false deed.

items.text: In particular, false declarations, falsification of documents, and the use of false documents will be reported by EDISU Piemonte to the Judicial Authority for the possible occurrence of the aforementioned offences.

items.text: Art. 38(2) BENEFIT WITHDRAWAL

classification.raw: Art. 38(2) BENEFIT WITHDRAWAL

items.text: Students who incur the grounds for exclusion and forfeiture of eligibility for benefits during the academic year 2025/26 must repay the sums received by way of scholarship and/or the value of the accommodation service used and other cash benefits by forfeiting their right to the tuition fee waiver, pursuant to the applicable law on debt collection.

classification.raw: Students who incur the grounds for exclusion and forfeiture of eligibility for benefits during the academic year 2025/26 must repay the sums received by way of scholarship and/or the value of the accommodation service used and other cash benefits by forfeiting their right to the tuition fee waiver, pursuant to the applicable law on debt collection.

items.text: Benefits are also withdrawn from students who have been subject to a serious disciplinary measure taken by their University, such as exclusion or suspension from several university progress examinations or suspension from university attendance for a period of one year or more.

items.text: Individuals whose benefits have been withdrawn and who have not indicated a valid residence or domicile address complete with any addressee for substituted service, and who are therefore unavailable, will be reported to the offices of their University for administrative blocking of their student career. These offices will block the student career based on the respective Regulations.

classification.raw: Individuals whose benefits have been withdrawn and who have not indicated a valid residence or domicile address complete with any addressee for substituted service, and who are therefore unavailable, will be reported to the offices of their University for administrative blocking of their student career. These offices will block the student career based on the respective Regulations.

items.text: This block will also be imposed on students who have not paid the balance of the amount due by the deadline indicated in the notification letter.

classification.raw: This block will also be imposed on students who have not paid the balance of the amount due by the deadline indicated in the notification letter.

items.text: Students are also asked to provide a valid telephone number.

classification.raw: Students are also asked to provide a valid telephone number.

items.text: 2. Students eligible for the scholarship but not assigned the accommodation service

classification.title: 2. Students eligible for the scholarship but not assigned the accommodation service
classification.raw: 2. Students eligible for the scholarship but not assigned the accommodation service

items.text: Notice will be given on the personal Online Helpdesk page in the “Assessments/Withdrawals” section, so that students will be informed of the conclusion of the merit procedure (art. 47(2.1)) and the outcome for them. No other individual written communication will be sent.

classification.raw: Notice will be given on the personal Online Helpdesk page in the “Assessments/Withdrawals” section, so that students will be informed of the conclusion of the merit procedure (art. 47(2.1)) and the outcome for them. No other individual written communication will be sent.

items.text: The repayment of benefits is subject to the regulations in force; in all cases of failure to repay the amounts due, any other benefits awarded will be suspended until the debt is settled.

classification.raw: The repayment of benefits is subject to the regulations in force; in all cases of failure to repay the amounts due, any other benefits awarded will be suspended until the debt is settled.

items.text: Art. 38(4) STUDENT CAREER BLOCKS

classification.title: Art. 38 (4) STUDENT CAREER BLOCKS
classification.raw: Art. 38(4) STUDENT CAREER BLOCKS

items.text: Individuals whose benefits have been revoked and who have not paid the balance of the amount due within the deadline indicated in the notification letter, or who do not comply with the instalment plan granted pursuant to the EDISU Debt Recovery Guidelines, will be subject to an administrative freeze on their student career. Names will be reported to the offices of their University for the administrative blocking of their student career. These offices will block the student career based on the respective Regulations. As indicated in the previous paragraph, this blocking will also be carried out on students who have not indicated a valid residence or domicile address complete with any addressee for substituted service, and who are therefore unavailable.

classification.raw: Individuals whose benefits have been revoked and who have not paid the balance of the amount due within the deadline indicated in the notification letter, or who do not comply with the instalment plan granted pursuant to the EDISU Debt Recovery Guidelines, will be subject to an administrative freeze on their student career. Names will be reported to the offices of their University for the administrative blocking of their student career. These offices will block the student career based on the respective Regulations. As indicated in the previous paragraph, this blocking will also be carried out on students who have not indicated a valid residence or domicile address complete with any addressee for substituted service, and who are therefore unavailable.

items.text: Art. 38(5) COMMUNICATION TO THE UNIVERSITY

classification.title: Art. 38 (5) COMMUNICATION TO THE UNIVERSITY
classification.raw: Art. 38(5) COMMUNICATION TO THE UNIVERSITY

items.text: The act of withdrawal will be communicated to the competent offices of the University, which will withdraw the fee waiver and request for payment of the amount due in accordance with the current Fee Regulations.

classification.raw: The act of withdrawal will be communicated to the competent offices of the University, which will withdraw the fee waiver and request for payment of the amount due in accordance with the current Fee Regulations.

items.text: Art. 38(6) ENTRY IN TAXPAYERS’ LIST

classification.title: Art. 38 (6) ENTRY IN TAXPAYERS’ LIST
classification.raw: Art. 38(6) ENTRY IN TAXPAYERS’ LIST

items.text: In the event of failure to repay the sums due, EDISU will proceed to recover the debt by communicating the debtors to Agenzia delle Entrate - Collection, which is responsible for compulsory collection.

classification.raw: In the event of failure to repay the sums due, EDISU will proceed to recover the debt by communicating the debtors to Agenzia delle Entrate - Collection, which is responsible for compulsory collection.

items.text: The methods of compulsory collection and the related procedures are governed by the respective legal provisions in force for the purpose of enforcement.

classification.raw: The methods of compulsory collection and the related procedures are governed by the respective legal provisions in force for the purpose of enforcement.

items.text: Art. 38(7) WITHDRAWAL OF ADDITIONAL SERVICES AND BENEFITS

classification.title: Art. 38 (7) WITHDRAWAL OF ADDITIONAL SERVICES AND BENEFITS
classification.raw: Art. 38(7) WITHDRAWAL OF ADDITIONAL SERVICES AND BENEFITS

items.text: EDISU Piemonte will withdraw additional services and other benefits according to the respective notices and internal regulations.

classification.raw: EDISU Piemonte will withdraw additional services and other benefits according to the respective notices and internal regulations.

items.text: ANNEX “E”

classification.title: ANNEX “E”
classification.raw: ANNEX “E”

items.text: SPECIFICATIONS AND SPECIAL CASES CONCERNING INFORMATION TO BE INCLUDED IN THE CONSULAR DOCUMENTATION OF INTERNATIONAL STUDENTS

classification.title: SPECIFICATIONS AND SPECIAL CASES CONCERNING INFORMATION TO BE INCLUDED IN THE CONSULAR DOCUMENTATION OF INTERNATIONAL STUDENTS
classification.raw: SPECIFICATIONS AND SPECIAL CASES CONCERNING INFORMATION TO BE INCLUDED IN THE CONSULAR DOCUMENTATION OF INTERNATIONAL STUDENTS

items.text: Below are detailed guidelines on the information that must be included in the financial documentation for students with family residing in non-EU and EU countries.

classification.raw: Below are detailed guidelines on the information that must be included in the financial documentation for students with family residing in non-EU and EU countries.

items.text: The most common special cases are also indicated. If the cases listed do not include a particular situation, students are requested to contact EDISU by opening a ticket.

classification.raw: The most common special cases are also indicated. If the cases listed do not include a particular situation, students are requested to contact EDISU by opening a ticket.

items.text: You can also consult the EDISU database on documentation received in previous years, divided by country.

classification.raw: You can also consult the EDISU database on documentation received in previous years, divided by country.

items.text: 1. HOUSEHOLD COMPOSITION

classification.title: 1. HOUSEHOLD COMPOSITION
classification.raw: 1. HOUSEHOLD COMPOSITION

items.text: This means a certificate in which all members living with the student appear. It must provide details of the first name, surname and date of birth of each cohabiting member.

classification.raw: This means a certificate in which all members living with the student appear. It must provide details of the first name, surname and date of birth of each cohabiting member.

items.text: Parents: information on parents must always be provided, unless an application is made as an independent student (see below) or the following special cases arise:

classification.raw: Parents: information on parents must always be provided, unless an application is made as an independent student (see below) or the following special cases arise:

items.text: a) Death: in the event of the death of a parent, this must be specified in the certificate. If this is not possible, the death certificate may be presented as a non-original copy without legalisation and is also acceptable in English, French, or Spanish. In the case of a student who is an orphan, the registered household must refer to the provisions of art. 6(1.6), only if the students reside alone. b) Divorce or legal separation: in the event of divorce or legal separation, this must be specified in the certificate. If this is not possible, the divorce or legal separation certificate may be presented as a non-original copy without legalisation and is also acceptable in English, French, or Spanish. c) Financial-emotional estrangement: if the student has only one parent and there are no financial or emotional ties with the other biological parent, this must be specified in the certificate. If this is not possible, the financial-emotional estrangement certificate may be presented as a non-original copy without legalisation and is also acceptable in English, French, or Spanish. If it is only possible to produce this document in Affidavit, it must be translated into Italian and legalised.

items: a) Death: in the event of the death of a parent, this must be specified in the certificate. If this is not possible, the death certificate may be presented as a non-original copy without legalisation and is also acceptable in English, French, or Spanish. In the case of a student who is an orphan, the registered household must refer to the provisions of art. 6(1.6), only if the students reside alone. b) Divorce or legal separation: in the event of divorce or legal separation, this must be specified in the certificate. If this is not possible, the divorce or legal separation certificate may be presented as a non-original copy without legalisation and is also acceptable in English, French, or Spanish. c) Financial-emotional estrangement: if the student has only one parent and there are no financial or emotional ties with the other biological parent, this must be specified in the certificate. If this is not possible, the financial-emotional estrangement certificate may be presented as a non-original copy without legalisation and is also acceptable in English, French, or Spanish. If it is only possible to produce this document in Affidavit, it must be translated into Italian and legalised.
classification.raw: a) Death: in the event of the death of a parent, this must be specified in the certificate. If this is not possible, the death certificate may be presented as a non-original copy without legalisation and is also acceptable in English, French, or Spanish. In the case of a student who is an orphan, the registered household must refer to the provisions of art. 6(1.6), only if the students reside alone. b) Divorce or legal separation: in the event of divorce or legal separation, this must be specified in the certificate. If this is not possible, the divorce or legal separation certificate may be presented as a non-original copy without legalisation and is also acceptable in English, French, or Spanish. c) Financial-emotional estrangement: if the student has only one parent and there are no financial or emotional ties with the other biological parent, this must be specified in the certificate. If this is not possible, the financial-emotional estrangement certificate may be presented as a non-original copy without legalisation and is also acceptable in English, French, or Spanish. If it is only possible to produce this document in Affidavit, it must be translated into Italian and legalised.

items.text: Non-cohabiting members: For all members aged 18 or more as of 1 January 2025 indicated in the certificate of household composition, documentation on the existence or no of income and assets must also be submitted. If the certificate of household composition includes members who no longer live in the same household as the student, they may be excluded from the household by submitting additional documentation to prove the lack of cohabitation; this documentation may be submitted as a non-original copy, without legalisation and is also acceptable in English, French, or Spanish.

classification.raw: Non-cohabiting members: For all members aged 18 or more as of 1 January 2025 indicated in the certificate of household composition, documentation on the existence or no of income and assets must also be submitted. If the certificate of household composition includes members who no longer live in the same household as the student, they may be excluded from the household by submitting additional documentation to prove the lack of cohabitation; this documentation may be submitted as a non-original copy, without legalisation and is also acceptable in English, French, or Spanish.

items.text: Married students: the household consists of the student, spouse, children and any other people living with them. Parents of spouses are to be included in the household only if they still reside with the student or if, although their residence is different, the student does not meet the requirements for independent status (see below).

classification.raw: Married students: the household consists of the student, spouse, children and any other people living with them. Parents of spouses are to be included in the household only if they still reside with the student or if, although their residence is different, the student does not meet the requirements for independent status (see below).

items.text: Adult members: “members aged 18 or more as of 1 January 2, 0, 2, 5 " means any member of the household (including students) who turned 18 years of age during the calendar year 2024 (1 January-31 December) and were therefore of legal age as of 1 January 2025. Any members born on 1 January 2007 itself are excluded from this definition.

classification.raw: Adult members: “members aged 18 or more as of 1 January 2, 0, 2, 5 " means any member of the household (including students) who turned 18 years of age during the calendar year 2024 (1 January-31 December) and were therefore of legal age as of 1 January 2025. Any members born on 1 January 2007 itself are excluded from this definition.

items.text: New marriage: if one of the student's parents is married in a new marriage to a person other than the other parent, the new spouse must be included in the household and therefore the relevant income and assets must be submitted.

classification.raw: New marriage: if one of the student's parents is married in a new marriage to a person other than the other parent, the new spouse must be included in the household and therefore the relevant income and assets must be submitted.

items.text: 2. INCOME

classification.title: 2. INCOME
classification.raw: 2. INCOME

items.text: For all household members aged 18 or more as of 1 January 2025, the total gross annual income must be submitted or, if there was no income, the certificate of no income or unemployment for the year requested. This information must be provided even if the household members were not of age in the requested year but are of age on 1 January 2025.

classification.raw: For all household members aged 18 or more as of 1 January 2025, the total gross annual income must be submitted or, if there was no income, the certificate of no income or unemployment for the year requested. This information must be provided even if the household members were not of age in the requested year but are of age on 1 January 2025.

items.text: The details of the information required are listed below:

classification.raw: The details of the information required are listed below:

items.text: • Type of income: the total gross annual income must be provided. Certificates specifying net income or a generic income (without the specification “gross”) will not be accepted. On the other hand, certificates in the form of tables or income tax returns are acceptable that, while not showing the specification “gross”, allow the same to be traced by showing tax and social security deductions and any bonus payments. • Currency required: income must be expressed in local currency only, so that EDISU can proceed to convert the currency into Euro for the calculation of the ISEE Parificato. Certificates showing income already converted into Euro or other currencies will not be accepted. If the document shows both income already converted into Euro and expressed in local currency, this will be accepted but the value converted into Euro will not be taken into account. • Income reference period: for non-EU students, only 2024 income is required. For EU students, only 2023 income is required. The total gross annual income or the total gross income for each individual month from January to December must be indicated. Certificates showing only some monthly payments, without further information on income or unemployment for the remaining months, will be considered ineligible.

items: • Type of income: the total gross annual income must be provided. Certificates specifying net income or a generic income (without the specification “gross”) will not be accepted. On the other hand, certificates in the form of tables or income tax returns are acceptable that, while not showing the specification “gross”, allow the same to be traced by showing tax and social security deductions and any bonus payments.

Certificates showing income already converted into Euro or other currencies will not be accepted. If the document shows both income already converted into Euro and expressed in local currency, this will be accepted but the value converted into Euro will not be taken into account.

• Income reference period: for non-EU students, only 2024 income is required. For EU students, only 2023 income is required. The total gross annual income or the total gross income for each individual month from January to December must be indicated. Certificates showing only some monthly payments, without further information on income or unemployment for the remaining months, will be considered ineligible.
